1	# AgentAssistPlcDev — Agent Guide
2	
3	## Project Overview
4	
5	Windows desktop app that assists PLC programming work, starting with Siemens TIA Portal V17. The app is decomposed into independent MCP servers (one per capability domain) so any MCP-compatible client can call each server individually. Each MCP hosts pluggable platform adapters behind a shared contract.
6	
7	**Status:** Phase 1 (mcp-engineering) complete 2026-07-18. Phase 2 — AI-generated network comments over the MCP chain — is split into steps; step 1 (mcp-knowledge ingest) design: `buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md`.
8	
9	## Tech Stack
10	
11	
12	| Layer           | Technology                                    | Target Framework |
13	| --------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
14	| UI              | WPF                                           | `net8-windows`   |
15	| AI              | DeepSeek cloud API (OpenAI-compatible)        | —                |
16	| MCP SDK         | `ModelContextProtocol` NuGet, stdio transport | varies           |
17	| Database        | SQLite (`Microsoft.Data.Sqlite`)              | `net8`           |
18	| Version control | LibGit2Sharp                                  | `net8`           |
19	
20	
21	## Framework Split (Hard Constraint)
22	
23	Siemens API assemblies require .NET Framework 4.8:
24	
25	| Target                   | Projects                               | Framework          |
26	| ------------------------ | -------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
27	| Engineering + Simulation | `Mcp.Engineering`, `Mcp.Simulation`    | **net48**          |
28	| Everything else          | All other src projects                 | **net8**           |
29	| Shared contracts         | `Contracts`                            | **netstandard2.0** |
30	
31	- **Never** add net48 dependency to a net8 project or vice versa.
32	- Always route Siemens-specific APIs through the contract interfaces (`IEngineeringPlatform`, `ISimulationPlatform`).
33	
34	## Solution Layout
35	
36	```
37	AgentAssistPlcDev.sln
38	├── src/
39	│   ├── Contracts/                 netstandard2.0 — shared DTOs + platform interfaces
40	│   ├── Mcp.Engineering/           net48 — TIA Openness adapter
41	│   ├── Mcp.Knowledge/             net8  — SQLite knowledge graph from exported XML
42	│   ├── Mcp.SourceEditor/          net8  — block XML parse/edit/generate
43	│   ├── Mcp.Simulation/            net48 — PLCSIM Advanced adapter (Phase 5)
44	│   ├── Mcp.VersionControl/        net8  — git operations
45	│   ├── Agent/                     net8  — DeepSeek client + MCP host
46	│   └── App/                       net8-windows — WPF UI shell
47	└── tests/
48	```
49	
50	## Build Commands
51	
52	- **Build solution:** `dotnet build AgentAssistPlcDev.sln`
53	- **Build specific project:** `dotnet build src/Mcp.Engineering/`
54	- **Run tests:** `dotnet test`
55	- **Restore packages:** `dotnet restore`
56	
57	## Key Architecture Rules
58	
59	1. **No useless prefixes on project names.** Names are scoped by what a thing IS within the solution, not by what the solution is called. Every project already belongs to this solution — repeating the app name as a prefix (`PlcAi.Contracts`, `PlcAi.Agent`) adds nothing. `Mcp.Engineering` is good (tells you it's an MCP server + domain). `PlcAi.Mcp.Engineering` is not. If removing a prefix would make the name ambiguous, keep it; otherwise cut it.
60	
61	2. **MCP naming convention:** plain `verb_noun` (no per-server prefix), e.g. `list_sessions`, `ingest_source`. Annotate tools with `readOnlyHint` or `destructiveHint`. Return structured JSON. Test each MCP server standalone with MCP Inspector before UI integration.
62	
63	3. **Openness dependency:** TIA Portal V17 DLLs at `C:\Program Files\Siemens\Automation\Portal V17\PublicAPI\V17\`. Windows user must be in "Siemens TIA Openness" group.
64	
65	4. **DeepSeek config:** API key stored in `%APPDATA%/PlcAiAssistant/config.json` (git-ignored). Endpoint: `https://api.deepseek.com` (OpenAI-compatible).
66	
67	5. **Platform expansion:** Adapter contracts should be written generically from the start. Rockwell ControlLogix (L5X XML) is the planned second platform.
68	
69	6. **Safety:** Never import a block into TIA without a `vc_snapshot` first. Always `src_validate` before `import_block`. Dry-run mode must produce diff-on-disk without importing.
70	
71	## Git Workflow
72	
73	- **Current branch:** `master`
74	- **Main branch:** `main`
75	- **Co-author commits** with Claude when applicable: `Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`
76	- Commit messages should reference the phase and component.
77	
78	## Key Files
79	
80	- `buildnote/plan/initialLaunch_20260717.md` — full phased build plan with exit criteria (source of truth for architecture decisions)
81	- `buildnote/plan/mcp-engineering.md` — Phase 0–1 detailed design for the engineering MCP server (complete 2026-07-18)
82	- `buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md` — Phase 2 step 1 detailed design for the knowledge MCP server
83	- `agent.md` — this file; concise rules and context for AI agents
84	- `%APPDATA%/PlcAiAssistant/config.json` — local config (git-ignored)
85	
86	## MCP Server Inventory
87	
88	| Server | Phase | Key Tools |
89	| ------ | ----- | --------- |
90	| Engineering | 1 (done) | `check_environment`, `list_sessions`, `connect`, `disconnect`, `save_project`, `get_project_info`, `list_blocks`, `export_block`, `export_all_blocks`, `import_block` (destructive), `compile_block`, `compile_plc` |
91	| Knowledge | 2, step 1 | `ingest_source`, `query` (read-only SQL), `get_schema`; later steps add `get_block`, `get_network`, `search` |
92	| Source Editor | 2, step 4 | `parse_block`, `insert_network_comment`, `diff`, `validate` |
93	| Version Control | 2, step 5 | `init`, `snapshot`, `log`, `diff`, `restore` (destructive) |
94	| Simulation | 5 | instance lifecycle, tag I/O, cycle control |
95	
96	## Phase Sequence
97	
98	
99	| Phase | What                                            | Exit Criteria                                                                 |
100	| ----- | ----------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
101	| 0     | Scaffold + 2 spikes (MCP+net48, XML round-trip) | DONE 2026-07-17 — both spikes passed; solution builds; Inspector calls skeleton server |
102	| 1     | mcp-engineering complete                        | DONE 2026-07-18 — full tool surface verified E2E (headless + attached)        |
103	| 2     | AI network comments over the MCP chain — split into steps: knowledge ingest → tag/UDT export+import → knowledge depth → source-editor → version-control → agent → WPF UI | Comments visible in TIA; block logic unchanged; git snapshot; LLM audit trail |
104	| 3     | Program understanding &amp; Q&amp;A             | Chat answers verifiable against DB                                            |
105	| 4     | AI-assisted modification &amp; generation       | AI-modified block round-trips and compiles                                    |
106	| 5     | PLCSIM simulation                               | Modified block runs in simulation; assertion passes                           |
107	| 6     | Version control depth                           | Full history browsable/diffable in-app                                        |
108	| 7     | Platform expansion (Rockwell) + hardening       | Installer, multi-platform adapters                                            |
109	
110	
111	## Notes for AI Agents
112	
113	- When planning changes, check `buildnote/plan/initialLaunch_20260717.md` for architectural context first.
114	- Do not add frameworks or patterns outside the approved tech stack without explicit user approval.
115	- Always consider the Framework Split constraint — net48 vs net8 vs netstandard2.0.
116	- MCP servers use **stdio transport** only (no HTTP/networking in MVP).
117	- The user's working directory is `C:\Users\Ansel\orca\projects\AgentAssistPlcDev`.
118	- Use english as default language, expect user ask for different language or ask to translate.

1	# mcp-knowledge — SQLite knowledge base built from mcp-engineering exports (Phase 2, step 1)
2	
3	## 0. Context
4	
5	Phase 2 of `initialLaunch_20260717.md` bundled four MCP servers + agent + UI into one phase — too aggressive. It is now split into small steps. **This document covers step 1 only**: a new MCP server that turns the XML files exported by `mcp-engineering` into a queryable SQLite database.
6	
7	**Rename (user decision, 2026-07-18):** `mcp-knowledge-store` → **`mcp-knowledge`** ("store" carries no meaning). Project: `src/Mcp.Knowledge/`. Doc references in `initialLaunch_20260717.md` and `agent.md` are updated when implementation starts.
8	
9	**Extraction method reference:** the PlcSourceExporter project (`C:\Users\Ansel\Documents\Siemens TIA Add-in Dev\PlcSourceExporter`) already solves exactly this problem — see its agent guide (`2026-07-18-195403-...txt`, kept next to that project's root). This plan ports its proven pipeline instead of inventing a new one.
10	
11	## 1. Purpose
12	
13	`mcp-knowledge` ingests a folder of TIA Openness XML exports (the `outputDir` filled by `export_block` / `export_all_blocks` of mcp-engineering) and builds a **property-graph SQLite database**: blocks, networks, calls, symbol reads/writes, data blocks and their members. Downstream consumers (agent, UI) query it for program understanding; the AI-comment workflow (later step) pulls per-network context from it.
14	
15	Nothing else is in scope: no LLM audit tables, no source editing, no git, no UI.
16	
17	## 2. Locked decisions
18	
19	1. **Adopt the PlcSourceExporter graph schema verbatim** (4 tables: `graph_nodes`, `graph_node_properties`, `graph_edges`, `graph_edge_properties` + indices). This supersedes the relational MVP sketch (`projects`/`blocks`/`networks`/`tags`) in `initialLaunch_20260717.md` §Phase 2 — the parser being ported writes exactly this schema, it is proven on real V17 exports, and IDs are deterministic. `llm_runs` stays deferred to the agent step.
20	2. **Port code, don't reference.** `PlcSourceExporter.Core` is netstandard2.0 with only `Microsoft.Data.Sqlite` as a dependency, so its parser/persistence code ports to net8 almost verbatim. We **copy and adapt** the needed files into `src/Mcp.Knowledge/` (provenance note in each file header) rather than adding a cross-repo project reference — this solution stays self-contained.
21	3. ~~**Crawl by root element, not `metadata.json`.**~~ **SUPERSEDED 2026-07-18 (stage 3):** mcp-engineering now writes a PlcSourceExporter-compatible `metadata.json` (schema "1.0") into its export roots, so the importer is **manifest-first**: when `<exportRoot>/metadata.json` exists, components are dispatched from the manifest (the previously unported `ImportExportRoot`/`LoadExportedComponents`/`IsProgramBlockCategory` were ported in stage 3); the root-element crawl remains as the fallback for manifest-less folders (legacy exports). Manifest mode adds reconciliation warnings: Exported-but-missing file, and on-disk `*.xml` not referenced by the manifest (catches legacy flat files and `spike/` copies). Malformed manifest → `MANIFEST_INVALID` error (loud over silent fallback).
22	4. **Rebuild-only ingest.** Delete-all + bulk insert in one transaction, same as the reference. Incremental re-ingest remains a Phase-3 item (`initialLaunch` §Phase 3).
23	5. **Scope = what engineering exports today.** mcp-engineering Phase 1 exports blocks only (OB/FB/FC/DB, including instance DBs). Tag-table and UDT import paths are **not** ported now; they land in the same step that adds tag/UDT export to mcp-engineering (§13). The dispatch is designed so those categories slot in without schema changes. **(2026-07-18, stage 4: the knowledge-side import of `Tags`/`UDT` categories and `SW.Tags.PlcTagTable`/`SW.Types.PlcStruct` root elements is now ported and wired into both ingest paths; engineering-side export lands in parallel.)**
24	6. **`logicStatements` (SCL-like network text) deferred.** It comes from `ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs` (2 097 lines, the largest file). Network nodes already carry title + reads/writes/calls without it; it ports in the step that builds `get_block`/`get_network` for the comment workflow.
25	7. **Tool surface this step = 3 tools** (`ingest_source`, `query`, `get_schema`), following the shipped mcp-engineering convention of plain verb_noun names with no server prefix.
26	
27	## 3. Input contract with mcp-engineering
28	
29	Facts verified against the Phase 1 implementation (`src/Mcp.Engineering/Adapter/TiaV17Adapter.cs`) and real output (`exported/TestPLCExportDemo/`):
30	
31	- Export folder is **flat**: `{BlockName} [{TypeCode}{Number}].xml`, e.g. `Main [OB1].xml`.
32	- Multi-PLC projects: one **subfolder per PLC** (`Path.Combine(outputDir, plc.Name)`), only when more than one PLC exists.
33	- No manifest file; files may be accompanied by unrelated working subfolders (e.g. `spike/`).
34	
35	Ingest rules:
36	
37	| Rule | Behaviour |
38	|---|---|
39	| Crawl | `*.xml` recursively under `exportRoot` |
40	| Classify | By XML **root element** (content, not filename): `SW.Blocks.OB/FB/FC` → program block; `SW.Blocks.GlobalDB/InstanceDB/ArrayDB/DB` → data block; `SW.Types.PlcStruct` → UDT (deferred → skipped with note); `SW.Tags.PlcTagTable` → tag table (deferred → skipped with note); anything else → warning, skipped |
41	| Duplicates | Same block identity (name+type from content) appearing in several files (e.g. `spike/reexport/` copies) → keep the file **closest to export root** (shallowest relative path, ties alphabetical), report skipped duplicates in `warnings[]` |
42	| Per-file failure | Malformed XML or unsupported schema → warning entry naming the file, continue with the rest. Abort only if **zero** files import successfully |
43	| Project node | `project:{exportRoot folder name}`, `CONTAINS` edges to every top-level object (matches reference behaviour) |
44	
45	**Amended 2026-07-18 (stage 3, manifest-first):** the export root layout is now `<exportRoot>/metadata.json` + `Blocks/*.xml` + `DB/*.xml`, manifest = PlcSourceExporter schema "1.0" (`components[]` with `name, sourcePath, category, status, exportedFile`, …). When the manifest exists it is authoritative: components with `status == "Exported"` and a non-empty `exportedFile` are imported ordered by `sourcePath`; `OB/FB/FC` → program-block import, `DB` → data-block import, `UDT`/`Tags` → deferred warning. The root-element crawl table above applies only to the manifest-less fallback (and to stage-1/2 fixtures). Reconciliation in manifest mode: Exported-but-missing file → warning; on-disk `*.xml` not referenced by any manifest entry → "not in manifest, ignored" warning. `exportedFile` may use `/` or `\`. `IngestResult` gained `source: "manifest" | "crawl"`.
46	
47	## 4. Architecture
48	
49	```
50	MCP Client (Inspector / Agent / App)
51	       │  stdio JSON-RPC
52	       ▼
53	┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
54	│  Mcp.Knowledge.exe                  │  net8 console app
55	│  ModelContextProtocol 1.4.1         │  same pin as Mcp.Engineering
56	│       │                             │
57	│  KnowledgeTools (MCP tools)         │  ingest_source / query / get_schema
58	│       │                             │
59	│  Importer ──► Graph ──► Store       │  ported from PlcSourceExporter.Core
60	│  (folder crawl)  (model)  (SQLite)  │  Microsoft.Data.Sqlite 8.0.x
61	└─────────────────────────────────────┘
62	```
63	
64	- **net8** per the framework split (no Siemens dependency at all — pure XML + SQLite).
65	- **Stateless per call**: each tool call opens the SQLite connection, does its work, closes. No session state, no `connect`/`disconnect` tools.
66	- **No `Contracts` dependency this step**: result DTOs live inside `Mcp.Knowledge`. Promote to `Contracts` when a second consumer (UI/agent) needs typed access.
67	- Logging to **stderr** only (stdout is the JSON-RPC channel), same as engineering.
68	- The reference project embeds `e_sqlite3.dll` as a resource because it is a net48 add-in; on net8 the `SQLitePCLRaw.bundle_e_sqlite3` transitive package handles native loading — the `NativeSqliteRuntime` extraction code is **not** ported.
69	
70	## 5. Database schema (ported verbatim)
71	
72	```sql
73	graph_nodes(id TEXT PK, kind TEXT, name TEXT)
74	graph_node_properties(node_id TEXT, name TEXT, value TEXT, PK(node_id, name))
75	graph_edges(id TEXT PK, from_node_id TEXT, to_node_id TEXT, type TEXT)
76	graph_edge_properties(edge_id TEXT, name TEXT, value TEXT, PK(edge_id, name))
77	-- + indices on kind, name, type, from_node_id, to_node_id
78	```
79	
80	Vocabulary produced by this step (subset of the reference; ~~UDT/tag kinds arrive later~~ **updated 2026-07-18, stage 4:** UDT/tag kinds now produced):
81	
82	- **Node kinds:** `Project`, `OB`, `FB`, `FC`, `Network`, `Instruction` (calls), `Variable` (symbols), `Global DB`, `Instance DB`, `DB Member`, `Data Type`, `UDT`, `UDT Member`, `PLC Tag`, `IO Address`
83	- **Edge types:** `CONTAINS`, `CALLS`, `READS`, `WRITES`, `HAS_TYPE`, `INSTANCE_OF`, `CONNECTED_TO`, `EXECUTES_BEFORE`, `EXECUTES_AFTER`
84	- **ID patterns:** `block:{Name}`, `network:{Block}:{Index}`, `instruction:{Block}:{N}:call:{Seq}`, `symbol:{Name}`, `db:{Name}`, `db-member:{Db}:{Path}`, `type:{Name}`, `udt:{Name}`, `udt-member:{Udt}:{Path}`, `tag:{Table}:{Name}:{Address}`, `io:{Address}` — deterministic: the same export always yields the same IDs.
85	- Reference behaviours kept: placeholder `block:{B}` node (`declaredByReference: true`) when a callee wasn't exported; symbol dedup by name; execution-order edges between consecutive networks; UDT import flattens **first-level members only** (reference behaviour — nested struct members are not expanded); tag tables get no project `CONTAINS` edge.
86	
87	`get_schema` returns the DDL plus this vocabulary (port of the reference `AGENT_SQLITE_GUIDE.md` essence) so agents can self-serve.
88	
89	## 6. Tool surface
90	
91	| Tool | Hint | Input | Output | Notes |
92	|---|---|---|---|---|
93	| `ingest_source` | write | `{ exportRoot: string, dbPath?: string }` | `IngestResult` | Crawl → classify → parse → rebuild DB. Default `dbPath` = `<exportRoot>/plc-knowledge.db` |
94	| `query` | read | `{ dbPath: string, sql: string, maxRows?: int }` | `{ columns[], rows[][], truncated: bool }` | Read-only SQL. Connection opened with `Mode=ReadOnly`; single statement, must start with `SELECT`/`WITH`/`EXPLAIN`; `maxRows` default 200, hard cap 1000, `truncated` flag set when cut |
95	| `get_schema` | read | — | `{ ddl: string, nodeKinds[], edgeTypes[], exampleQueries[] }` | Static content; no DB needed |
96	
97	`IngestResult`:
98	
99	```jsonc
100	{
101	  "dbPath": "C:\\...\\exported\\TestPLCExportDemo\\plc-knowledge.db",
102	  "filesFound": 4, "filesImported": 2,
103	  "nodes": 41, "edges": 55,
104	  "byKind": { "OB": 1, "FC": 1, "Network": 7, "Variable": 9 },
105	  "warnings": [ "skipped duplicate: spike/reexport/FC_LAD_SimulateCylinder_Call [FC1].xml" ],
106	  "durationMs": 320
107	}
108	```
109	
110	Deferred to later steps: `get_block`, `get_network`, `search` (arrive with `logicStatements` and the comment workflow); UDT/tag import (arrives with engineering-side export).
111	
112	## 7. Ingest pipeline
113	
114	```
115	ingest_source(exportRoot, dbPath?)
116	 1. Validate exportRoot exists → error EXPORT_ROOT_NOT_FOUND otherwise
117	 2. (2026-07-18) if <exportRoot>/metadata.json exists → manifest mode (§3 amendment):
118	    parse manifest (malformed → MANIFEST_INVALID), filter status=="Exported" + exportedFile,
119	    order by sourcePath, dispatch per category, reconciliation warnings
120	 3. else → crawl mode: crawl *.xml recursively (ordinal, sorted by relative path),
121	    classify each file by root element (§3); collect skips/duplicates as warnings
122	 4. Zero importable files → error NO_SOURCE_FILES (list what was found)
123	 5. Parse per category:
124	    - program block → ProgramSemanticReferenceBuilder.Parse() → block + network
125	      nodes, EXECUTES_* chains, call instructions, CALLS/READS/WRITES edges
126	    - data block   → XDocument walk of Interface sections → DB + member nodes,
127	      HAS_TYPE edges, INSTANCE_OF edge for instance DBs
128	 6. Save: open dbPath (create dir), delete-all + bulk insert in ONE transaction
129	 7. Return IngestResult with counts + warnings + source ("manifest" | "crawl")
130	```
131	
132	Error convention mirrors engineering: tool failures are normal tool results with `isError: true` and a structured `{ code, message, remediation }` payload. Codes: `EXPORT_ROOT_NOT_FOUND`, `NO_SOURCE_FILES`, `MANIFEST_INVALID` (2026-07-18: metadata.json present but not valid/readable JSON), `DB_LOCKED` (SQLite busy — likely another process holds the file), `QUERY_REJECTED` (non-read-only SQL), `DB_NOT_FOUND` (query against missing file).
133

168	## 10. Testing & exit criteria
169	
170	**Unit tests** (`tests/Mcp.Knowledge.Tests`):
171	
172	- Parser: fixture OB/FC → expected network count, titles, reads/writes/calls; fixture DBs → member tree, `INSTANCE_OF`.
173	- Store round-trip: import fixtures → save to temp DB → load → identical graph.
174	- Determinism: same fixtures ingested twice → identical node/edge IDs.
175	- Crawler: duplicate block in a subfolder is skipped with warning (mirrors the real `spike/reexport/` situation); unknown root element skipped.
176	- `query` guard: non-SELECT statements rejected.
177	
178	**E2E** (real data): add `scripts/e2e-knowledge.json` for the existing `scripts/mcp-e2e.mjs` runner: `ingest_source` on `exported/TestPLCExportDemo` → `query` node/edge counts → spot-check one network's READS. Then a manual MCP Inspector pass.
179	
180	**Exit criteria:**
181	
182	- `dotnet build` + `dotnet test` green.
183	- Ingest of `exported/TestPLCExportDemo` (2 real blocks: `Main [OB1]`, `FC_LAD_SimulateCylinder_Call [FC1]`) succeeds: exactly 2 block nodes; every `<SW.Blocks.CompileUnit>` in the source XMLs has a `Network` node; symbol Variable nodes exist for the FC's accesses; the FC's call produces `CALLS` + `Instruction` nodes and a placeholder callee if absent; duplicate copies under `spike/` are reported in `warnings[]`, not double-ingested.
184	- Re-ingest of the same folder yields identical IDs (verified via `query`).
185	- MCP Inspector walkthrough of all 3 tools passes.
186	
187	## 11. Risks & mitigations
188	
189	| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
190	|---|---|---|
191	| Port drift vs the reference implementation | Subtle parse regressions | Port verbatim where possible; provenance headers; port the reference's relevant unit-test cases into `tests/Mcp.Knowledge.Tests` |
192	| Engineering export layout changes (e.g. adds subfolders) | Ingest misses/duplicates files | Classification is content-based, not path-based; duplicate rule is deterministic |
193	| Concurrent ingest from two server instances | SQLite lock errors | Single-writer design documented; `DB_LOCKED` error with remediation; not a scenario for MVP (one agent) |
194	| V17 XML schema variants (ArrayDB etc.) | Files skipped silently | Explicit `warnings[]` per skipped file with reason; widen classifier as real files appear |
195

199	- No changes to mcp-engineering (its export output is already sufficient input).

Adds GetNetworkStatementTextByCompileUnitId() in ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter, wires it into TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter, updates SQLite agent guide with search query, and updates AI export guide reading order.

diff --git a/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs b/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs
index 767f769..1526db1 100644
--- a/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs
+++ b/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs
@@ -56,6 +56,23 @@ public static class ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter
             blocks.Sum(block => block.Networks.Count));
     }
 
+    public static IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> GetNetworkStatementTextByCompileUnitId(string xml, ProgramBlockComponent component)
+    {
+        if (component == null)
+        {
+            throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(component));
+        }
+
+        var block = ParseBlock(xml, component);
+        return block.Networks
+            .Where(network => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(network.CompileUnitId) && network.Statements.Count > 0)
+            .GroupBy(network => network.CompileUnitId, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
+            .ToDictionary(
+                group => group.Key,
+                group => string.Join("\n", group.First().Statements),
+                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
+    }
+
     private static LogicBlock ParseBlock(string xml, ProgramBlockComponent component)
     {
         XDocument document;
diff --git a/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/SemanticPlcGraph.cs b/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/SemanticPlcGraph.cs
index 9b3a1d1..9551cd4 100644
--- a/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/SemanticPlcGraph.cs
+++ b/src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/SemanticPlcGraph.cs
@@ -276,22 +276,30 @@ public static class TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter
             }));
 
         var parsed = ProgramSemanticReferenceBuilder.Parse(xml, component);
+        var logicStatementsByCompileUnitId = ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.GetNetworkStatementTextByCompileUnitId(xml, component);
         ProgramNetworkRecord? previousNetwork = null;
         foreach (var network in parsed.Networks)
         {
+            var networkProperties = new Dictionary<string, string>
+            {
+                ["block"] = network.Block,
+                ["language"] = network.Language,
+                ["title"] = network.Title,
+                ["networkIndex"] = network.NetworkIndex.ToString(System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture),
+                ["compileUnitId"] = network.CompileUnitId,
+                ["sourceFile"] = network.SourceFile
+            };
+            if (logicStatementsByCompileUnitId.TryGetValue(network.CompileUnitId, out var logicStatements) &&
+                !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(logicStatements))
+            {
+                networkProperties["logicStatements"] = logicStatements;
+            }
+
             graph.UpsertNode(new SemanticGraphNode(
                 network.Id,
                 SemanticNodeKind.Network,
                 $"{component.Name} Network {network.NetworkIndex}",

-                    ["compileUnitId"] = network.CompileUnitId,
-                    ["sourceFile"] = network.SourceFile
-                }));

             AddEdge(graph, blockId, network.Id, SemanticRelationshipType.Contains, new Dictionary<string, string>

                 ["sourceFile"] = network.SourceFile
@@ -1243,6 +1251,8 @@ public static class SemanticPlcGraphAgentGuide
 
         Folder paths are metadata only. Do not infer engineering semantics from folder structure. Prefer relationships in `graph_edges`.
 
+        Network nodes may have a `logicStatements` property. This is newline-delimited translated network logic from `translate\program-blocks.yaml` statements only, stored as plain text for quick agent inspection. Empty networks do not have this property.
+
         ## Schema
 
         ```sql
@@ -1310,6 +1320,21 @@ public static class SemanticPlcGraphAgentGuide
         ORDER BY block.name, CAST(idx.value AS INTEGER);
         ```
 
+        Search translated network logic:
+
+        ```sql
+        SELECT
+          network.id,
+          network.name,
+          logic.value AS logic_statements
+        FROM graph_nodes network
+        JOIN graph_node_properties logic
+          ON logic.node_id = network.id AND logic.name = 'logicStatements'
+        WHERE network.kind = 'Network'
+          AND logic.value LIKE '%Time_Base%'
+        ORDER BY network.id;
+        ```

20	    public int NetworkCount { get; }\r
21	}\r
22	\r
23	public static class ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter\r
24	{\r
25	    public const string FolderName = "translate";\r
26	    public const string FileName = "program-blocks.yaml";\r
27	    private const string SchemaVersion = "1.0";\r
28	\r
29	    public static ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult Write(string exportRoot, DateTimeOffset generatedUtc)
30	    {
31	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exportRoot))
32	        {
33	            throw new ArgumentException("Export root is required.", nameof(exportRoot));
34	        }\r
35	\r
36	        var components = ProgramBlockComponentCatalog.LoadExportedProgramBlocks(exportRoot);\r
37	        var blocks = new List<LogicBlock>();\r
38	        foreach (var component in components)\r
39	        {\r
40	            var filePath = Path.Combine(exportRoot, component.ExportedFile);\r
41	            if (!File.Exists(filePath))\r
42	            {\r
43	                continue;\r
44	            }\r
45	\r
46	            blocks.Add(ParseBlock(File.ReadAllText(filePath), component));\r
47	        }\r
48	\r
49	        var translateFolder = Path.Combine(exportRoot, FolderName);\r
50	        Directory.CreateDirectory(translateFolder);\r
51	        var yamlPath = Path.Combine(translateFolder, FileName);\r
52	        File.WriteAllText(yamlPath, Serialize(exportRoot, generatedUtc, blocks));\r
53	        return new ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult(\r
54	            yamlPath,\r
55	            blocks.Count,\r
56	            blocks.Sum(block => block.Networks.Count));
57	    }
58	
59	    public static IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> GetNetworkStatementTextByCompileUnitId(string xml, ProgramBlockComponent component)
60	    {
61	        if (component == null)
62	        {
63	            throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(component));
64	        }
65	
66	        var block = ParseBlock(xml, component);
67	        return block.Networks
68	            .Where(network => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(network.CompileUnitId) && network.Statements.Count > 0)
69	            .GroupBy(network => network.CompileUnitId, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
70	            .ToDictionary(
71	                group => group.Key,
72	                group => string.Join("\n", group.First().Statements),
73	                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
74	    }
75	
76	    private static LogicBlock ParseBlock(string xml, ProgramBlockComponent component)
77	    {
78	        XDocument document;
79	        try
80	        {
81	            document = XDocument.Parse(xml);\r
82	        }\r
83	        catch (XmlException)\r
84	        {\r
85	            return new LogicBlock(\r
86	                component.Name,\r
87	                component.Category,\r
88	                component.ExportedFile,\r
89	                string.Empty,\r
90	                new[]\r
91	                {\r
92	                    LogicNetwork.Untranslated(1, string.Empty, "Network 1", string.Empty, "Malformed XML source.")\r
93	                });\r
94	        }\r
95	\r
96	        var blockLanguage = GetBlockLanguage(document);\r
97	        var networks = document\r
98	            .Descendants()\r
99	            .Where(element => element.Name.LocalName == "SW.Blocks.CompileUnit")\r
100	            .Select((element, index) => ParseNetwork(element, index + 1, blockLanguage))\r
101	            .ToArray();\r
102	\r
103	        return new LogicBlock(\r
104	            component.Name,\r
105	            component.Category,\r
106	            component.ExportedFile,\r
107	            blockLanguage,\r
108	            networks);\r
109	    }\r
110	\r
111	    private static LogicNetwork ParseNetwork(XElement compileUnit, int networkIndex, string blockLanguage)\r
112	    {\r
113	        var compileUnitId = ((string?)compileUnit.Attribute("ID"))?.Trim() ?? string.Empty;\r
114	        var title = GetPreferredMultilingualText(compileUnit, "Title");\r
115	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(title))\r
116	        {\r
117	            title = $"Network {networkIndex}";\r
118	        }\r
119	\r
120	        var language = GetDirectAttributeValue(compileUnit, "ProgrammingLanguage");\r
121	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(language))\r
122	        {\r
123	            language = blockLanguage;\r
124	        }\r
125	\r
126	        if (IsLadOrFbd(language))\r
127	        {\r
128	            var flgNet = compileUnit.Descendants().FirstOrDefault(element => element.Name.LocalName == "FlgNet");\r
129	            if (flgNet == null)\r
130	            {\r
131	                if (IsEmptyNetworkSource(compileUnit))\r
132	                {\r
133	                    return new LogicNetwork(\r
134	                        networkIndex,\r
135	                        compileUnitId,\r
136	                        title,\r
137	                        language,\r
138	                        "scl-like",\r
139	                        "exact",\r
140	                        Array.Empty<string>(),\r
141	                        Array.Empty<string>());\r
142	                }\r
143	\r
144	                return LogicNetwork.Untranslated(\r
145	                    networkIndex,\r
146	                    compileUnitId,\r
147	                    title,\r
148	                    language,\r
149	                    "Unsupported network language or missing FlgNet source.");\r
150	            }\r
151	\r
152	            return TranslateFlgNet(flgNet, networkIndex, compileUnitId, title, language);\r
153	        }\r
154	\r
155	        if (string.Equals(language, "SCL", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))\r
156	        {\r
157	            var source = GetCompactSclSource(compileUnit);\r
158	            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(source))\r
159	            {\r
160	                return new LogicNetwork(\r
161	                    networkIndex,\r
162	                    compileUnitId,\r
163	                    title,\r
164	                    language,\r
165	                    "scl",\r
166	                    "exact",\r
167	                    SplitSclStatements(source),\r
168	                    Array.Empty<string>());\r
169	            }\r
170	\r
171	            if (HasStructuredTextSource(compileUnit) || IsEmptyNetworkSource(compileUnit))\r
172	            {\r
173	                return new LogicNetwork(\r
174	                    networkIndex,\r
175	                    compileUnitId,\r
176	                    title,\r
177	                    language,\r
178	                    "scl",\r
179	                    "exact",\r
180	                    Array.Empty<string>(),\r
181	                    Array.Empty<string>());\r
182	            }\r
183	        }\r
184	\r
185	        return LogicNetwork.Untranslated(\r
186	            networkIndex,\r
187	            compileUnitId,\r
188	            title,\r
189	            language,\r
190	            "Unsupported network language or missing FlgNet source.");\r
191	    }\r
192	\r
193	    private static LogicNetwork TranslateFlgNet(\r
194	        XElement flgNet,\r
195	        int networkIndex,\r

</details>

// Ported from PlcSourceExporter (src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/ProgramBlockCallGraph.cs) — adapted for mcp-knowledge; keep changes minimal to ease future re-syncs.
// Only the ProgramBlockComponent record class ports; the call-graph builder and the

namespace Mcp.Knowledge.Parsing;

public sealed class ProgramBlockComponent
{
    public ProgramBlockComponent(string name, string category, string sourcePath, string exportedFile)
    {
        Name = name;
        Category = category;
        SourcePath = sourcePath;
        ExportedFile = exportedFile;
    }

    public string Name { get; }

    public string Category { get; }

    public string SourcePath { get; }

    public string ExportedFile { get; }
}

1	using System.ComponentModel;
2	using System.Diagnostics;
3	using Mcp.Knowledge.Graph;
4	using Mcp.Knowledge.Import;
5	using Microsoft.Data.Sqlite;
6	using ModelContextProtocol.Protocol;
7	using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
8	
9	namespace Mcp.Knowledge.Tools;
10	
11	/// <summary>
12	/// MCP tool surface for mcp-knowledge (buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md §6).
13	/// Failures are normal tool results with isError=true + { code, message, remediation } (§7).
14	/// </summary>
15	[McpServerToolType]
16	public sealed class KnowledgeTools
17	{
18	    private const int DefaultMaxRows = 200;
19	    private const int HardMaxRows = 1000;
20	
21	    [McpServerTool(Name = "get_schema")]
22	    [Description("SQLite property-graph schema of the PLC knowledge base: table DDL, node kinds, edge types and example read-only queries (read-only, static content).")]
23	    public CallToolResult GetSchema() => Invoke(() => new
24	    {
25	        ddl = PlcSemanticGraphSqliteSchema.CreateScript,
26	        nodeKinds = SchemaVocabulary.NodeKinds,
27	        edgeTypes = SchemaVocabulary.EdgeTypes,
28	        exampleQueries = SchemaVocabulary.ExampleQueries,
29	    });
30	
31	    [McpServerTool(Name = "ingest_source")]
32	    [Description("Crawl a folder of TIA Openness XML exports, build the PLC property graph and write it as a SQLite knowledge base (write: full rebuild of dbPath; duplicates and unsupported files are skipped with warnings).")]
33	    public CallToolResult IngestSource(
34	        [Description("Export folder filled by mcp-engineering export_block / export_all_blocks.")] string exportRoot,
35	        [Description("SQLite output path. Default: <exportRoot>/plc-knowledge.db.")] string? dbPath = null)
36	        => Invoke(() => Ingest(exportRoot, dbPath));
37	
38	    [McpServerTool(Name = "query")]
39	    [Description("Run a single read-only SQL statement (SELECT / WITH / EXPLAIN) against a PLC knowledge base (read-only).")]
40	    public CallToolResult Query(
41	        [Description("Path to the plc-knowledge.db file.")] string dbPath,
42	        [Description("One read-only SQL statement; must start with SELECT, WITH or EXPLAIN.")] string sql,
43	        [Description("Maximum rows to return (default 200, hard cap 1000).")] int? maxRows = null)
44	        => Invoke(() => RunQuery(dbPath, sql, maxRows));
45	
46	    private static object Ingest(string exportRoot, string? dbPath)
47	    {
48	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exportRoot) || !Directory.Exists(exportRoot))
49	        {
50	            throw new KnowledgeToolException(
51	                "EXPORT_ROOT_NOT_FOUND",
52	                $"Export root '{exportRoot}' was not found.",
53	                "Pass the folder filled by mcp-engineering export_block / export_all_blocks.");
54	        }
55	
56	        var stopwatch = Stopwatch.StartNew();
57	        var import = ExportFolderCrawler.Import(exportRoot);
58	        if (import.FilesImported == 0)
59	        {
60	            var details = import.Warnings.Count == 0
61	                ? "No .xml files found."
62	                : string.Join(" ", import.Warnings);
63	            throw new KnowledgeToolException(
64	                "NO_SOURCE_FILES",
65	                $"Nothing importable under '{exportRoot}': {import.FilesFound} .xml file(s) found, 0 imported. {details}",
66	                "Point exportRoot at a folder of TIA Openness block exports (SW.Blocks.* content).");
67	        }
68	
69	        var targetPath = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dbPath)
70	            ? Path.Combine(exportRoot, "plc-knowledge.db")
71	            : dbPath;
72	        SqliteSemanticGraphStore.Save(targetPath, import.Graph);
73	        stopwatch.Stop();
74	
75	        var byKind = new SortedDictionary<string, int>(StringComparer.Ordinal);
76	        foreach (var group in import.Graph.Nodes.GroupBy(node => node.Kind))
77	        {
78	            byKind[group.Key] = group.Count();
79	        }
80	
81	        return new
82	        {
83	            dbPath = targetPath,
84	            source = import.Source,
85	            filesFound = import.FilesFound,
86	            filesImported = import.FilesImported,
87	            nodes = import.Graph.Nodes.Count,
88	            edges = import.Graph.Edges.Count,
89	            byKind,
90	            warnings = import.Warnings,
91	            durationMs = stopwatch.ElapsedMilliseconds,
92	        };
93	    }
94	
95	    private static object RunQuery(string dbPath, string sql, int? maxRows)
96	    {
97	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dbPath) || !File.Exists(dbPath))
98	        {
99	            throw new KnowledgeToolException(
100	                "DB_NOT_FOUND",
101	                $"Database '{dbPath}' was not found.",
102	                "Run ingest_source first, or check the dbPath.");
103	        }
104	
105	        var statement = ValidateReadOnlyStatement(sql);
106	        var limit = maxRows is null ? DefaultMaxRows : Math.Clamp(maxRows.Value, 1, HardMaxRows);
107	
108	        SqliteSemanticGraphStore.EnsureSqliteInitialized();
109	        var connectionString = new SqliteConnectionStringBuilder
110	        {
111	            DataSource = dbPath,
112	            Mode = SqliteOpenMode.ReadOnly,
113	        }.ToString();
114	        using var connection = new SqliteConnection(connectionString);
115	        connection.Open();
116	        using var command = connection.CreateCommand();
117	        command.CommandText = statement;
118	        using var reader = command.ExecuteReader();
119	
120	        var columns = Enumerable.Range(0, reader.FieldCount).Select(reader.GetName).ToArray();
121	        var rows = new List<object?[]>();
122	        var truncated = false;
123	        while (reader.Read())
124	        {
125	            if (rows.Count == limit)
126	            {
127	                truncated = true;
128	                break;
129	            }
130	
131	            var row = new object?[reader.FieldCount];
132	            for (var index = 0; index < row.Length; index++)
133	            {
134	                var value = reader.IsDBNull(index) ? null : reader.GetValue(index);
135	                row[index] = value is byte[] bytes ? Convert.ToBase64String(bytes) : value;
136	            }
137	
138	            rows.Add(row);
139	        }
140	
141	        return new { columns, rows, truncated };
142	    }
143	
144	    private static string ValidateReadOnlyStatement(string sql)
145	    {
146	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sql))
147	        {
148	            throw new KnowledgeToolException(
149	                "QUERY_REJECTED",
150	                "SQL must not be empty.",
151	                "Pass a single SELECT, WITH or EXPLAIN statement.");
152	        }
153	
154	        var statement = sql.Trim();
155	        if (statement.EndsWith(';'))
156	        {
157	            statement = statement[..^1].TrimEnd();
158	        }
159	
160	        if (statement.Length == 0 || statement.Contains(';'))
161	        {
162	            throw new KnowledgeToolException(
163	                "QUERY_REJECTED",
164	                "Only a single statement is allowed.",
165	                "Remove the extra statements and run them as separate query calls.");
166	        }
167	
168	        if (!statement.StartsWith("SELECT", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
169	            !statement.StartsWith("WITH", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
170	            !statement.StartsWith("EXPLAIN", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
171	        {
172	            throw new KnowledgeToolException(
173	                "QUERY_REJECTED",
174	                "Only read-only SELECT, WITH or EXPLAIN statements are allowed.",
175	                "Rewrite as a SELECT; the connection is opened read-only as a backstop.");
176	        }
177	
178	        return statement;
179	    }
180	
181	    private static CallToolResult Invoke(Func<object> action)
182	    {
183	        try
184	        {
185	            return ToolJson.Ok(action());
186	        }
187	        catch (KnowledgeToolException ex)
188	        {
189	            return ToolJson.Fail(ex.Code, ex.Message, ex.Remediation);
190	        }
191	        catch (ManifestInvalidException ex)
192	        {
193	            return ToolJson.Fail(
194	                "MANIFEST_INVALID",
195	                ex.Message,
196	                "Fix the manifest, or delete metadata.json to use the root-element folder crawl instead.");
197	        }
198	        catch (SqliteException ex) when (ex.SqliteErrorCode is 5 or 6) // SQLITE_BUSY / SQLITE_LOCKED
199	        {
200	            return ToolJson.Fail(
201	                "DB_LOCKED",
202	                ex.Message,
203	                "Another process holds the database file; close it and retry.");
204	        }
205	        catch (Exception ex)
206	        {
207	            return ToolJson.Fail("UNEXPECTED_ERROR", ex.Message);
208	        }
209	    }
210	}

</details>

1	// New code (not ported): get_schema payload vocabulary for mcp-knowledge.
2	// Node kinds / edge types are the subset produced by this step (buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md §5);
3	// example queries are lifted from SemanticPlcGraphAgentGuide.Content.
4	namespace Mcp.Knowledge.Graph;
5	
6	public sealed record SchemaExampleQuery(string Name, string Sql);
7	
8	public static class SchemaVocabulary
9	{
10	    public static readonly IReadOnlyList<string> NodeKinds = new[]
11	    {
12	        SemanticNodeKind.Project,
13	        SemanticNodeKind.OrganizationBlock,
14	        SemanticNodeKind.FunctionBlock,
15	        SemanticNodeKind.Function,
16	        SemanticNodeKind.Network,
17	        SemanticNodeKind.Instruction,
18	        SemanticNodeKind.Variable,
19	        SemanticNodeKind.GlobalDataBlock,
20	        SemanticNodeKind.InstanceDataBlock,
21	        SemanticNodeKind.DataBlockMember,
22	        SemanticNodeKind.DataType,
23	        SemanticNodeKind.UserDataType,
24	        SemanticNodeKind.UserDataTypeMember,
25	        SemanticNodeKind.PlcTag,
26	        SemanticNodeKind.IoAddress,
27	    };
28	
29	    public static readonly IReadOnlyList<string> EdgeTypes = new[]
30	    {
31	        SemanticRelationshipType.Contains,
32	        SemanticRelationshipType.Calls,
33	        SemanticRelationshipType.Reads,
34	        SemanticRelationshipType.Writes,
35	        SemanticRelationshipType.HasType,
36	        SemanticRelationshipType.InstanceOf,
37	        SemanticRelationshipType.ConnectedTo,
38	        SemanticRelationshipType.ExecutesBefore,
39	        SemanticRelationshipType.ExecutesAfter,
40	    };
41	
42	    public static readonly IReadOnlyList<SchemaExampleQuery> ExampleQueries = new[]
43	    {
44	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
45	            "Count node kinds",
46	            "SELECT kind, COUNT(*) AS count\nFROM graph_nodes\nGROUP BY kind\nORDER BY kind;"),
47	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
48	            "Count relationship types",
49	            "SELECT type, COUNT(*) AS count\nFROM graph_edges\nGROUP BY type\nORDER BY type;"),
50	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
51	            "List blocks",
52	            "SELECT id, kind, name\nFROM graph_nodes\nWHERE kind IN ('OB', 'FB', 'FC')\nORDER BY kind, name;"),
53	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
54	            "Which blocks call which blocks",
55	            """
56	            SELECT
57	              caller.kind AS caller_kind,
58	              caller.name AS caller,
59	              callee.kind AS callee_kind,
60	              callee.name AS callee,
61	              sf.value AS source_file,
62	              ni.value AS network_index
63	            FROM graph_edges e
64	            JOIN graph_nodes caller ON caller.id = e.from_node_id
65	            JOIN graph_nodes callee ON callee.id = e.to_node_id
66	            LEFT JOIN graph_edge_properties sf
67	              ON sf.edge_id = e.id AND sf.name = 'sourceFile'
68	            LEFT JOIN graph_edge_properties ni
69	              ON ni.edge_id = e.id AND ni.name = 'networkIndex'
70	            WHERE e.type = 'CALLS'
71	              AND caller.kind IN ('OB', 'FB', 'FC')
72	              AND callee.kind IN ('OB', 'FB', 'FC')
73	            ORDER BY caller.name, CAST(ni.value AS INTEGER), callee.name;
74	            """),
75	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
76	            "Which blocks read or write variables",
77	            """
78	            SELECT
79	              e.type AS access,
80	              block.kind AS block_kind,
81	              block.name AS block_name,
82	              variable.kind AS variable_kind,
83	              variable.name AS variable_name,
84	              sf.value AS source_file,
85	              ni.value AS network_index
86	            FROM graph_edges e
87	            JOIN graph_nodes block ON block.id = e.from_node_id
88	            JOIN graph_nodes variable ON variable.id = e.to_node_id
89	            LEFT JOIN graph_edge_properties sf
90	              ON sf.edge_id = e.id AND sf.name = 'sourceFile'
91	            LEFT JOIN graph_edge_properties ni
92	              ON ni.edge_id = e.id AND ni.name = 'networkIndex'
93	            WHERE e.type IN ('READS', 'WRITES')
94	              AND block.kind IN ('OB', 'FB', 'FC', 'Network')
95	            ORDER BY variable.name, access, block.name, CAST(ni.value AS INTEGER);
96	            """),
97	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
98	            "DB members and data types",
99	            """
100	            SELECT
101	              db.kind AS db_kind,
102	              db.name AS db_name,
103	              member.name AS member_name,
104	              dtype.name AS data_type
105	            FROM graph_edges contains
106	            JOIN graph_nodes db ON db.id = contains.from_node_id
107	            JOIN graph_nodes member ON member.id = contains.to_node_id
108	            LEFT JOIN graph_edges typed
109	              ON typed.from_node_id = member.id AND typed.type = 'HAS_TYPE'
110	            LEFT JOIN graph_nodes dtype ON dtype.id = typed.to_node_id
111	            WHERE contains.type = 'CONTAINS'
112	              AND db.kind IN ('Global DB', 'Instance DB')
113	              AND member.kind = 'DB Member'
114	            ORDER BY db.name, member.name;
115	            """),
116	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
117	            "Instance DB to FB relationship",
118	            "SELECT db.name AS instance_db, fb.name AS function_block\nFROM graph_edges e\nJOIN graph_nodes db ON db.id = e.from_node_id\nJOIN graph_nodes fb ON fb.id = e.to_node_id\nWHERE e.type = 'INSTANCE_OF'\nORDER BY db.name;"),
119	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
120	            "PLC tags connected to IO addresses",
121	            """
122	            SELECT
123	              tag.name AS tag_name,
124	              address.name AS io_address,
125	              dtype.name AS data_type
126	            FROM graph_edges connected
127	            JOIN graph_nodes tag ON tag.id = connected.from_node_id
128	            JOIN graph_nodes address ON address.id = connected.to_node_id
129	            LEFT JOIN graph_edges typed
130	              ON typed.from_node_id = tag.id AND typed.type = 'HAS_TYPE'
131	            LEFT JOIN graph_nodes dtype ON dtype.id = typed.to_node_id
132	            WHERE connected.type = 'CONNECTED_TO'
133	              AND tag.kind = 'PLC Tag'
134	              AND address.kind = 'IO Address'
135	            ORDER BY address.name, tag.name;
136	            """),
137	        new SchemaExampleQuery(
138	            "UDT members and data types",
139	            """
140	            SELECT
141	              udt.name AS udt_name,
142	              member.name AS member_name,
143	              dtype.name AS data_type
144	            FROM graph_edges contains
145	            JOIN graph_nodes udt ON udt.id = contains.from_node_id
146	            JOIN graph_nodes member ON member.id = contains.to_node_id
147	            LEFT JOIN graph_edges typed
148	              ON typed.from_node_id = member.id AND typed.type = 'HAS_TYPE'
149	            LEFT JOIN graph_nodes dtype ON dtype.id = typed.to_node_id
150	            WHERE contains.type = 'CONTAINS'
151	              AND udt.kind = 'UDT'
152	              AND member.kind = 'UDT Member'
153	            ORDER BY udt.name, member.name;
154	            """),
155	    };
156	}

529	    private static string Serialize(string exportRoot, DateTimeOffset generatedUtc, IReadOnlyList<LogicBlock> blocks)\r
530	    {\r
531	        var builder = new StringBuilder();\r
532	        builder.Append("schemaVersion: ").AppendLine(Quote(SchemaVersion));\r
533	        builder.Append("generatedUtc: ").AppendLine(Quote(generatedUtc.UtcDateTime.ToString("O", System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)));\r
534	        builder.Append("exportRoot: ").AppendLine(Quote(exportRoot));\r
535	        builder.AppendLine("blocks:");\r
536	        foreach (var block in blocks)\r
537	        {\r
538	            builder.Append("  - name: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.Name));\r
539	            builder.Append("    kind: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.Kind));\r
540	            builder.Append("    sourceFile: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.SourceFile));\r
541	            builder.Append("    programmingLanguage: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.ProgrammingLanguage));\r
542	            builder.AppendLine("    networks:");\r
543	            foreach (var network in block.Networks)\r
544	            {\r
545	                builder.Append("      - networkIndex: ").AppendLine(network.NetworkIndex.ToString(System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));\r
546	                builder.Append("        compileUnitId: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.CompileUnitId));\r
547	                builder.Append("        title: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.Title));\r
548	                builder.Append("        language: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.Language));\r
549	                builder.AppendLine("        translation:");\r
550	                builder.Append("          language: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.TranslationLanguage));\r
551	                builder.Append("          confidence: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.Confidence));\r
552	                builder.AppendLine("          statements:");\r
553	                WriteStringArray(builder, network.Statements, "            ");\r
554	                builder.AppendLine("          notes:");\r
555	                WriteStringArray(builder, network.Notes, "            ");\r
556	            }\r
557	        }\r
558	\r
559	        return builder.ToString();\r
560	    }\r
561	\r
562	    private static void WriteStringArray(StringBuilder builder, IReadOnlyList<string> values, string indent)\r
563	    {\r
564	        if (values.Count == 0)\r
565	        {\r
566	            builder.Append(indent).AppendLine("[]");\r
567	            return;\r
568	        }\r
569	\r
570	        foreach (var value in values)\r
571	        {\r
572	            builder.Append(indent).Append("- ").AppendLine(Quote(value));\r
573	        }\r
574	    }\r
575	\r
576	    private static string Quote(string value)\r
577	    {\r
578	        var builder = new StringBuilder(value.Length + 2);\r
579	        builder.Append('"');\r
580	        foreach (var character in value)\r
581	        {\r
582	            switch (character)\r
583	            {\r
584	                case '\\':\r
585	                    builder.Append("\\\\");\r
586	                    break;\r
587	                case '"':\r
588	                    builder.Append("\\\"");\r
589	                    break;\r
590	                case '\r':\r
591	                    builder.Append("\\r");\r
592	                    break;\r
593	                case '\n':\r
594	                    builder.Append("\\n");\r
595	                    break;\r
596	                case '\t':\r
597	                    builder.Append("\\t");\r
598	                    break;\r
599	                default:\r
600	                    if (char.IsControl(character))\r
601	                    {\r
602	                        builder.Append("\\u").Append(((int)character).ToString("x4", System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));\r
603	                    }\r
604	                    else\r
605	                    {\r
606	                        builder.Append(character);\r
607	                    }\r
608	\r
609	                    break;\r
610	            }\r
611	        }\r
612	\r
613	        builder.Append('"');\r
614	        return builder.ToString();\r
615	    }\r
616	\r

From the reference read:
```
    public const string FolderName = "translate";\r
    public const string FileName = "program-blocks.yaml";\r
    private const string SchemaVersion = "1.0";\r
\r
    public static ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult Write(string exportRoot, DateTimeOffset generatedUtc)\r
    {\r
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exportRoot))\r
        {\r
            throw new ArgumentException("Export root is required.", nameof(exportRoot));\r
        }\r
\r
        var components = ProgramBlockComponentCatalog.LoadExportedProgramBlocks(exportRoot);\r
        var blocks = new List<LogicBlock>();\r
        foreach (var component in components)\r
        {\r
            var filePath = Path.Combine(exportRoot, component.ExportedFile);\r
            if (!File.Exists(filePath))\r
            {\r
                continue;\r
            }\r
\r
            blocks.Add(ParseBlock(File.ReadAllText(filePath), component));\r
        }\r
\r
        var translateFolder = Path.Combine(exportRoot, FolderName);\r
        Directory.CreateDirectory(translateFolder);\r
        var yamlPath = Path.Combine(translateFolder, FileName);\r
        File.WriteAllText(yamlPath, Serialize(exportRoot, generatedUtc, blocks));\r
        return new ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult(\r
            yamlPath,\r
            blocks.Count,\r
            blocks.Sum(block => block.Networks.Count));\r
    }\r
```
(line 58 blank, no \r — LF)

    public const string FolderName = "translate";\r
    public const string FileName = "program-blocks.yaml";\r
    private const string SchemaVersion = "1.0";\r
\r
    public static ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult Write(string exportRoot, DateTimeOffset generatedUtc)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exportRoot))
        {
            throw new ArgumentException("Export root is required.", nameof(exportRoot));\r
        }\r
\r
        var components = ProgramBlockComponentCatalog.LoadExportedProgramBlocks(exportRoot);\r
        var blocks = new List<LogicBlock>();\r
        foreach (var component in components)\r
        {\r
            var filePath = Path.Combine(exportRoot, component.ExportedFile);\r
            if (!File.Exists(filePath))\r
            {\r
                continue;\r
            }\r
\r
            blocks.Add(ParseBlock(File.ReadAllText(filePath), component));\r
        }\r
\r
        var translateFolder = Path.Combine(exportRoot, FolderName);\r
        Directory.CreateDirectory(translateFolder);\r
        var yamlPath = Path.Combine(translateFolder, FileName);\r
        File.WriteAllText(yamlPath, Serialize(exportRoot, generatedUtc, blocks));\r
        return new ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult(\r
            yamlPath,\r
            blocks.Count,\r
            blocks.Sum(block => block.Networks.Count));
    }

```
    public const string FolderName = "translate";
    public const string FileName = "program-blocks.yaml";
    private const string SchemaVersion = "1.0";

    public static ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult Write(string exportRoot, DateTimeOffset generatedUtc)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exportRoot))
        {
            throw new ArgumentException("Export root is required.", nameof(exportRoot));
        }

        var components = ProgramBlockComponentCatalog.LoadExportedProgramBlocks(exportRoot);
        var blocks = new List<LogicBlock>();
        foreach (var component in components)
        {
            var filePath = Path.Combine(exportRoot, component.ExportedFile);
            if (!File.Exists(filePath))
            {
                continue;
            }

            blocks.Add(ParseBlock(File.ReadAllText(filePath), component));
        }

        var translateFolder = Path.Combine(exportRoot, FolderName);
        Directory.CreateDirectory(translateFolder);
        var yamlPath = Path.Combine(translateFolder, FileName);
        File.WriteAllText(yamlPath, Serialize(exportRoot, generatedUtc, blocks));
        return new ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult(
            yamlPath,
            blocks.Count,
            blocks.Sum(block => block.Networks.Count));
    }

```

Line 529-617 content:
```
    private static string Serialize(string exportRoot, DateTimeOffset generatedUtc, IReadOnlyList<LogicBlock> blocks)
    {
        var builder = new StringBuilder();
        builder.Append("schemaVersion: ").AppendLine(Quote(SchemaVersion));
        builder.Append("generatedUtc: ").AppendLine(Quote(generatedUtc.UtcDateTime.ToString("O", System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)));
        builder.Append("exportRoot: ").AppendLine(Quote(exportRoot));
        builder.AppendLine("blocks:");
        foreach (var block in blocks)
        {
            builder.Append("  - name: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.Name));
            builder.Append("    kind: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.Kind));
            builder.Append("    sourceFile: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.SourceFile));
            builder.Append("    programmingLanguage: ").AppendLine(Quote(block.ProgrammingLanguage));
            builder.AppendLine("    networks:");
            foreach (var network in block.Networks)
            {
                builder.Append("      - networkIndex: ").AppendLine(network.NetworkIndex.ToString(System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
                builder.Append("        compileUnitId: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.CompileUnitId));
                builder.Append("        title: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.Title));
                builder.Append("        language: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.Language));
                builder.AppendLine("        translation:");
                builder.Append("          language: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.TranslationLanguage));
                builder.Append("          confidence: ").AppendLine(Quote(network.Confidence));
                builder.AppendLine("          statements:");
                WriteStringArray(builder, network.Statements, "            ");
                builder.AppendLine("          notes:");
                WriteStringArray(builder, network.Notes, "            ");
            }
        }

        return builder.ToString();
    }

    private static void WriteStringArray(StringBuilder builder, IReadOnlyList<string> values, string indent)
    {
        if (values.Count == 0)
        {
            builder.Append(indent).AppendLine("[]");
            return;
        }

        foreach (var value in values)
        {
            builder.Append(indent).Append("- ").AppendLine(Quote(value));
        }
    }

    private static string Quote(string value)
    {
        var builder = new StringBuilder(value.Length + 2);
        builder.Append('"');
        foreach (var character in value)
        {
            switch (character)
            {
                case '\\':
                    builder.Append("\\\\");
                    break;
                case '"':
                    builder.Append("\\\"");
                    break;
                case '\r':
                    builder.Append("\\r");
                    break;
                case '\n':
                    builder.Append("\\n");
                    break;
                case '\t':
                    builder.Append("\\t");
                    break;
                default:
                    if (char.IsControl(character))
                    {
                        builder.Append("\\u").Append(((int)character).ToString("x4", System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        builder.Append(character);
                    }

                    break;
            }
        }

        builder.Append('"');
        return builder.ToString();
    }

    private sealed class FlgNetContext
    {
```

// Ported from PlcSourceExporter (src/PlcSourceExporter.Core/ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs) — adapted for mcp-knowledge; keep changes minimal to ease future re-syncs.
// Only the FlgNet → SCL-like translator ports (GetNetworkStatementTextByCompileUnitId and everything below);
// the YAML file generation (Write / Serialize / Quote, ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult) stays behind — mcp-knowledge
// stores translated statements as the `logicStatements` network property, not a translate\program-blocks.yaml file.
using System.Text;
using System.Xml;
using System.Xml.Linq;

namespace Mcp.Knowledge.Parsing;

public static class ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter
{
    public static IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> GetNetworkStatementTextByCompileUnitId(string xml, ProgramBlockComponent component)
    {
        if (component == null)
        {
            throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(component));
        }

        var block = ParseBlock(xml, component);
        return block.Networks
            .Where(network => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(network.CompileUnitId) && network.Statements.Count > 0)
            .GroupBy(network => network.CompileUnitId, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
            .ToDictionary(
                group => group.Key,
1963 src/Mcp.Knowledge/Parsing/ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs


</details>

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

Both port and wiring are done. Update todos, then move to the new tools (get_block, get_network, search).

For the tools, I need to design the query implementations. They go in KnowledgeTools.cs. Let me think about the SQL and result shapes:

`get_block { dbPath, block }`:
- Find the block node: `SELECT id, kind, name FROM graph_nodes WHERE name = @name AND kind IN ('OB','FB','FC','Global DB','Instance DB')` — hmm, should get_block work for DBs too? The comment workflow cares about program blocks (OB/FB/FC). But a "get_block" that also handles DBs could return members. Keep scope tight: program blocks only (OB/FB/FC). If not found → error BLOCK_NOT_FOUND with remediation.
- Networks: join CONTAINS edges block→network, get network properties (networkIndex, compileUnitId, title, language, logicStatements, sourceFile), order by CAST(networkIndex AS INTEGER).
- Per network also: reads/writes (symbols) and calls? The network-level READS/WRITES edges: from network id → symbol id, edge properties include networkId etc. Calls: CALLS edges from block→callee with networkIndex property, plus Instruction nodes per network.

Result shape:
```json
{
  "block": { "id": "block:Main", "kind": "OB", "name": "Main", "sourceFile": "...", "folderPath": "..." },
  "networks": [
    { "index": 1, "compileUnitId": "9", "title": "...", "language": "LAD",
      "logicStatements": "...",   // null/absent when none
      "reads": ["A","B"], "writes": ["C"], "calls": [{"callee": "FC1", "calleeKind": "FC"}] }
  ]
}
```

Hmm — is per-network reads/writes/calls needed for get_block? It makes the response big but it's exactly the network context the comment workflow needs. The reference guide shows READS/WRITES queryable per network. But minimalism: get_block = block + networks (with logic text); get_network = single network full detail including reads/writes/calls. That division is cleaner:

- `get_block`: block info + ordered network list, each with index/compileUnitId/title/language/logicStatements (the full logic text of the block — the primary artifact for commenting). Plus maybe block-level calls summary? Skip.
- `get_network { dbPath, block, networkIndex }`: one network: same fields + reads[], writes[], calls[] detail.
- `search { dbPath, text, kind?, maxRows? }`: LIKE search over node names + selected property values (title, logicStatements). Return matches with node id/kind/name + which field matched + snippet.

Wait, but for the comment workflow (insert AI comments per network), the agent needs to see the network's logic + what it reads/writes to write a good comment. get_network giving full detail per network, get_block giving the overview with logic — reasonable. Keep per-network reads/writes/calls out of get_block to keep payloads sane.

Implementation approach: all three tools open the DB read-only and run parameterized SQL (LIKE with parameter for search to avoid injection; name = @name exact match OrdinalIgnoreCase? SQLite = is case-insensitive for ASCII by default? No — SQLite = is case-sensitive for = unless COLLATE NOCASE. Block names: exact match expected; but agents may pass wrong case. Use `COLLATE NOCASE`? Hmm, deterministic IDs are case-sensitive. Safer: exact match first... keep it simple: `WHERE name = @name COLLATE NOCASE AND kind IN (...)`. If multiple match (different case), take... ORDER BY exact match first? Overkill. Use COLLATE NOCASE and take the first; ambiguous case is unlikely.

For search: `WHERE name LIKE @pattern ESCAPE '\'`? LIKE case-insensitive for ASCII by default in SQLite. Good for search. Escape %/_ in user text? Agents pass plain text; escaping % and _ is polite. Keep simple: escape `\`, `%`, `_` with ESCAPE '\'.

Search scope: match against node name OR property value for properties 'title' and 'logicStatements' (and maybe 'sourceFile'? no). Two queries UNION: 
```sql
SELECT n.id, n.kind, n.name, 'name' AS matchedIn, NULL AS snippet FROM graph_nodes n WHERE n.name LIKE @p
UNION ALL
SELECT n.id, n.kind, n.name, p.name AS matchedIn, p.value AS snippet FROM graph_nodes n JOIN graph_node_properties p ON p.node_id = n.id AND p.name IN ('title','logicStatements') WHERE p.value LIKE @p
ORDER BY kind, id LIMIT @limit
```
Snippet for logicStatements could be long — truncate to e.g. 200 chars in C#. Also dedupe? A node could match both name and property — that's fine, two rows with different matchedIn. Hmm, or keep it simple.

Also `kind?` optional filter (e.g. kind = 'Network').

maxRows for search: default 50, hard cap 200.

Error codes: reuse DB_NOT_FOUND; add BLOCK_NOT_FOUND / NETWORK_NOT_FOUND? The convention: { code, message, remediation }. For get_block with unknown block — is that an error or empty result? I'd say error BLOCK_NOT_FOUND with remediation "check the block name; list blocks via query ...". For get_network unknown index → NETWORK_NOT_FOUND.

Now — where to put the SQL? KnowledgeTools.cs currently holds everything (210 lines). Adding 3 tools with SQL each will grow it. Options: keep in KnowledgeTools (mirrors current style) or add a Graph/GraphQueries.cs helper. The reference had `PlcSemanticGraphQueries` which was dropped in the port. Hmm! The reference project HAS a queries class — let me check what it contains; maybe get_block/get_network/search should mirror it. Let me look at PlcSemanticGraphQueries in the reference.

Actually wait — the header said "Dropped PlcSemanticGraphQueries". Let me check what that class offers in the reference — if it already implements these helpers, porting it (or its essence) is the right move and keeps fidelity with the reference.
