result.GetProperty(\"network\");\n        Assert.Equal(\"network:Main:1\", network.GetProperty(\"id\").GetString());\n        Assert.Equal(\"3\", network.GetProperty(\"compileUnitId\").GetString());\n item.GetString()).ToArray();\n        Assert.Contains(\"Btn_ForwardCommand\", reads);\n        Assert.Equal(reads.Distinct().Count(), reads.Length);\n\n        var writes = tools = new KnowledgeTools();\n\n        var error = ToolResults.ErrorJson(tools.GetNetwork(db.Path, \"Main\", 42));\n\n        Assert.Equal(\"NETWORK_NOT_FOUND\", <     public static ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult Write(string exportRoot, DateTimeOffset generatedUtc)
<     {
<         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exportRoot))
<         {
<             throw new ArgumentException("Export root is required.", nameof(exportRoot));
<         }
< 
<         var components = ProgramBlockComponentCatalog.LoadExportedProgramBlocks(exportRoot);
<         var blocks = new List<LogicBlock>();
<         foreach (var component in components)
<         {
<             var filePath = Path.Combine(exportRoot, component.ExportedFile);
<             if (!File.Exists(filePath))
<             {
<                 continue;
<             }
< 
<             blocks.Add(ParseBlock(File.ReadAllText(filePath), component));
<         }
< 
<         var translateFolder = Path.Combine(exportRoot, FolderName);
<         Directory.CreateDirectory(translateFolder);
<         var yamlPath = Path.Combine(translateFolder, FileName);
<         File.WriteAllText(yamlPath, Serialize(exportRoot, generatedUtc, blocks));
<         return new ProgramBlockLogicYamlResult(
<             yamlPath,
<             blocks.Count,
<             blocks.Sum(block => block.Networks.Count));
<     }

## Done — 32 translation tests ported, all green on first run

**File created:** `tests/Mcp.Knowledge.Tests/ProgramBlockLogicTests.cs` (~1 150 lines, 79 KB) — the only file touched. Style follows `SemanticPlcGraphTests.cs` (sealed class, Allman braces, file-scoped namespace `Mcp.Knowledge.Tests`). Usings: the three prescribed (`System.Linq`, `Mcp.Knowledge.Parsing`, `Xunit`) plus `System.Collections.Generic`, which was unavoidable for the `IReadOnlyDictionary<string,string>` helper signatures.

**Tests ported: 32** — every network-logic translation test in the reference file (lines 309–1697), names kept identical, one test per reference test, reference order preserved:
`WritesCompactProgramBlockLogicYamlAfterMetadata`, `TranslatesSclStructuredTextFromOrderedXmlTokens`, `TranslatesLadSliceAccessModifiersAsBitAccesses`, `TranslatesProgramAlarmSigFromSetCoilOutput`, `TranslatesMoveEnoAsEnablePathForDownstreamLatch`, `TranslatesNamedCallInputsFromCallInfoParameters`, `TranslatesEnabledCallWithOnlyOpenParameters`, `TranslatesZeroInputTimeReadFunctionsAsOutputBindingCalls`, `TranslatesNamedLocalConstantsInLadExpressions`, `TranslatesMoveCardOutputsAndIn1Source`, `TranslatesBuiltInSrPartAndQOutput`, `TreatsEmptyStructuredTextNetworkAsExactEmpty`, `TranslatesReturnValueAndArrayOperands`, `TranslatesJumpToLabel`, `TranslatesPulseCoilAndIncrement`, `TranslatesInstanceInstructionParts`, `TranslatesLadNegatedBranchAndCompareExpressionsToSclLikeYaml`, `TranslatesParameterlessCallGuardedByNegatedContact`, `TranslatesSetAndResetCoils`, `TranslatesLatchCoilOutputAsInputCondition`, `TranslatesMoveDirectAssignment`, `TranslatesTimerOnDelayOutput`, `TranslatesPositiveContact`, `TranslatesCalcDirectAssignment`, `TranslatesInRangeExpression`, `TranslatesArithmeticPartsAsExpressions`, `TranslatesNegativeEdgeContactsAndBoxes`, `TranslatesTimerCounterAndTriggerParts`, `TranslatesGenericFunctionPartsAsExpressions`, `TranslatesRemainingMoveCoilAndRuntimeParts`, `EmitsUnsupportedNetworksAsUntranslatedYamlTrace`, `EmitsEmptyLadNetworksAsEmptyNetworkTrace`.

**Conversion mechanics:**
- FlgNet-only tests go through a `TranslateLadBlock(blockName, compileUnitId, flgNet)` helper that wraps the fragment in the exact same `<Document>/<SW.Blocks.FB>` template the reference's `ExportSingleLadBlockYaml` used (component: `FB`, `Blocks/{name}`, `{name}.xml`). Full-document tests embed the reference XML verbatim (`<?xml?>` + `<Document>`) and call `GetNetworkStatementTextByCompileUnitId` with components mapped from the FakeExportableObject args (`OrganizationBlock→OB`, `FunctionBlock→FB`, `Function→FC`).
- `Assert.Contains("- \"STMT\"", yaml)` → `Assert.Contains("STMT", statements)` with `statements = result.Values.SelectMany(v => v.Split('\n')).ToArray()`; `DoesNotContain` → against `string.Join("\n", result.Values)`. Expected strings copied character-for-character.
- YAML-structure assertions dropped as instructed (schemaVersion, generatedUtc, sourceFile, kind, title, language, confidence, translation:, notes:). `compileUnitId` assertions ported as `result.ContainsKey(...)` where the network yields statements (`cu-scl`, `cu-guarded-call`, `cu-latches`).
- Multi-block test #1: both blocks translated and merged via a `Merge` helper; the `UDT_Cell` / `Default tag table` exclusion assertions were dropped (those objects never reach the translator), `DataOnly`/`UId`/`part-contact`/`access-source` exclusions kept.

**Notable porting decisions (verified, not discrepancies):**
- `EmitsUnsupportedNetworksAsUntranslatedYamlTrace`: in **both** codebases `LogicNetwork.Untranslated` puts the diagnostic in `Notes` with empty `Statements` (checked reference writer line 1960 and ported line 1826 — identical). So the STL network produces no dictionary entry; ported as `Assert.Empty(result)` with an explanatory comment.
- `TreatsEmptyStructuredTextNetworkAsExactEmpty` / `EmitsEmptyLadNetworksAsEmptyNetworkTrace`: empty networks → `Assert.Empty(result)`.
- `Assert.DoesNotContain("- \"01\"", yaml)` ported as "no statement equals exactly `01`" (`Assert.DoesNotContain(statements, s => s == "01")`).

**Skipped reference tests** (pure export orchestration, per instructions): `ExportsNestedObjectsToFlatCategoryFolders`, `ContinuesAfterAnObjectExportFails`, `SkipsObjectsThatDeclareSkipReason`, `TreatsSiemensNotPermittedBlockExportAsSkipped`, `WritesComponentMetadataForExportedSkippedAndFailedObjects`, `DoesNotWriteRetiredDerivedArtifactsAfterExport`, `WritesBlockProfilesAndOptimizationHintsAfterSemanticExports`, `WritesAiExportGuideIntoExportRoot`, `WritesSemanticModelFilesUnderModelFolder`, `UsesInjectedSemanticModelWriterForAddInSafeOutOfProcessGeneration`, `ReportsProgressThroughObjectExportAndPostProcessing`, `ReportsExportedCountWithoutCountingObjectsFirst`, `StreamsExportWithoutPreEnumeratingAllObjects`, `HonorsCancellationBetweenExportedObjects`.

**Behavior discrepancies: none** — all expected statements matched the verbatim-ported translator on the first run; no assertion was weakened.

The step is complete. Summary of what landed:

**Translator port (respecting your polished conventions)**
- `src/Mcp.Knowledge/Parsing/ProgramBlockLogicYamlWriter.cs` — the FlgNet→SCL-like translator copied from the reference project, which already contains your translation fixes (`f65c728`). I diffed the port against the reference: byte-identical outside the intended removals (only the YAML file generation — `Write`/`Serialize`/`Quote` — stayed behind; mcp-knowledge stores statements in the DB instead). So the SCL-like text follows exactly the conventions you polished in `translate\program-blocks.yaml`.

**`logicStatements` in the graph**
- Network nodes now carry newline-joined statements (empty networks omit the property), mirroring reference commit `2b3dace` — `SemanticPlcGraph.cs:218`. Agent guide + `get_schema` vocabulary gained the "Search translated network logic" example.

**New query helpers** (`KnowledgeTools.cs`, +298 lines)
- `get_block(dbPath, block)` — OB/FB/FC + networks with index/compileUnitId/title/language/logicStatements.
- `get_network(dbPath, block, networkIndex)` — one network + distinct reads/writes + calls.
- `search(dbPath, text, kind?, maxRows?)` — case-insensitive substring over node names and title/logicStatements, snippets capped at 300 chars. Errors: `BLOCK_NOT_FOUND`, `NETWORK_NOT_FOUND`, `SEARCH_TEXT_REQUIRED`.

**Verification**
- 32 translation test cases ported from the reference (`ProgramBlockLogicTests.cs`) — every polished expectation passes unchanged; the porting agent reported zero behavior discrepancies.
- Wiring test + 8 tool tests (`QueryHelperToolTests.cs`); full suite **89/89 green**, solution builds with 0 warnings.
- E2E: `scripts/e2e-knowledge.json` extended to 14 steps — all pass against the real `exported/TestPLCExportDemo` (ingest 7 files → 109 nodes/279 edges, 18 networks with logic text).

**Docs** — stage-5 amendments in `buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md` (decisions 6–7, tool table, error codes, port map, step 3 marked done), plus `initialLaunch` and `agent.md` inventory.

## Turn 4

### User

which mcp tools is called to generate all sqlite data (plc context)? in my plan, this tool can be called by UI interface request, like a button named Read Project Context, or AI agent can call this function followed by user prompt. is that feasible for now?

### Assistant

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

The user asks: which MCP tool(s) are called to generate all sqlite data (PLC context)? They want to know if it's feasible to wire this to a UI button ("Read Project Context") or have an AI agent call it after a user prompt.

This is a question, not a task. Answer directly and concisely.

The answer:

The full pipeline from TIA project to SQLite involves two MCP servers today:

1. **mcp-engineering** (net48, attaches to TIA Portal):
   - `check_environment` — verify TIA/Openness availability
   - `list_sessions` / `connect` — attach to a running TIA Portal session (or open headless)
   - `export_all_blocks` (or `export_block` per block) — writes the XML export folder + `metadata.json` manifest
   - Also `export_tag_tables` / `export_udts` if tags/UDTs are wanted (per plan stage 4 — engineering-side export landed "in parallel"; the scripts e2e-export-tags-udts.json exist, so those tools exist)
   - `disconnect` / `save_project` as needed

2. **mcp-knowledge** (net8):
   - `ingest_source(exportRoot, dbPath?)` — crawls the export folder (manifest-first), rebuilds the SQLite DB (delete-all + bulk insert). This is THE tool that generates all sqlite data.

So the answer to "which mcp tools is called to generate all sqlite data": `ingest_source` on mcp-knowledge — but it needs an export folder filled first by mcp-engineering's `export_all_blocks` (+ `export_tag_tables`/`export_udts`).

Feasibility for UI button "Read Project Context": yes, feasible now. The UI (WPF App, net8) would spawn both MCP servers as stdio processes and call in sequence:
1. `list_sessions` → pick the open TIA session (or connect)
2. `export_all_blocks(outputDir)` (+ tags/udts)
3. `ingest_source(exportRoot)` → returns counts/warnings to display

Caveats to mention honestly:
- mcp-engineering is net48 and needs the Siemens Openness environment (user in "Siemens TIA Openness" group, TIA running or headless start).
- Currently mcp-engineering sessions: does it support attach mode? Yes — Phase 1 verified "headless + attached" E2E. So the UI can either attach to a running TIA or start one.
- The UI itself doesn't exist yet (App is Phase 2 step 7 / later); the agent host doesn't exist yet either (step 6). What's proven today is the MCP chain via stdio — the e2e scripts drive exactly this flow.
- Export is a full rebuild (rebuild-only ingest), fine for a button.
- Time: export of a big project takes a while (the PEI project has hundreds of blocks) — UI should be async with progress.

One nuance: there's no single "one-call" tool that does export+ingest in one — the two-step is by design (servers are independent per the architecture). If the user wants a single button press, the UI/agent orchestrates the chain. That's exactly the Phase 2 design (the comment workflow will do the same).

Keep the answer concise: name the tools, show the call chain, confirm feasibility, note the caveats. Maybe show a small sequence. No need for tools — pure explanation. Actually, let me double check the exact engineering tool names for tags/udts export — the agent.md inventory says: `check_environment`, `list_sessions`, `connect`, `disconnect`, `save_project`, `get_project_info`, `list_blocks`, `export_block`, `export_all_blocks`, `import_block`, `compile_block`, `compile_plc`. It doesn't mention export_tag_tables/export_udts in agent.md, but scripts/e2e-export-tags-udts.json exists and the plan said engineering-side export lands in parallel. Let me verify quickly with a grep for tool names in Mcp.Engineering to be accurate.

</details>

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

</details>

Yes, feasible — that's exactly the chain the architecture was designed for. It takes **two MCP servers in sequence**, because "generate the SQLite data" is one step (`ingest_source`) that consumes the output of another (the TIA export):

**Step A — mcp-engineering** (net48, talks to TIA Portal):
1. `check_environment` — TIA/Openness available
2. `list_sessions` → `connect` — attach to the running TIA session (or start headless)
3. `export_all_blocks` (+ `export_tag_tables`, `export_udts`) — writes the XML export folder with the `metadata.json` manifest
4. `disconnect`

**Step B — mcp-knowledge** (net8):
5. `ingest_source(exportRoot, dbPath?)` — this is **the** tool that generates all the SQLite data: manifest-first parse, full rebuild of `plc-knowledge.db` (blocks, networks with `logicStatements`, calls, reads/writes, DB members, UDTs, tags). Returns counts + warnings you can show in the UI.

So a "Read Project Context" button = spawn/call both servers over stdio and run steps A→B, then surface the `IngestResult` summary (nodes/edges by kind, warnings, duration). An AI agent does the same thing — the plan's comment workflow already assumes this chain, and it's exactly what the E2E scripts (`scripts/e2e-full.json`, `e2e-knowledge.json`) drive today, headless and attached.

Practical caveats for the UI button:
- **Long-running**: a full export of a project like PEI_SinoARP takes a while — run async with progress, not on the UI thread.
- **Environment**: the machine needs TIA Portal + the user in the "Siemens TIA Openness" group; `check_environment` gives you the actionable error to display.
- **Full rebuild**: ingest is delete-all + bulk insert (incremental re-ingest is a Phase 3 item), so treat the button as "refresh whole context".
- What doesn't exist yet is the *caller* side: the WPF `App` shell and the DeepSeek agent host are later Phase 2 steps (7 and 6). The MCP chain itself is proven and ready to be driven by either.

One design note: there's deliberately no single "do everything" tool — each server stays independently callable (per the MCP-per-domain rule). The orchestration (export → ingest → later: read `get_block`/`get_network` for comments) belongs to the UI or the agent, whichever triggers it.

1	# PLC AI Assistant — Phased Build Plan (MCP architecture)
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows desktop app that assists PLC programming work, initially Siemens TIA Portal V17:
6	
7	1. Read/write engineering projects via **TIA Openness** (V17 first, other versions later)
8	2. Translate program source (blocks/networks/tags) into a **SQLite** database
9	3. **DeepSeek cloud API** for program understanding, suggestions, and source modification
10	4. Test modified logic against **PLCSIM Advanced** (installed)
11	5. Version control for program source files (git)
12	6. One WPF UI combining all of the above
13	
14	**MVP (Phase 1):** `mcp-engineering` complete — all TIA Openness operations (connect/disconnect, export/import, compile) accessible as MCP tools, verified standalone via MCP Inspector, ready for downstream consumers.
15	
16	**Architecture direction (user-approved):** the app is decomposed into **independent MCP servers**, one per capability domain, so an agent (and any MCP-compatible client) can call each with minimal friction. Each MCP hosts **pluggable platform adapters** (TIA V17 today; Rockwell ControlLogix / other TIA versions later) behind a shared contract.
17	
18	## Confirmed decisions
19	
20	- **Language:** C# everywhere. WPF for the UI shell. DeepSeek cloud API (`https://api.deepseek.com`, OpenAI-compatible).
21	- **MCP SDK:** official C# SDK (`ModelContextProtocol` NuGet), **stdio transport** — the UI spawns each MCP server as a child process; servers are also usable standalone from any MCP client (Inspector, Claude Code, etc.).
22	- **Framework split (hard constraint):** Siemens API assemblies require .NET Framework 4.8 → `mcp-engineering` and `mcp-simulation` target **net48**; all other components target **net8** (better SDK/async support). `Contracts` is netstandard2.0 so both sides share it.
23	- **Location:** `C:\Users\Ansel\orca\projects\AgentAssistPlcDev` (project folder already created by the user; this plan is saved there as `buildnote/plan/initialLaunch_20260717.md`).
24	- **Environment:** TIA Portal V17 + PLCSIM Advanced already installed.
25	
26	## Solution layout
27	
28	```
29	C:\Users\Ansel\orca\projects\AgentAssistPlcDev\
30	├── AgentAssistPlcDev.sln
31	├── src/
32	│   ├── Contracts/           netstandard2.0 — shared DTOs (BlockInfo, NetworkModel,
33	│   │                              SourceDocument, TagValue) + platform capability interfaces
34	│   │                              (IEngineeringPlatform, ISimulationPlatform)
35	│   ├── Mcp.Engineering/     net48 — MCP server: engineering software interface
36	│   │                              Adapter: TiaV17Adapter (Openness). Future: TiaV18+, RockwellL5x
37	│   ├── Mcp.Knowledge/       net8  — MCP server: SQLite knowledge graph built
38	│   │                              from exported XML (Microsoft.Data.Sqlite)
39	│   ├── Mcp.SourceEditor/    net8  — MCP server: source parse/edit/generate/diff/validate
40	│   │                              (TIA block XML first; L5X + SCL/ST generation later)
41	│   ├── Mcp.Simulation/      net48 — MCP server: simulation (Phase 5)
42	│   │                              Adapter: PlcSimAdvancedAdapter
43	│   ├── Mcp.VersionControl/  net8  — MCP server: git for source folders (LibGit2Sharp)
44	│   ├── Agent/               net8  — DeepSeek client + MCP client host + tool-routing
45	│   │                              agent loop + prompt templates + token/run logging
46	│   └── App/                 net8-windows WPF — UI shell (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm);
47	│                                  spawns/attaches MCP servers, hosts chat + review panes
48	└── tests/                         unit tests + sample TIA V17 XML fixtures
49	```
50	
51	## MCP server tool surfaces (full planned surface; ships per phase)
52	
53	Naming convention: tools use a plain verb_noun pattern (no per-server prefix). Each tool annotated (`readOnlyHint`/`destructiveHint`), structured JSON output, actionable error messages. Each server tested standalone with MCP Inspector before UI integration.
54	
55	- **mcp-engineering** (Phase 1): `check_environment`, `list_sessions`, `connect`, `get_project_info`, `list_blocks`, `export_block`, `export_all_blocks`, `import_block` (destructive), `compile_block`, `compile_plc`, `save_project`, `disconnect`
56	- **mcp-knowledge** (Phase 2, split — design: `buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md`): step 1 ships `ingest_source`, `query` (read-only SQL), `get_schema`; knowledge-depth step (done 2026-07-18) adds `get_block`, `get_network`, `search` + `logicStatements` network text
57	- **mcp-source-editor** (Phase 2): `src_parse_block`, `src_insert_network_comment`, `src_diff`, `src_validate` (well-formedness + TIA schema sanity checks)
58	- **mcp-version-control** (Phase 2): `vc_init`, `vc_snapshot`, `vc_log`, `vc_diff`, `vc_restore` (destructive)
59	- **mcp-simulation** (Phase 5): `sim_list_instances`, `sim_create_instance`, `sim_power`, `sim_set_state`, `sim_read_tag`, `sim_write_tag`, `sim_run_assertion`
60	
61	## Prerequisites (validated in Phase 0)
62	
63	- Windows user is member of **"Siemens TIA Openness"** group; Openness DLLs at `C:\Program Files\Siemens\Automation\Portal V17\PublicAPI\V17\`
64	- PLCSIM Advanced runtime API present (path confirmed in Phase 5)
65	- DeepSeek API key (stored in `%APPDATA%/PlcAiAssistant/config.json`, git-ignored)
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Phase 0 — Scaffold & two de-risking spikes (DONE 2026-07-17)
70	
71	- Create solution/projects as above; git init with `.gitignore` (config, `*.db`, exported source working dirs)
72	- **Spike A (MCP+net48):** minimal `mcp-engineering` skeleton — C# MCP SDK server on net48, referencing `Siemens.Engineering.dll`, answering one stdio tool call (`list_sessions`) from MCP Inspector. Proves the riskiest combination before anything is built on it.
73	- **Spike B (XML round-trip):** manually export one FB via Openness, hand-edit a network `<Comment>` node, re-import, confirm in TIA editor the comment appears and logic is unchanged. Locks the exact V17 XML schema (multilingual comment nodes) that `mcp-source-editor` targets.
74	- Env-check tool shared by UI and engineering MCP: user group, DLL paths, attach to running TIA process
75	- **Exit criteria:** both spikes pass on this machine; solution builds; Inspector can call the skeleton server
76	
77	## Phase 1 — mcp-engineering complete (DONE 2026-07-18)
78	
79	Deliver a fully functional TIA V17 engineering adapter behind the MCP protocol, verified end-to-end with MCP Inspector. This is the foundation everything else builds on — all downstream MCP servers, the agent, and the UI consume engineering as a dependency.
80	
81	1. **TiaV17Adapter — full implementation:**
82	   - Connect modes: attach to running TIA instance (with UI), open project headless (`TiaPortalMode.WithoutUI`), open with visible UI
83	   - Enumerate PLC devices and block groups (OB/FB/FC/DB)
84	   - Export single block / all blocks / named selection to XML in a per-project working folder
85	   - Import modified XML with safety guards (block-not-open check, re-export interface verify)
86	   - Compile single block or full PLC software, returning structured error messages per block
87	   - Guard: refuse import into a block open-with-changes in the TIA editor
88	
89	2. **Transport & lifetime:**
90	   - Stdio MCP server (child process model)
91	   - Graceful shutdown — release COM, dispose portal process on exit
92	   - Multiple concurrent server instances allowed (one per client)
93	
94	3. **Diagnostics:**
95	   - `check_environment` — user group, DLL paths, TIA version, Openness registration
96	   - `get_project_info` — project name, PLC devices, block count, last modified
97	
98	4. **Standalone validation (MCP Inspector):**
99	   - Full cycle: attach/open → list blocks → export → import → compile → disconnect
100	   - XML round-trip verify: export → modify comment → import → re-export → confirm logic byte-identical
101	   - Both headless and attached modes tested
102	
103	- **Exit criteria:** all `mcp-engineering` tools pass end-to-end in MCP Inspector on a real test project; import safety guards reject invalid inputs with actionable messages; compile reports structured errors per block; server exits cleanly with no COM leaks.
104	
105	**Phase 1 tool surface:**
106	
107	- **mcp-engineering** (TIA V17 adapter): `check_environment`, `list_sessions`, `connect`, `get_project_info`, `list_blocks`, `export_block`, `export_all_blocks`, `import_block` (destructive), `compile_block`, `compile_plc`, `save_project`, `disconnect`
108	
109	(Other MCP servers — knowledge, source-editor, version-control, simulation — are scoped to their respective phases below.)
110	
111	## Phase 2 — AI network comments over the MCP chain (split into steps, 2026-07-18)
112	
113	Reviewed as too aggressive for one phase — split into small, independently verifiable steps. Assumes Phase 1 mcp-engineering is complete and usable.
114	
115	1. **mcp-knowledge — ingest** (renamed from `mcp-knowledge-store`; detailed design: `buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md`): crawl the export folder filled by mcp-engineering, build the SQLite knowledge graph (4-table graph schema adopted from the PlcSourceExporter reference — supersedes the earlier relational `projects`/`blocks`/`networks`/`tags` sketch). Tools: `ingest_source`, `query` (read-only SQL), `get_schema`.
116	2. **Engineering export of tag tables & UDTs** + knowledge import of them.
117	3. **mcp-knowledge depth:** network logic text (`logicStatements`, SCL-like translation) + query helpers `get_block`, `get_network`, `search`.
118	4. **mcp-source-editor (MVP):** parse block XML into Block/Network model; insert/replace only the comment node (round-trip safe); `src_validate` before any import.
119	5. **mcp-version-control (MVP):** `vc_snapshot` of the working folder before every write-back.
120	6. **Agent:** DeepSeek client + MCP client host; comment-generation workflow: pull block context via knowledge queries (interface tags, network instructions, neighboring titles) → DeepSeek returns strict JSON `{network_number, comment}` per network → map to `src_insert_network_comment` edits. `llm_runs` audit table (full prompt/response) lands here.
121	7. **WPF UI (MVP flow):** Connect to TIA → block tree → select blocks → "Generate comments" → review grid (network #, generated comment, editable, accept/reject) → "Apply" (triggers `vc_snapshot` → XML edit → `src_validate` → `import_block` → re-export verify) → result log. Chat panel can be minimal/hidden in MVP. Dry-run mode: produce the commented XML + diff on disk without importing.
122	- **Exit criteria (unchanged):** on a real test project, generated comments are applied to an FB/FC and visible in TIA V17 editors, block logic byte-identical, pre-write snapshot exists in git, and every LLM call is auditable in `llm_runs`
123	
124	## Phase 3 — Program understanding & Q&A
125	
126	- Knowledge store deepened: instruction-level parsing (STL/LAD/FBD), PLC tag tables, data types, block-call cross-reference graph; incremental re-ingest on export change
127	- Agent Q&A workflow: natural-language question → SQL/cross-reference retrieval → grounded answer; suggestion mode per selected block/network
128	- UI: chat panel + cross-reference browser
129	- **Exit criteria:** chat answers verifiable against the DB; cross-reference queries correct on the test project
130	
131	## Phase 4 — AI-assisted source modification & generation
132	
133	- `src_generate_block` (new network/block from instruction, SCL/ST and LAD/FBD where expressible in XML), `src_replace_network`
134	- Agent modification workflow with mandatory `src_diff` preview in UI → approve → snapshot → validate → import → verify compiles in TIA
135	- Rollback via `vc_restore`; keep N previous XML versions per block
136	- **Exit criteria:** an AI-generated modification round-trips and compiles in TIA without errors
137	
138	## Phase 5 — Simulation (PLCSIM Advanced)
139	
140	- mcp-simulation with PlcSimAdvancedAdapter (`Siemens.Simatic.Simulation.Runtime.*`): instance lifecycle, power/RUN/STOP, tag I/O (I/Q/M/DB), cycle control
141	- Spike first: download path for a modified program to the virtual PLC from V17 (TIA download vs API) — version-specific, validated before building the runner
142	- Test runner: set inputs → run N cycles → assert outputs; agent proposes test cases for modified blocks
143	- **Exit criteria:** Phase-4-modified block runs in PLCSIM Advanced; scripted assertion passes from inside the app
144	
145	## Phase 6 — Version control depth
146	
147	- History/timeline UI, commit messages auto-generated from change summaries, in-app diffs between any two snapshots, branch per experiment
148	- **Exit criteria:** full iterated history of a block browseable/diffable in-app
149	
150	## Phase 7 — Platform expansion & hardening
151	
152	- Adapter contracts hardened from V17 experience; **Rockwell ControlLogix** support: engineering adapter via **L5X XML import/export** (no free Openness-equivalent — Studio 5000 exchange is file-based), source-editor L5X parser, simulation adapter evaluated (FactoryTalk Logix Echo/Emulate — flagged as research task); TIA V18+ adapter
153	- Packaging: installer + first-run wizard (env check, API key, MCP server health); DeepSeek rate-limit/backoff, token cost display, cancellation
154	- Optional: MCP eval suites per server (10-question read-only evals per mcp-builder guidance)
155	
156	## Build order discipline
157	
158	Phase 0 spikes first — nothing proceeds until both pass. Each phase ends with a working app meeting its exit criteria on a real test project.
159	
160	## Out of scope (for now)
161	
162	- HMI/WinCC objects, Safety (F) blocks, multi-user TIA projects, non-Siemens/Rockwell platforms, remote/networked MCP deployment (stdio-local only)

</details>

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

The user asks: "let's see how we proceed to build, propose some idea based on previous planning md."

They're asking for a proposal on how to proceed — given the context of the previous conversation (UI button "Read Project Context", AI agent callable). The remaining Phase 2 steps are:

4. mcp-source-editor (MVP): parse/edit/validate block XML
5. mcp-version-control (MVP): vc_snapshot
6. Agent: DeepSeek client + MCP host + comment workflow
7. WPF UI (MVP flow): Connect → block tree → select → generate → review → apply

The user's "Read Project Context" button idea is a UI feature. Interesting tension: the plan says source-editor (4) and version-control (5) come next, then agent (6), then UI (7). But the user's immediate interest seems to be the UI entry point (Read Project Context button) — which actually doesn't need steps 4-6! The export→ingest chain is read-only. A UI that just reads project context (button → export_all_blocks + ingest → show summary/block tree) can be built TODAY against the two finished MCP servers.

So the proposal should offer a sensible sequencing with options:

**Option A (plan-strict):** continue step 4 (source-editor) → 5 (version-control) → 6 (agent) → 7 (UI). Rationale: each step builds on the previous; UI arrives when the full comment workflow is ready. Downside: no visible UI for a long time; the user's button waits.

**Option B (UI-first read-only shell, recommended):** build a thin WPF MVP now that hosts the read-only chain: Connect to TIA (via engineering tools) → "Read Project Context" button → progress → ingest summary → block tree from the knowledge DB (get_block/get_network/search wired to panes). This gives:
- immediate user value + a real harness to dogfood the MCP chain
- the MCP client host infrastructure (spawn servers, call tools, async progress) that step 7 needs anyway
- the block tree + selection that the comment workflow (step 7) reuses
Then continue with 4 (source-editor), 5 (version-control), 6 (agent) — and the UI grows the "Generate comments → review → apply" flow on top.

**Option C (agent-first):** build the Agent (DeepSeek host) next, drive the comment workflow headless/CLI first, UI last. Downside: user asked for UI entry.

Key architectural pieces needed for the UI:
- MCP client host: the ModelContextProtocol NuGet client side (net8) — spawn Mcp.Engineering.exe (net48) and Mcp.Knowledge.exe as child processes, call tools, parse results. There's also a consideration: the Agent project (step 6) will also need an MCP client host — so building a small shared "McpClientHost" now (in App or a shared lib) pays off twice. But careful about framework: App is net8-windows, Agent is net8 — a shared net8 library for MCP client hosting could live in... the plan says Contracts is netstandard2.0 shared DTOs; an McpClientHost helper could be part of App or its own small project. Keep simple: put it in App for now; Agent step can extract/share later. Or reference the same MCP client factory code.
- Actually — should the UI talk to the DB directly instead of MCP? No — the architecture rule is all through MCP servers; the UI spawns them. Keep that.

Proposed concrete increments for Option B:

**UI-0 (shell + MCP host):** WPF app skeleton (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm per plan), MCP client host that starts/stops the two servers, health check (check_environment), server stderr → log pane. Exit: app starts servers, shows env check result.

**UI-1 (Read Project Context):** session picker (list_sessions → attach) → output folder convention (%LOCALAPPDATA%/PlcAiAssistant/exports/<project> or per-project working folder — check what engineering uses) → export_all_blocks + export_tag_tables + export_udts with progress → ingest_source → summary view (counts by kind, warnings) + dbPath recorded. Exit: button produces DB and summary on a real project.

**UI-2 (context browser):** block list/tree (query: blocks by kind) → select block → get_block → networks with logicStatements; search box → search tool → jump to block/network. Exit: browse full context from DB.

Then back to plan steps 4→5→6, and UI-3 adds "Generate comments" review/apply.

Also mention: the "AI agent can call this function followed by user prompt" — in step 6 the Agent gets the same MCP client host + DeepSeek; the user prompt "read project context" maps to the same chain. The UI button and the AI agent are two triggers of one orchestration — so implement the orchestration once (a small "Workflows" layer: ReadProjectContextWorkflow) callable from button and agent. That's a clean idea aligned with their question.

Where does the workflow layer live? If both App and Agent need it: Agent project is "DeepSeek client + MCP host + tool-routing agent loop". The UI spawns MCP servers... Actually per plan, App "spawns/attaches MCP servers, hosts chat + review panes" and Agent is the DeepSeek + MCP client host. Hmm — plan line 44-47: Agent = DeepSeek client + MCP client host + tool-routing agent loop + prompt templates + token/run logging; App = WPF UI shell that spawns/attaches MCP servers, hosts chat + review panes.

So the MCP client host could live in Agent (net8, no DeepSeek coupling if structured well) and App references Agent? Or App has its own minimal client. To keep it stupidly simple: build the MCP client host inside App now (net8-windows can use the ModelContextProtocol client package); when Agent arrives, decide: either Agent references the same host code (extract to a small shared project like src/McpClient/ or reuse via Contracts? Contracts is netstandard2.0 — the MCP client package targets net8/netstandard? ModelContextProtocol client supports netstandard2.0? The SDK supports netstandard2.0 I believe, but let's not overpromise). Simplest: duplicate tiny amount or extract later. Note it as a decision point.

The AskUserQuestion tool could be used to structure the choice. Given they said "propose some idea", I'll write the proposal and use AskUserQuestion at the end for the direction choice. Actually — better to just present and let them react freely. Hmm, the tool guidance: use AskUserQuestion when user input genuinely changes the next action. Here it does: which step to build next. My proposal: split step 7 into UI-0..UI-3 and pull the read-only part forward, because it needs nothing from steps 4-6.

Also worth noting: what "Read Project Context" needs from engineering is already E2E-verified (attached + headless). And ingest of PEI-scale project: filesFound 7 for the demo; real project has hundreds of blocks — export_all_blocks duration could be minutes; progress reporting via logs; the MCP stdio has no progress notifications in current servers (engineering has ReportsProgress tests in reference... actually mcp-engineering may log progress to stderr — UI can stream stderr lines to a log pane. Good enough for MVP).

One more consideration: output/export folder convention. mcp-engineering export_all_blocks(outputDir) — UI should standardize per-project folder like `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\<projectName>` (or next to the project). Ingest default dbPath = <exportRoot>/plc-knowledge.db. Fine.

Also the "one workflow, two triggers" idea directly answers their previous question.

Structure of my answer:
1. Where we stand (done: steps 1-3 of Phase 2; remaining: 4 source-editor, 5 version-control, 6 agent, 7 UI).
2. Key observation: the "Read Project Context" chain is read-only → UI can start NOW without steps 4-6; and the MCP client host the UI needs is also what the Agent (step 6) needs.
3. Proposed sequencing: UI-0/1/2 increments (read-only shell → Read Project Context → context browser), then 4→5→6 per plan, then UI-3 (generate/review/apply) completes step 7.
4. The workflow-layer idea: ReadProjectContextWorkflow called by both button and agent.
5. Alternatives: strict plan order (4→5→6→7) or agent-first; trade-offs.
6. Ask which direction.

Keep it reasonably concise. Use the user's language (English). Then AskUserQuestion with 3 options: UI-first read-only shell (recommended), strict plan order (source-editor next), agent-first.

Based on the plan docs, here's where Phase 2 stands and the honest options for what's next.

**Done:** steps 1–3 (knowledge ingest + tags/UDTs + knowledge depth). **Remaining:** step 4 `mcp-source-editor`, step 5 `mcp-version-control`, step 6 Agent (DeepSeek), step 7 WPF UI.

**Key observation from your "Read Project Context" idea:** that chain (`list_sessions` → `connect` → `export_all_blocks`/`export_tag_tables`/`export_udts` → `ingest_source`) is **entirely read-only**. It needs nothing from steps 4–6 (no XML editing, no snapshots, no LLM). So a UI that reads and browses project context can start *today* against the two finished servers — and the thing the UI must build first (an MCP client host that spawns servers, calls tools, streams progress) is exactly what the Agent in step 6 also needs. Building the UI shell now buys infrastructure step 6/7 reuse, and gives you a real harness to dogfood the chain on PEI_SinoARP instead of only E2E scripts.

**My recommended sequencing:**

- **UI-0 — shell + MCP client host.** WPF skeleton (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm per plan), spawns `Mcp.Engineering.exe` + `Mcp.Knowledge.exe` as child processes, `check_environment` panel, server stderr → log pane. Exit: app starts both servers and shows env health.
- **UI-1 — "Read Project Context" button.** Session picker (`list_sessions` → attach, or headless) → export with progress → `ingest_source` → summary view (nodes/edges by kind, warnings, duration) + remembered `dbPath`. Export folder convention under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\<project>\`.
- **UI-2 — context browser.** Block tree (via `query`), block → `get_block` → networks with `logicStatements`; a search box wired to `search`. This is where your polished translation text pays off visibly.
- Then back to plan order: **step 4** source-editor → **step 5** version-control → **step 6** Agent — and **UI-3** grows "Generate comments → review grid → Apply" on the same shell (that's the plan's step-7 MVP flow, unchanged).

One design rule I'd bake in from UI-1: implement the chain **once as a workflow** (`ReadProjectContextWorkflow` — orchestrates the 5 calls, reports progress, returns the ingest summary), and let both the button *and* the later AI agent trigger it. Two triggers, one orchestration — that's the direct answer to your earlier question about agent-callability.

**Alternatives:**
- *Strict plan order* (4 → 5 → 6 → 7): cleanest dependency-wise, but you get no usable UI until everything else is done, and the comment workflow gets dogfooded much later.
- *Agent-first* (step 6 next): proves the DeepSeek comment loop headless before any UI, but skips your stated UI entry point and delays the shell the review/apply flow needs anyway.

2	using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting;
3	using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;
4	
5	var builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder(args);
6	
7	// MCP stdio transport: stdout is the JSON-RPC channel — all logging must go to stderr.
8	builder.Logging.AddConsole(options => options.LogToStandardErrorThreshold = LogLevel.Trace);
9	
10	builder.Services

12	    .WithStdioServerTransport()

1	using System.ComponentModel;
2	using Contracts;
3	using Contracts.Engineering;
4	using Mcp.Engineering.Adapter;
5	using Mcp.Engineering.Openness;
6	using ModelContextProtocol.Protocol;
7	using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
8	
9	namespace Mcp.Engineering.Tools;
10	
11	/// <summary>
12	/// MCP tool surface (mcp-engineering.md §3). Thin wrappers over <see cref="IEngineeringPlatform"/>
13	/// (registered as <see cref="StaAdapter"/> so all Openness calls run on one STA thread);
14	/// all failures become structured isError results via <see cref="ToolJson"/>.
15	/// </summary>
16	[McpServerToolType]
17	public sealed class EngineeringTools
18	{
19	    private readonly IEngineeringPlatform _adapter;
20	
21	    public EngineeringTools(IEngineeringPlatform adapter) => _adapter = adapter;
22	
23	    [McpServerTool(Name = "check_environment")]
24	    [Description("Validate TIA Openness installation, DLL paths, and user group membership (read-only).")]
25	    public CallToolResult CheckEnvironment() => Invoke(() => _adapter.CheckEnvironment());
26	
27	    [McpServerTool(Name = "list_sessions")]
28	    [Description("Enumerate running TIA Portal processes that can be attached to (read-only).")]
29	    public CallToolResult ListSessions() => Invoke(() => _adapter.ListSessions());
30	
31	    [McpServerTool(Name = "connect")]
32	    [Description("Attach to a running TIA session (sessionId) or open a project (projectPath; headless unless withUI=true). Provide exactly one.")]
33	    public CallToolResult Connect(
34	        [Description("TIA process id from list_sessions → attach mode.")] int? sessionId = null,
35	        [Description("Path to the .ap17 project file → open mode.")] string? projectPath = null,
36	        [Description("Open mode only: show the TIA UI. Default false (headless).")] bool withUI = false,
37	        [Description("Max seconds to wait for Openness startup. Default 60.")] int timeoutSeconds = 60)
38	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.Connect(new ConnectOptions
39	        {
40	            SessionId = sessionId,
41	            ProjectPath = projectPath,
42	            WithUI = withUI,
43	            TimeoutSeconds = timeoutSeconds,
44	        }));
45	
46	    [McpServerTool(Name = "disconnect")]
47	    [Description("Release project and portal handles. Never saves; never closes a project owned by an attached session. Reports unsaved changes.")]
48	    public CallToolResult Disconnect() => Invoke(() => _adapter.Disconnect());
49	
50	    [McpServerTool(Name = "save_project")]
51	    [Description("Explicitly save the open TIA project — the only tool that persists it.")]
52	    public CallToolResult SaveProject() => Invoke(() => { _adapter.SaveProject(); return new { }; });
53	
54	    [McpServerTool(Name = "get_project_info")]
55	    [Description("Project name, path, PLC devices, block count, last modified (read-only).")]
56	    public CallToolResult GetProjectInfo() => Invoke(() => _adapter.GetProjectInfo());
57	
58	    [McpServerTool(Name = "list_blocks")]
59	    [Description("Enumerate all blocks (OB/FB/FC/DB) incl. nested block groups (read-only).")]
60	    public CallToolResult ListBlocks(
61	        [Description("PLC device name; optional for single-PLC projects.")] string? plcName = null)
62	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.ListBlocks(plcName));
63	
64	    [McpServerTool(Name = "export_block")]
65	    [Description("Export a single block to XML under outputDir/Blocks|DB and upsert its record in outputDir/metadata.json (read-only w.r.t. the project).")]
66	    public CallToolResult ExportBlock(
67	        [Description("Block name as listed by list_blocks.")] string blockName,
68	        [Description("Export root directory.")] string outputDir)
69	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.ExportBlock(blockName, outputDir));
70	
71	    [McpServerTool(Name = "export_all_blocks")]
72	    [Description("Export every PLC block to XML under outputDir (Blocks/ and DB/ subfolders; per-PLC subfolder when multiple PLCs) and write a metadata.json manifest per export root.")]
73	    public CallToolResult ExportAllBlocks(
74	        [Description("Export root directory for the XML files.")] string outputDir)
75	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.ExportAllBlocks(outputDir));
76	
77	    [McpServerTool(Name = "export_tag_tables")]
78	    [Description("Export every PLC tag table to XML under outputDir/Tags (recursing nested groups) and upsert one metadata.json record per table. Per-PLC subfolder when the project has multiple PLCs, unless plcName is given.")]
79	    public CallToolResult ExportTagTables(
80	        [Description("Export root directory.")] string outputDir,
81	        [Description("PLC device name; optional for single-PLC projects.")] string? plcName = null)
82	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.ExportTagTables(outputDir, plcName));
83	
84	    [McpServerTool(Name = "export_udts")]
85	    [Description("Export every PLC data type (UDT) to XML under outputDir/UDT (recursing nested groups) and upsert one metadata.json record per type. Per-PLC subfolder when the project has multiple PLCs, unless plcName is given.")]
86	    public CallToolResult ExportUdts(
87	        [Description("Export root directory.")] string outputDir,
88	        [Description("PLC device name; optional for single-PLC projects.")] string? plcName = null)
89	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.ExportUdts(outputDir, plcName));
90	
91	    [McpServerTool(Name = "import_block")]
92	    [Description("Import a modified block XML back into the project (DESTRUCTIVE: overwrites the block). Caller must validate the XML and snapshot the working folder first.")]
93	    public CallToolResult ImportBlock(
94	        [Description("Block name to overwrite.")] string blockName,
95	        [Description("Path to the modified XML file.")] string xmlFilePath)
96	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.ImportBlock(blockName, xmlFilePath));
97	
98	    [McpServerTool(Name = "compile_block")]
99	    [Description("Compile the PLC software and report messages for the named block (write: mutates project compile state). V17 has no per-block compile — this is compile_plc + per-block filtering.")]
100	    public CallToolResult CompileBlock(
101	        [Description("Block name to report on.")] string blockName)
102	        => Invoke(() => _adapter.CompileBlock(blockName));
103	
104	    [McpServerTool(Name = "compile_plc")]
105	    [Description("Compile the whole PLC software, returning all messages (write: mutates project compile state).")]
106	    public CallToolResult CompilePlc() => Invoke(() => _adapter.CompilePlc());
107	
108	    private CallToolResult Invoke(Func<object> action)
109	    {
110	        try
111	        {
112	            return ToolJson.Ok(action());
113	        }
114	        catch (AdapterException ae)
115	        {
116	            return ToolJson.Fail(ae.Code, ae.Message, ae.Remediation);
117	        }
118	        catch (Exception ex)
119	        {
120	            return ToolJson.Fail(OpennessErrorMapper.CodeFor(ex), ex.Message);

</details>

If not on the feed... 8.4.x definitely exists on nuget.org. I'll note "8.4.x (latest stable at restore time)".

Mcp.Engineering exe: bin/Debug/net48/Mcp.Engineering.exe (exists). Mcp.Knowledge exe: bin/Debug/net8.0/Mcp.Knowledge.exe.

Solution: uses solution folders "src" and "tests" — App goes under "src", App.Tests under "tests".

Now the design decisions to lock:

**A. Project structure**
- `src/App/App.csproj` — net8-windows, UseWPF=true, nullable+implicit usings like other net8 projects. Packages: `ModelContextProtocol.Core` 1.4.1 (client only — the full `ModelContextProtocol` package is server-oriented), `CommunityToolkit.Mvvm` 8.4.x.
- Namespace: `App` (per "no useless prefixes" rule).
- Tests: `tests/App.Tests/App.Tests.csproj` — net8, xUnit pins matching existing test projects (check Mcp.Knowledge.Tests.csproj pins for xunit versions).

**B. MCP client host (inside App)**
- `Mcp/McpServerProcess.cs` — wraps one server: starts `McpClient.CreateAsync(new StdioClientTransport(new StdioClientTransportOptions { Command = exePath, Name = "engineering" }))`; stderr lines (StandardErrorLines callback) → App log; exposes `CallToolAsync<T>(name, args)` helper that deserializes the first TextContentBlock JSON into T or throws a ToolCallException with code/message/remediation.
- `Mcp/McpHost.cs` — owns engineering + knowledge clients, start/stop, IAsyncDisposable. Server exe paths: `AppSettings` (see D).
- Test seam: the tool-call helper is behind an interface `IMcpToolCaller { Task<T> CallAsync<T>(string tool, object args); }` so workflows are unit-testable with fakes (no MCP needed in tests).

**C. Workflow layer (the "two triggers, one orchestration" idea)**
- `Workflows/ReadProjectContextWorkflow.cs` — pure orchestration over IMcpToolCaller:
  1. `get_project_info` (verify connected) → project name
  2. resolve exportRoot = `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\<projectName>\`
  3. `export_all_blocks` → counts; `export_tag_tables`; `export_udts` (progress callbacks between steps)
  4. `ingest_source(exportRoot)` → IngestResult
  5. returns `ReadProjectContextResult { projectName, exportRoot, dbPath, exported counts, ingest summary, warnings }`
- Progress via `IProgress<string>` (or a small event) → UI log pane.
- This same class is later callable from the Agent (step 6) — note the extraction point (could move to a shared net8 lib or the Agent references App? No — when Agent lands, move Workflows + Mcp host into a small shared project; flagged as a decision, keep in App now per KISS).

Hmm wait — actually, should the workflow layer live in App at all, or should I create `src/Agent/` now (the plan's Agent project is "DeepSeek client + MCP host + tool routing") and put the MCP host + workflows there without DeepSeek? That's arguably cleaner for the "agent calls it too" requirement... but it creates a project whose DeepSeek part doesn't exist yet. The plan positions Agent as step 6. KISS: put host+workflows in App now; extraction documented. I'll go with App-internal + documented extraction.

**D. AppSettings**
- `%APPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\config.json` already exists for DeepSeek (git-ignored). App reads server exe paths from it (optional keys `engineeringServerPath`, `knowledgeServerPath`); defaults: dev-layout convention — `..\..\..\..\Mcp.Engineering\bin\Debug\net48\Mcp.Engineering.exe` resolved relative to AppContext.BaseDirectory... fragile across Debug/Release. Better default: `<solutionDir>` found by walking up from BaseDirectory for `AgentAssistPlcDev.sln`, then `src/<proj>/bin/<Debug|Release>/<tfm>/<proj>.exe` with configuration matching the running build (`#if DEBUG`). Acceptable for MVP dev harness; installer story is Phase 7. Log pane shows the resolved paths on startup; if exe missing → clear error in env panel.

**E. UI slices (this plan covers UI-0 + UI-1; UI-2 gets a design sketch but is its own next step?)**

Hmm — scope decision. The user approved "UI-first read-only" with increments UI-0 (shell+host), UI-1 (Read Project Context), UI-2 (context browser). Should this plan cover UI-0+UI-1 only, or through UI-2? UI-0+UI-1 is a coherent first deliverable with clear exit criteria (button produces DB + summary on a real project). UI-2 (browser) is a natural second plan. I'll plan UI-0+UI-1 for implementation now, with UI-2 sketched as "next". That keeps the implementation reviewable (~small). Actually — since I'm writing the plan doc for the whole "App" step, I'll structure it as: this doc covers UI-0+UI-1 (build order), UI-2 outlined as follow-up. 

**F. UI-0+UI-1 screens**
- Single MainWindow: top = environment/status strip (env check result, server status), left = session list (list_sessions) + connect inputs (sessionId attach OR projectPath open) + Connect/Disconnect buttons, center = "Read Project Context" button + progress log (text box), right/bottom = last ingest summary (nodes/edges by kind grid + warnings list).
- MVVM: MainViewModel with ObservableCollections; RelayCommand from CommunityToolkit.
- Long-running work on background task; cancellation token (Cancel button) — CallToolAsync takes CancellationToken; the server export itself isn't cancellable mid-call but between steps we abort.

**G. Tests (tests/App.Tests)**
- `ReadProjectContextWorkflowTests`: fake IMcpToolCaller scripting the 5 calls; assert call order, args (exportRoot under expected project folder), result mapping, error propagation (tool error → workflow surfaces code/message), cancellation between steps.
- `AppSettingsTests`: path resolution from config override + default convention.
- No WPF UI automation.

**H. Docs**
- New plan doc `buildnote/plan/app.md` (the repo convention — this file I'm writing becomes the basis, but the repo convention is to keep durable design docs in buildnote/plan/). The plan-mode plan file is the session plan; as part of implementation I'll also create buildnote/plan/app.md. Yes — repo convention (mcp-engineering.md, mcp-knowledge.md) expects it.
- agent.md: add App row to inventory + status line + Key Files entry. initialLaunch: annotate step 7 split (UI-0/1 read-only pulled forward).

**I. Build order within this step**
1. Scaffold App project (csproj, App.xaml, MainWindow placeholder) + add to sln; build green.
2. MCP host + IMcpToolCaller + settings; env panel shows check_environment via the real client; manual smoke.
3. ReadProjectContextWorkflow + unit tests with fakes.
4. MainWindow UI (sessions, connect, button, progress, summary); wire workflow.
5. E2E manual: run App, attach to real TIA session (PEI or TestPLCExportDemo project), press button, verify DB + summary. Record in buildnote/log/.
6. Docs: buildnote/plan/app.md (on implementation start), agent.md, initialLaunch annotations.

Also verify how `connect` result looks (ConnectionInfo) for the UI. Quick read of ConnectionInfo.cs. And check EnvCheckResult for the env panel. Let me read those two + the test csproj pins.

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

I have everything I need. One design point to reconsider: should the App reference `Contracts` (netstandard2.0) to reuse the DTOs (SessionInfo, ConnectionInfo, ProjectInfo, EnvCheckResult, ExportResult)? App is net8-windows; Contracts is netstandard2.0 → compatible. Referencing Contracts lets the App deserialize tool results into typed DTOs instead of anonymous/JSON-element handling. That's exactly the "shared DTOs" purpose of Contracts. The knowledge-side results (IngestResult) don't live in Contracts ("No Contracts dependency this step" per mcp-knowledge.md — "Promote to Contracts when a second consumer (UI/agent) needs typed access" — the App IS that second consumer!). So part of this step: add knowledge result DTOs to Contracts? Hmm — the knowledge plan explicitly deferred promoting DTOs to Contracts "when a second consumer needs typed access". The App is now that consumer. Options:
- (a) App references Contracts; add `Contracts/Knowledge/IngestResult.cs` (+ maybe KnowledgeBlock/Network DTOs for UI-2 later) to Contracts. KnowledgeTools returns anonymous objects today — promoting means KnowledgeTools references Contracts and returns the typed DTOs. Small refactor of Mcp.Knowledge + tests untouched (JSON shape must stay identical).
- (b) App defines its own client-side DTOs mirroring the JSON. Duplication, no refactor.

(a) is cleaner and fulfills the plan's own promotion rule. Scope: IngestResult only for UI-1 (dbPath, source, filesFound, filesImported, nodes, edges, byKind, warnings, durationMs). For UI-0/1 we don't need get_block DTOs yet (UI-2). Keep it minimal: promote IngestResult now; note get_block/get_network/search DTOs get promoted in UI-2.

Wait — careful: engineering tools return typed Contracts DTOs already (EngineeringTools Invoke returns _adapter methods which return Contracts types). So App referencing Contracts gets those for free. For knowledge, I'll promote IngestResult → Contracts/Knowledge/IngestResult.cs. Also the error envelope { code, message, remediation } — engineering has a shared ToolJson; App needs an error DTO client-side: small class in App (ToolErrorException with Code/Message/Remediation).

Also CallToolResult parsing: CallToolAsync returns CallToolResult with Content list; parse TextContentBlock → JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>. Note camelCase policy matches servers' ToolJson.

Now — WPF + net8-windows: project file needs `<UseWPF>true</UseWPF>`, `<OutputType>WinExe</OutputType>`. Nullable/implicit usings enabled.

MVVM: CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.4.x — is it restorable? Not in cache; assume nuget.org reachable (previous restores worked... they must have, packages exist in cache from earlier builds). Note risk in plan: if restore fails offline, fallback to hand-rolled ViewModelBase (tiny). I'll note it.

App.xaml / MainWindow: plain WPF, no extra UI libs. Styles minimal.

Structure:
```
src/App/
  App.csproj
  App.xaml / App.xaml.cs
  MainWindow.xaml / .cs
  ViewModels/MainViewModel.cs (+ SessionItemViewModel? keep flat: MainViewModel only)
  Mcp/IMcpToolCaller.cs, McpServerConnection.cs, McpHost.cs, ToolCallException.cs
  Workflows/ReadProjectContextWorkflow.cs, ReadProjectContextResult.cs
  AppSettings.cs
  (Converters if needed)
tests/App.Tests/
  App.Tests.csproj
  ReadProjectContextWorkflowTests.cs
  AppSettingsTests.cs
  FakeToolCaller.cs
```

Workflow details (ReadProjectContextWorkflow.RunAsync):
- input: IMcpToolCaller engineering, IMcpToolCaller knowledge, IProgress<WorkflowLog>? or events, CancellationToken
- steps:
  1. engineering get_project_info → name (fail NOT_CONNECTED if error) — actually connect happens in UI before; workflow requires an already-connected engineering session. Wait — should the workflow include connect? The UI flow: user picks session → Connect button (UI-level, keeps connection state visible). Then "Read Project Context" assumes connected; get_project_info validates. Keep connect OUT of the workflow (connection is UI session state). Document it.
  2. exportRoot = Path.Combine(%LOCALAPPDATA%, "PlcAiAssistant", "exports", projectName) — sanitize invalid chars.
  3. export_all_blocks(exportRoot) → ExportResult[] summary (count, failures)
  4. export_tag_tables(exportRoot), export_udts(exportRoot) — count; tolerate projects without tags/udts? The tools return arrays; fine.
  5. knowledge ingest_source(exportRoot) → IngestResult
  6. return result aggregate.
- Error handling: any tool isError → WorkflowException carrying code/message/remediation; UI shows it in log + status.
- Progress: IProgress<string> log lines ("Exporting blocks…", "245 blocks exported in 38s", …). Timing via Stopwatch per step.

AppSettings (in App): reads %APPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\config.json (System.Text.Json, tolerant of missing file); properties EngineeringServerPath/KnowledgeServerPath (optional); resolution order: config value → solution-layout default (walk up from AppContext.BaseDirectory to find AgentAssistPlcDev.sln; then src/Mcp.Engineering/bin/<Debug|Release>/net48/Mcp.Engineering.exe; src/Mcp.Knowledge/bin/<Debug|Release>/net8.0/Mcp.Knowledge.exe) — pick configuration via #if DEBUG. Unit-testable: pure function from (baseDir, config) → paths.

UI panels (UI-0/1 scope):
- Status strip: env check summary (from check_environment: TIA version, group membership), server states (Engineering/Knowledge: running/stopped), resolved exe paths in tooltip/log.
- Sessions group: List sessions button → DataGrid (Id, Mode, ProjectPath); Connect (attach selected) + optional "open project path" textbox + Connect headless; Disconnect button; connection label (ProjectName, Attached/Headless).
- Read Project Context group: big button, Cancel, progress ListBox (log), summary grid: files/nodes/edges/byKind (kind+count grid), warnings ListBox, dbPath text.
- MainViewModel orchestrates; all MCP calls awaited off-UI-thread (async, ConfigureAwait(false) in services; ViewModel resumes on UI thread via await in async void command handlers — CommunityToolkit RelayCommand supports async Task).

Tests:
- FakeToolCaller: dictionary tool→(args→response) with recorded calls; can enqueue isError results.
- WorkflowTests: happy path (call order get_project_info → export_all_blocks → export_tag_tables → export_udts → ingest_source; exportRoot ends with exports\<ProjectName>; result counts mapped), NOT_CONNECTED propagation when get_project_info errors, export failure aborts before ingest, cancellation between steps.
- AppSettingsTests: config override wins; default resolution finds exe relative to a fake solution layout created in temp dir.

Manual E2E (real): build App, run, attach to TIA with TestPLCExportDemo (or PEI), press button, summary shows; record in buildnote/log/app-ui01.md.

Docs:
- buildnote/plan/app.md created at implementation start (design doc, repo convention).
- agent.md: solution layout (add App), inventory table? App isn't MCP server — layout section + Key Files + status line update.
- initialLaunch_20260717.md: annotate step 7 split (UI-0/1 read-only shell pulled ahead of steps 4-6; UI-2 browser next; UI-3 = generate/review/apply after step 6).

Contracts change: add Contracts/Knowledge/IngestResult.cs; update Mcp.Knowledge KnowledgeTools to return it (namespace Contracts.Knowledge; Mcp.Knowledge references Contracts — check Mcp.Knowledge.csproj currently has NO Contracts reference — add ProjectReference). Framework rule: Contracts is netstandard2.0, Mcp.Knowledge net8 — fine. JSON shape must remain identical (camelCase) — ToolJson uses camelCase policy; Contracts DTO property names PascalCase serialize to camelCase. byKind is SortedDictionary<string,int> → serialize as object. IngestResult fields: DbPath, Source, FilesFound, FilesImported, Nodes, Edges, ByKind, Warnings, DurationMs. Hmm — wait, does promoting change mcp-knowledge's "No Contracts dependency this step" note? That note said "Promote to Contracts when a second consumer (UI/agent) needs typed access" — the App is that consumer, so this is the planned trigger. Update the note in mcp-knowledge.md? Minor doc touch: yes, one line in app.md is enough; leave mcp-knowledge.md untouched... actually its §4 bullet says "No Contracts dependency this step" — historically accurate for that step. The app.md doc will record the promotion. Fine.

Also App referencing Contracts: Contracts.csproj netstandard2.0 — includes Class1.cs (leftover). Fine.

Exit criteria for this step (UI-0+UI-1):
- dotnet build sln + dotnet test green.
- App launches, both servers start, env check displayed.
- On a real TIA session: Read Project Context completes → plc-knowledge.db written under exports\<project>\, summary matches a CLI ingest of same folder.
- buildnote/log entry + docs updated.

Risks:
- Long export on PEI-scale project (hundreds of blocks): async + cancellation + log; engineering server is single STA — calls serialized, fine.
- CommunityToolkit.Mvvm restore availability → fallback hand-rolled MVVM base (2 tiny classes) noted.
- Exe path resolution across Debug/Release → config override escape hatch.
- StdioClientTransport stderr capture → verify StandardErrorLines type at implementation; fallback: own Process spawn? No — trust SDK, it's designed for this (StandardErrorLines is a callback collection). Let me quickly check its type to be accurate in the plan... one more grep.

## Approved Plan:
# App (WPF) — UI-0 shell+MCP host & UI-1 "Read Project Context" (Phase 2, step 7a)

Direction approved by user 2026-07-18: **UI-first read-only** — pull the read-only slices of step 7 (plan `initialLaunch_20260717.md` §Phase 2.7) ahead of steps 4–6 (source-editor, version-control, agent), because the export→ingest chain needs nothing from them. This plan implements **UI-0 + UI-1**; UI-2 (context browser) is the follow-up step; UI-3 (generate/review/apply comments) stays after step 6. A durable copy of this design lands in `buildnote/plan/app.md` at implementation start (repo convention).

## 0. What exists (verified)

- `mcp-engineering` (net48, `src/Mcp.Engineering/bin/<cfg>/net48/Mcp.Engineering.exe`): `check_environment`, `list_sessions`, `connect(sessionId|projectPath, withUI)`, `get_project_info`, `export_all_blocks(outputDir)`, `export_tag_tables(outputDir)`, `export_udts(outputDir)`, `disconnect`… Results are typed `Contracts.Engineering` DTOs (`SessionInfo`, `ConnectionInfo`, `ProjectInfo`, `EnvCheckResult`, `ExportResult`).
- `mcp-knowledge` (net8, `src/Mcp.Knowledge/bin/<cfg>/net8.0/Mcp.Knowledge.exe`): `ingest_source(exportRoot, dbPath?)` (default dbPath `<exportRoot>/plc-knowledge.db`), `query`, `get_schema`, `get_block`, `get_network`, `search`. Results are anonymous JSON today.
- MCP SDK pinned `ModelContextProtocol` 1.4.1. **Client** types live in `ModelContextProtocol.Core` 1.4.1: `McpClient.CreateAsync(IClientTransport, McpClientOptions?, ILoggerFactory?, ct)`, `McpClient.CallToolAsync(...)`, `StdioClientTransport` + `StdioClientTransportOptions { Command, Arguments, Name, WorkingDirectory, EnvironmentVariables, StandardErrorLines }` — `StandardErrorLines` is a per-line stderr callback (drives the UI log pane).
- CommunityToolkit.Mvvm NOT in local NuGet cache → will restore from nuget.org on first build (fallback if restore impossible: hand-rolled 30-line `ViewModelBase`+`RelayCommand`; decide at scaffold time).
- `.sln` uses solution folders `src`/`tests`. Test pins to mirror: `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` 17.11.1, `xunit` 2.9.2, `xunit.runner.visualstudio` 2.8.2.
- DeepSeek config convention: `%APPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\config.json` (git-ignored) — reused for optional server-path overrides.

## 1. Locked decisions

1. **New project `src/App/`** — WPF, `net8.0-windows`, `WinExe`, `UseWPF=true`, nullable + implicit usings (net8 conventions of the repo). Namespace `App` (no useless prefix). Packages: `ModelContextProtocol.Core` 1.4.1 (client only — full `ModelContextProtocol` package is server-oriented), `CommunityToolkit.Mvvm` 8.4.x.
2. **Promote `IngestResult` to `Contracts`** — mcp-knowledge.md §4 already defers this until "a second consumer (UI/agent) needs typed access"; the App is that consumer. Add `Contracts/Knowledge/IngestResult.cs` (DbPath, Source, FilesFound, FilesImported, Nodes, Edges, ByKind `SortedDictionary<string,int>`, Warnings `List<string>`, DurationMs), make `KnowledgeTools.Ingest` return it, add Contracts ProjectReference to Mcp.Knowledge. **JSON shape must stay byte-identical** (existing tests + e2e guard this). get_block/get_network/search DTOs promote later with UI-2.
3. **MCP host inside App, extraction documented.** `App/Mcp/` holds the client host + `IMcpToolCaller` seam; when step 6 (Agent) lands, `Mcp/` + `Workflows/` move to a shared net8 library. No premature shared project now.
4. **Workflow layer = two triggers, one orchestration.** `ReadProjectContextWorkflow` orchestrates the read-only chain and is callable from the UI button now and from the AI agent later. Connect/disconnect stays UI session state (NOT part of the workflow); the workflow validates the connection via `get_project_info`.
5. **Export root convention:** `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\<projectName>\` (projectName from `get_project_info`, invalid path chars stripped; exports can be large → Local, not Roaming). dbPath = default `<exportRoot>/plc-knowledge.db`.
6. **Server exe resolution:** `%APPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\config.json` optional keys `engineeringServerPath`/`knowledgeServerPath` override; default = walk up from `AppContext.BaseDirectory` to the folder containing `AgentAssistPlcDev.sln`, then `src/Mcp.Engineering/bin/<Debug|Release>/net48/Mcp.Engineering.exe` and `src/Mcp.Knowledge/bin/<Debug|Release>/net8.0/Mcp.Knowledge.exe` (configuration via `#if DEBUG`). Missing exe → explicit error in the status strip + log (no silent fallback).
7. **All MCP traffic through `IMcpToolCaller`** (`Task<T> CallAsync<T>(string tool, object args, CancellationToken)`): parses first `TextContentBlock` JSON → `T`; on `isError` throws `ToolCallException(Code, Message, Remediation)`. Workflows + ViewModels depend only on the interface → unit tests use a scripted fake, no MCP/WPF needed.
8. **No UI automation tests.** Tests cover workflow orchestration + settings resolution only.

## 2. Project & file layout

```
src/App/
  App.csproj                     net8.0-windows, WinExe, UseWPF, ModelContextProtocol.Core 1.4.1, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.4.x, ProjectReference → Contracts
  App.xaml(.cs)                  StartupUri → MainWindow
  MainWindow.xaml(.cs)           status strip + sessions group + Read-Project-Context group (below)
  ViewModels/MainViewModel.cs    CommunityToolkit [ObservableProperty]/[RelayCommand]; owns McpHost; async commands
  Mcp/IMcpToolCaller.cs
  Mcp/ToolCallException.cs       Code/Message/Remediation
  Mcp/McpServerConnection.cs     one server: StdioClientTransport + McpClient; stderr callback → log event; IMcpToolCaller impl
  Mcp/McpHost.cs                 owns Engineering + Knowledge connections; start/stop (IAsyncDisposable); exposes both as IMcpToolCaller
  Workflows/ReadProjectContextWorkflow.cs
  Workflows/ReadProjectContextResult.cs
  AppSettings.cs                 config.json read + exe-path resolution (pure functions for tests)
tests/App.Tests/
  App.Tests.csproj               net8, xUnit pins mirrored from Mcp.Knowledge.Tests; ProjectReference → App
  FakeToolCaller.cs              scripted responses + recorded calls
  ReadProjectContextWorkflowTests.cs
  AppSettingsTests.cs
```

`MainWindow` (UI-0/1 scope, plain WPF, no styling library):
- **Status strip:** env summary from `check_environment` (TIA version, Openness group membership — red/green), server states (Engineering/Knowledge: running/stopped).
- **Sessions group:** "Refresh sessions" → grid of `list_sessions` (Id, Mode, ProjectPath); "Connect" attaches selected session; optional project-path textbox + "Open headless"; "Disconnect"; connection label (`ProjectName`, attached/headless).
- **Read Project Context group:** button + Cancel; progress `ListBox` (workflow log lines + server stderr); summary area: files/nodes/edges, by-kind grid (kind/count), warnings list, `dbPath`.

## 3. ReadProjectContextWorkflow (the orchestration, both triggers)

```
RunAsync(ct)
 1. engineering.get_project_info            → projectName (error ⇒ abort, surface code/message)
 2. exportRoot = %LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\<sanitized projectName>
 3. engineering.export_all_blocks(exportRoot)   → log "N blocks exported (Xs)"
 4. engineering.export_tag_tables(exportRoot)   → log count (0 is fine)
 5. engineering.export_udts(exportRoot)         → log count (0 is fine)
 6. knowledge.ingest_source(exportRoot)         → IngestResult (dbPath default)
 7. return ReadProjectContextResult { ProjectName, ExportRoot, DbPath, BlocksExported, TagTablesExported, UdtsExported, Ingest }
```

- Progress: `IProgress<string>` log lines with per-step timings (Stopwatch); CancellationToken checked between steps (a running export call can't be aborted — cancel takes effect at the next step boundary).
- Any `ToolCallException` aborts the workflow; UI shows code+message+remediation in the log and status strip.

## 4. Build order (implementation steps)

1. Create `buildnote/plan/app.md` from this plan; annotate `initialLaunch_20260717.md` §Phase 2.7 (step 7 split: 7a read-only shell UI-0/1 ← this, 7b browser UI-2, 7c comment flow after step 6) and `agent.md` (layout + status + Key Files).
2. `Contracts/Knowledge/IngestResult.cs` + `KnowledgeTools` returns it + Mcp.Knowledge references Contracts. Run existing knowledge tests (JSON shape guard).
3. Scaffold `src/App` (+ add to `.sln` under `src`); placeholder MainWindow; `dotnet build` green. (CommunityToolkit restore check here → fallback hand-rolled MVVM if needed.)
4. `App/Mcp/*` + `AppSettings` + `tests/App.Tests` (AppSettingsTests); status strip runs real `check_environment` at startup.
5. `ReadProjectContextWorkflow` + `FakeToolCaller` + workflow tests (call order, args incl. exportRoot, result mapping, error propagation, cancel between steps).
6. MainWindow UI + MainViewModel wiring (sessions grid, connect/disconnect, button+cancel, log, summary).
7. `dotnet build AgentAssistPlcDev.sln` + `dotnet test` green.
8. **Manual E2E on real TIA:** launch App → attach to a running TIA session (TestPLCExportDemo or PEI) → Read Project Context → summary matches a CLI `ingest_source` of the same folder; screenshots/log to `buildnote/log/app-ui01-YYYYMMDD.md`.

## 5. Tests

- `ReadProjectContextWorkflowTests` — happy path (exact tool call order `get_project_info`→`export_all_blocks`→`export_tag_tables`→`export_udts`→`ingest_source`; exportRoot ends `exports\<ProjectName>`; counts mapped into result), get_project_info error aborts before any export, export error aborts before ingest, cancellation stops between steps (ingest never called).
- `AppSettingsTests` — config override wins; default resolution finds both exes in a temp fake solution layout (`src/Mcp.Engineering/bin/Debug/net48/...`); missing sln → explicit error.
- Existing suites must stay green (Contracts promotion is shape-preserving).

## 6. Exit criteria (UI-0+UI-1)

- `dotnet build` + `dotnet test` green across the solution.
- App starts both servers, shows env check, attaches to a real TIA session.
- One button press runs export→ingest end-to-end on a real project; summary (nodes/edges by kind, warnings, dbPath) displayed; DB on disk under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\<project>\plc-knowledge.db`.
- `buildnote/plan/app.md`, `agent.md`, `initialLaunch` annotations, `buildnote/log/` E2E record.

## 7. Non-goals (this step)

UI-2 context browser (block tree, get_block/get_network/search panes), chat panel, comment generation/apply, DeepSeek, source-editor, version-control, installer/packaging, multi-server beyond the current two.

## 8. Risks & mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| CommunityToolkit.Mvvm not restorable offline | Fallback hand-rolled `ViewModelBase`/`RelayCommand` (tiny); decide at scaffold step |
| Exe path resolution wrong config (Debug/Release) | config.json override; resolved paths shown in log at startup; missing exe = explicit error |
| Long export on PEI-scale project | async commands, cancel between steps, per-step timing log |
| Engineering server single-STA serialization | calls are awaited sequentially by design (workflow is sequential) |
