```json
{
  "content": "# App (WPF) — UI-0 shell+MCP host & UI-1 \"Read Project Context\" (Phase 2, step 7a)\n\nDirection approved by user 2026-07-18: **UI-first read-only** — the read-only slices of `initialLaunch_20260717.md` §Phase 2.7 are pulled ahead of steps 4–6 (source-editor, version-control, agent), because the export→ingest chain needs nothing from them. This document covers **UI-0 + UI-1**; UI-2 (context browser) is the follow-up step; UI-3 (generate/review/apply comments) stays after step 6.\n\n## 1. Purpose\n\nA WPF shell that spawns the two existing MCP servers as child processes and exposes the read-only project-context chain behind one button: **Read Project Context** = `get_project_info` → `export_all_blocks` → `export_tag_tables` → `export_udts` → `ingest_source`, ending in a summary view (nodes/edges by kind, warnings, dbPath). The same orchestration is later triggered by the AI agent — two triggers, one workflow.\n\n## 2. Locked decisions\n\n1. **`src/App/`** — WPF, `net8.0-windows`, `WinExe`, `UseWPF=true`, nullable + implicit usings. Namespace `App`. Packages: `ModelContextProtocol.Core` 1.4.1 (client types live there in SDK 1.4.x — `McpClient`, `StdioClientTransport`), `CommunityToolkit.Mvvm` 8.4.x. ProjectReference → `Contracts`.\n2. **`IngestResult` promoted to `Contracts`** (`Contracts/Knowledge/IngestResult.cs`) — mcp-knowledge.md §4 deferred this until a second consumer needed typed access; the App is that consumer. `KnowledgeTools.Ingest` returns it; JSON shape byte-identical (guarded by existing tests + e2e). get_block/get_network/search DTOs promote with UI-2.\n3. **MCP host lives in App** (`App/Mcp/`) with an `IMcpToolCaller` seam; when step 6 (Agent) lands, `Mcp/` + `Workflows/` move to a shared net8 library. No premature shared project.\n4. **`ReadProjectContextWorkflow` = the single orchestration** (UI button now, AI agent later). Connect/disconnect is UI session state, NOT part of the workflow; the workflow validates the connection via `get_project_info`.\n5. **Export root:** `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\PlcAiAssistant\\exports\\<projectName>\\` (invalid path chars stripped; Local, not Roaming — exports are large). dbPath = default `<exportRoot>/plc-knowledge.db`.\n6. **Server exe resolution:** `%APPDATA%\\PlcAiAssistant\\config.json` optional keys `engineeringServerPath` / `knowledgeServerPath` override; default = walk up from `AppContext.BaseDirectory` to `AgentAssistPlcDev.sln`, then `src/Mcp.Engineering/bin/<Debug|Release>/net48/Mcp.Engineering.exe` and `src/Mcp.Knowledge/bin/<Debug|Release>/net8.0/Mcp.Knowledge.exe` (`#if DEBUG`). Missing exe → explicit error; resolved paths logged at startup.\n7. **All MCP traffic through `IMcpToolCaller`** — `Task<T> CallAsync<T>(string tool, object args, CancellationToken)`: first `TextContentBlock` JSON → `T`; `isError` ⇒ `ToolCallException(Code, Message, Remediation)`. Server stderr streams via `StdioClientTransportOptions.StandardErrorLines` into the UI log pane.\n8. **No UI automation tests.** Tests cover workflow orchestration + settings resolution with a scripted fake caller.\n\n## 3. Layout\n\n```\nsrc/App/\n  App.csproj / App.xaml(.cs) / MainWindow.xaml(.cs)\n  ViewModels/MainViewModel.cs\n  Mcp/IMcpToolCaller.cs, ToolCallException.cs, McpServerConnection.cs, McpHost.cs\n  Workflows/ReadProjectContextWorkflow.cs, ReadProjectContextResult.cs\n  AppSettings.cs\ntests/App.Tests/\n  App.Tests.csproj (xUnit pins as Mcp.Knowledge.Tests; ProjectReference → App)\n  FakeToolCaller.cs, ReadProjectContextWorkflowTests.cs, AppSettingsTests.cs\n```\n\nMainWindow: status strip (env check summary, server states), sessions group (list/attach/open-headless/disconnect), Read-Project-Context group (button + cancel, progress log, ingest summary + warnings + dbPath).\n\n## 4. Workflow\n\n```\nRunAsync(ct)\n 1. engineering.get_project_info            → projectName (error ⇒ abort)\n 2. exportRoot = %LOCALAPPDATA%\\PlcAiAssistant\\exports\\<sanitized projectName>\n 3. engineering.export_all_blocks(exportRoot)\n 4. engineering.export_tag_tables(exportRoot)   (0 is fine)\n 5. engineering.export_udts(exportRoot)         (0 is fine)\n 6. knowledge.ingest_source(exportRoot)         → IngestResult\n 7. return ReadProjectContextResult { ProjectName, ExportRoot, DbPath, BlocksExported,\n                                       TagTablesExported, UdtsExported, Ingest }\n```\n\n`IProgress<string>` log lines with per-step timings; CancellationToken checked between steps (a running export call finishes; cancel takes effect at the next boundary). Any `ToolCallException` aborts and surfaces code/message/remediation.\n\n## 5. Tests\n\n- Workflow: exact call order; exportRoot ends `exports\\<ProjectName>`; counts mapped; `get_project_info` error aborts before export; export error aborts before ingest; cancel between steps ⇒ ingest never called.\n- AppSettings: config override wins; default resolution finds both exes in a temp fake solution layout; missing sln ⇒ explicit error.\n- Existing suites stay green (Contracts promotion is shape-preserving).\n\n## 6. Exit criteria\n\n- `dotnet build` + `dotnet test` green.\n- App starts both servers, shows env check, attaches to a real TIA session.\n- One button press runs export→ingest on a real project; summary displayed; DB under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\PlcAiAssistant\\exports\\<project>\\plc-knowledge.db`.\n- E2E recorded in `buildnote/log/`.\n\n## 7. Non-goals\n\nUI-2 context browser, chat panel, comment generation/apply, DeepSeek, source-editor, version-control, packaging, servers beyond the current two.\n\n## 8. Risks\n\n| Risk | Mitigation |\n|---|---|\n| CommunityToolkit.Mvvm restore fails | Hand-rolled `ViewModelBase`/`RelayCommand` fallback |\n| Wrong exe config resolved (Debug/Release) | config.json override; resolved paths logged at startup |\n| Long export on large projects | async + cancel between steps + per-step timing log |\n| Contracts promotion breaks knowledge JSON | existing tests + `scripts/e2e-knowledge.json` as shape guard |\n",
```
```json
  "path": "C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/buildnote/plan/app.md"
```
```json
}
```

```xml
  <PropertyGroup>

    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>

</Project>
```

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

The namespace/class collision I worried about: `namespace App { class App }` — the generated `App.g.cs` references `App.App` and gets confused (CS0426: type name 'App' does not exist in type 'App'). The WPF generated code does `App.App.Main()` or similar and the namespace swallows the class name.

Fix: rename the application class to avoid the collision. Options:
- Root namespace `App`, application class named `App` → broken (current).
- Rename application class to something else — but WPF convention... The cleanest: keep namespace `App`, name the application class `Program`? No — WPF needs Application-derived class; common alternative: `AppShell`? Or use `x:Class="App.AppShell"`. Hmm, but `App.xaml` file name vs class name don't need to match... Actually cleaner: class `App : Application` in a *nested* namespace? No.

Simplest: rename the Application class to `AppShell`? Or keep file App.xaml but class `App.Program`? Unusual.

Standard fix people use for namespace==App collision: name the application class `App` and add `<RootNamespace>` different from class... the collision is inherent: within `namespace App`, referring to `App.App` resolves weirdly in generated code which writes `App.App.Main()`. The .g.cs generated code at line 63 probably has `global::App.App.Main()` — hmm, with global:: it should work. The error is in obj App.g.cs(63,17): `The type name 'App' does not exist in the type 'App'` — the generated code does `App.App app = new App.App();` without global::, and inside... whatever.

Pragmatic fix: rename the class to `AppHost`? Let me just rename Application class to `AppShell`:
- App.xaml: `x:Class="App.AppShell"`, file name stays App.xaml? x:Class and file name mismatch is allowed. But template-generated App.g.cs derives from x:Class name. Rename both files to AppShell.xaml for clarity? StartupUri="MainWindow.xaml" unchanged.

Wait — actually simpler and more conventional: keep the WPF Application class in the root namespace but name it `App`, and set the project's `<RootNamespace>` to something else like `App.Ui`? No — the "no useless prefixes" rule wants namespace `App`. The class INSIDE namespace App shouldn't be named App. `AppShell` is clear and honest. Or `App.Program`? AppShell it is.

Changes:
- App.xaml → AppShell.xaml (rename via mv), x:Class="App.AppShell"
- App.xaml.cs → AppShell.xaml.cs, class AppShell
- StartupUri unchanged.

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

CS5001: no Main method. WPF generates Main() from the App.xaml when its build action is `ApplicationDefinition`. After `mv`, the new file AppShell.xaml may have been picked up as `Page` instead of `ApplicationDefinition`? Actually SDK-style WPF auto-detects XAML by item type: `App.xaml` is automatically ApplicationDefinition by **file name convention**? No — in SDK-style projects, the default globs include `**/*.xaml` as Page, and `App.xaml` specifically gets ApplicationDefinition via default items? Let me recall: Microsoft.NET.Sdk.WindowsDesktop has default item `ApplicationDefinition` include `App.xaml` only. Since I renamed to AppShell.xaml, it's now treated as Page → no Main generated.

Fix: explicitly set ApplicationDefinition in csproj:
```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <ApplicationDefinition Include="AppShell.xaml" />
</ItemGroup>
```
And possibly remove the duplicate Page inclusion — the default glob will include AppShell.xaml as Page too; ApplicationDefinition + Page duplicate → need `<Page Remove="AppShell.xaml" />`. In SDK-style WPF projects:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Page Remove="AppShell.xaml" />
  <ApplicationDefinition Include="AppShell.xaml" />
</ItemGroup>
```

Alternative: rename back to App.xaml (keep class AppShell) — file name App.xaml gets ApplicationDefinition automatically, class can be AppShell. That's simpler (no csproj change): the auto ApplicationDefinition only depends on the FILE name App.xaml. Let me do that: mv AppShell.xaml → App.xaml (x:Class stays "App.AppShell"), and AppShell.xaml.cs → keep name or rename to App.xaml.cs for the partial-class file convention. File name of the code-behind doesn't matter. Convention: App.xaml + App.xaml.cs. Class: AppShell. Slightly unusual but works and avoids csproj items.

Hmm — mixed feelings: file named App.xaml containing class AppShell is a bit confusing for future readers. The explicit csproj approach keeps names consistent (AppShell.xaml as ApplicationDefinition). I'll go with the csproj approach — clearer long-term.

```csharp
/// <summary>An MCP tool returned isError=true with the structured { code, message, remediation } payload.</summary>
```

3. `src/App/Mcp/McpServerConnection.cs` — wraps one server process:
```csharp
using System.Text.Json;
using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
using ModelContextProtocol.Client;
using ModelContextProtocol.Protocol;

namespace App.Mcp;

/// <summary>One MCP server child process (stdio). Stderr lines are forwarded to <see cref="StderrLine"/>.</summary>
public sealed class McpServerConnection : IMcpToolCaller, IAsyncDisposable
{
    private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions Json = new()
    {
        PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
        PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
    };

    private readonly string serverName;
    private McpClient? client;

    public McpServerConnection(string serverName, string exePath)
    {
        ...
    }

    public event Action<string>? StderrLine;

    public bool IsRunning => client != null;

    public async Task StartAsync(CancellationToken ct = default)
    {
        if (!File.Exists(exePath)) throw new FileNotFoundException(...)
        var transport = new StdioClientTransport(new StdioClientTransportOptions
        {
            Command = exePath,
            Name = serverName,
            StandardErrorLines = line => StderrLine?.Invoke($"[{serverName}] {line}"),
        });
        client = await McpClient.CreateAsync(transport, cancellationToken: ct);
    }

    public async Task<T> CallAsync<T>(string tool, object args, CancellationToken ct = default)
    {
        if (client == null) throw new InvalidOperationException($"Server '{serverName}' is not started.");
        var arguments = args.GetType().GetProperties()... 
    }
}
```

Hmm — CallToolAsync signature: `CallToolAsync(string toolName, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?>? arguments = null, IProgress<ProgressNotificationValue>? progress = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)` (returns ValueTask<CallToolResult>? or Task<CallToolResult>). To convert `object args` (anonymous like `new { exportRoot }`) to the dictionary: serialize args to JsonElement then enumerate properties:

```csharp
var element = JsonSerializer.SerializeToElement(args, Json);
var arguments = element.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Object
    ? element.EnumerateObject().ToDictionary(p => p.Name, p => (object?)p.Value)
    : new Dictionary<string, object?>();
```
Wait — but property names must match the server's parameter names. The servers' MCP tool params are C# parameter names (e.g. `exportRoot`, `dbPath`, `outputDir`, `sessionId`). With camelCase naming policy, anonymous `new { exportRoot = x }` serializes to `exportRoot`. Since callers pass anonymous objects with already-correct camelCase names, serialize with camelCase policy is consistent. Good.

Then:
```csharp
var result = await client.CallToolAsync(tool, arguments, cancellationToken: ct);
if (result.IsError == true) → parse error payload { error: { code, message, remediation } } → throw ToolCallException
var text = result.Content.OfType<TextContentBlock>().First().Text;
return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(text, Json)!;
```

TextContentBlock type is in ModelContextProtocol.Protocol namespace (the Core package).

Error payload shape: `{ "error": { "code": "...", "message": "...", "remediation": "..." } }`. Parse via JsonDocument.

4. `src/App/Mcp/McpHost.cs`:
```csharp
namespace App.Mcp;

/// <summary>Owns the MCP server child processes for the app session (engineering + knowledge).</summary>
public sealed class McpHost : IAsyncDisposable
{
    public McpHost(AppSettings settings)
    {
        Engineering = new McpServerConnection("engineering", settings.EngineeringServerPath);
        Knowledge = new McpServerConnection("knowledge", settings.KnowledgeServerPath);
    }

    public McpServerConnection Engineering { get; }
    public McpServerConnection Knowledge { get; }

    public event Action<string>? ServerLog — forward both connections' StderrLine.

    public Task StartAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) — start both (sequential? parallel Task.WhenAll fine).
    public async ValueTask DisposeAsync() — dispose clients (McpClient implements IAsyncDisposable? In 1.4.x, McpClient : IAsyncDisposable). StdioClientTransport also disposable. McpClient.DisposeAsync should shut down transport/process. 
}
```

Does McpServerConnection need to keep the transport reference for disposal? Disposing McpClient should dispose the transport (the SDK: McpClient.DisposeAsync disposes the session transport... I believe CreateAsync's transport gets owned by the client; disposing the client disposes transport and the child process gets killed? StdioClientTransport kills the process on dispose (ShutdownTimeout option exists for graceful shutdown). To be safe, keep the transport field and dispose it too (IAsyncDisposable).

McpServerConnection.DisposeAsync:
```csharp
if (client != null) await client.DisposeAsync();
if (transport != null) await transport.DisposeAsync();
```
Wrap in try/catch (best effort, log).

5. `src/App/AppSettings.cs`:
```csharp
using System.Text.Json;

namespace App;

/// <summary>Resolves MCP server exe paths: %APPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\config.json override, else solution-layout default.</summary>
public sealed class AppSettings
{
    public AppSettings(string engineeringServerPath, string knowledgeServerPath) { ... }

    public string EngineeringServerPath { get; }
    public string KnowledgeServerPath { get; }

    public static AppSettings Load() => Load(
        Path.Combine(Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData), "PlcAiAssistant", "config.json"),
        AppContext.BaseDirectory);

    public static AppSettings Load(string configPath, string baseDirectory)
    {
        var (engineeringOverride, knowledgeOverride) = ReadOverrides(configPath);
        var solutionDir = FindSolutionDirectory(baseDirectory) ?? throw new InvalidOperationException(...);
        var engineering = engineeringOverride ?? Path.Combine(solutionDir, "src", "Mcp.Engineering", "bin", Configuration, "net48", "Mcp.Engineering.exe");
        var knowledge = knowledgeOverride ?? Path.Combine(solutionDir, "src", "Mcp.Knowledge", "bin", Configuration, "net8.0", "Mcp.Knowledge.exe");
        return new AppSettings(engineering, knowledge);
    }

    private const string Configuration =
#if DEBUG
        "Debug";
#else
        "Release";
#endif

    public static string? FindSolutionDirectory(string startDirectory)
    {
        var directory = new DirectoryInfo(startDirectory);
        while (directory != null)
        {
            if (File.Exists(Path.Combine(directory.FullName, "AgentAssistPlcDev.sln"))) return directory.FullName;
            directory = directory.Parent;
        }
        return null;
    }

    private static (string? Engineering, string? Knowledge) ReadOverrides(string configPath)
    {
        try
        {
            if (!File.Exists(configPath)) return (null, null);
            using var document = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(configPath));
            var root = document.RootElement;
            return (GetString(root, "engineeringServerPath"), GetString(root, "knowledgeServerPath"));
        }
        catch (JsonException) { return (null, null); }
    }
}
```

Note: config.json is the DeepSeek config (has apiKey etc.) — we only read our two keys; tolerant parse. 

Export root helper — also in AppSettings? The workflow needs `ResolveExportRoot(projectName)`:
```csharp
public static string ResolveExportRoot(string projectName)
{
    var sanitized = string.Concat(projectName.Select(c => Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars().Contains(c) ? '_' : c));
    return Path.Combine(Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData), "PlcAiAssistant", "exports", sanitized);
}
```
Put in workflow or AppSettings? It's a path convention — put it in AppSettings as static, or in the workflow. I'll put it in AppSettings (all path conventions in one place) — testable.

```json
{
  "content": "using System.Text.Json;\nusing ModelContextProtocol.Client;\nusing ModelContextProtocol.Protocol;\n\nnamespace App.Mcp;\n\n/// <summary>\n/// One MCP server child process over stdio (buildnote/plan/app.md §2.7).\n/// The server's stderr lines are forwarded via <see cref=\"StderrLine\"/> (stdout is the JSON-RPC channel).\n/// </summary>\npublic sealed class McpServerConnection : IMcpToolCaller, IAsyncDisposable\n{\n    private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions Json = new()\n    {\n        PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,\n        PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,\n    };\n\n    private readonly string serverName;\n    private readonly string exePath;\n    private StdioClientTransport? transport;\n    private McpClient? client;\n\n    public McpServerConnection(string serverName, string exePath)\n    {\n        this.serverName = serverName;\n        this.exePath = exePath;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>Raised once per stderr line received from the server process.</summary>\n    public event Action<string>? StderrLine;\n\n    public bool IsRunning => client != null;\n\n    public async Task StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        if (!File.Exists(exePath))\n        {\n            throw new FileNotFoundException(\n                $\"MCP server '{serverName}' exe not found: {exePath}. Build the server project first, or set the override in %APPDATA%\\\\PlcAiAssistant\\\\config.json.\",\n                exePath);\n        }\n\n        transport = new StdioClientTransport(new StdioClientTransportOptions\n        {\n            Command = exePath,\n            Name = serverName,\n            StandardErrorLines = line => StderrLine?.Invoke($\"[{serverName}] {line}\"),\n        });\n        client = await McpClient.CreateAsync(transport, cancellationToken: cancellationToken);\n    }\n\n    public async Task<T> CallAsync<T>(string tool, object args, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        if (client == null)\n        {\n            throw new InvalidOperationException($\"MCP server '{serverName}' is not started.\");\n        }\n\n        var result = await client.CallToolAsync(tool, ToArguments(args), cancellationToken: cancellationToken);\n        var text = result.Content.OfType<TextContentBlock>().FirstOrDefault()?.Text ?? string.Empty;\n        if (result.IsError == true)\n        {\n            throw ParseError(text);\n        }\n\n        return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(text, Json)\n            ?? throw new InvalidOperationException($\"Tool '{tool}' on server '{serverName}' returned an empty result.\");\n    }\n\n    public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()\n    {\n        try\n        {\n            if (client != null)\n            {\n                await client.DisposeAsync();\n            }\n        }\n        catch\n        {\n            // best effort; the child process dies with the transport anyway\n        }\n\n        try\n        {\n            if (transport != null)\n            {\n                await transport.DisposeAsync();\n            }\n        }\n        catch\n        {\n            // best effort\n        }\n\n        client = null;\n        transport = null;\n    }\n\n    private static IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?> ToArguments(object args)\n    {\n        var element = JsonSerializer.SerializeToElement(args, Json);\n        if (element.ValueKind != JsonValueKind.Object)\n        {\n            return new Dictionary<string, object?>();\n        }\n\n        return element.EnumerateObject().ToDictionary(property => property.Name, property => (object?)property.Value);\n    }\n\n    private static ToolCallException ParseError(string text)\n    {\n        try\n        {\n            using var document = JsonDocument.Parse(text);\n            var error = document.RootElement.GetProperty(\"error\");\n            return new ToolCallException(\n                error.GetProperty(\"code\").GetString() ?? \"UNEXPECTED_ERROR\",\n                error.GetProperty(\"message\").GetString() ?? text,\n                error.TryGetProperty(\"remediation\", out var remediation) ? remediation.GetString() : null);\n        }\n        catch (JsonException)\n        {\n            return new ToolCallException(\"UNEXPECTED_ERROR\", text, null);\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "path": "C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/src/App/Mcp/McpServerConnection.cs"
}
```

```json
{

Path.Combine(solutionDirectory, \"src\", \"Mcp.Engineering\", \"bin\", BuildConfiguration, \"net48\", \"Mcp.Engineering.exe\"),\n            knowledgeOverride ?? '_' : character).ToArray());\n        return Path.Combine(\n            Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData),\n            \"PlcAiAssistant\",\n            \"exports\",\n            sanitized);\n    }\n\n    public static string? FindSolutionDirectory(string startDirectory)\n    {\n        var directory = new DirectoryInfo(startDirectory);\n        while (directory != null)\n        {\n            if (File.Exists(Path.Combine(directory.FullName, SolutionFileName)))\n            {\n                return directory.FullName;\n            }\n\n            directory = directory.Parent;\n        }\n\n        return null;\n    }\n\n    private static (string? Engineering, string? Knowledge) ReadOverrides(string configPath)\n    {\n        try\n        {\n            if (!File.Exists(configPath))\n            {\n                return (null, null);\n            }\n\n            using var document = JsonDocument.Parse(File.ReadAllText(configPath));\n            var root = document.RootElement;\n            return (GetString(root, \"engineeringServerPath\"), GetString(root, \"knowledgeServerPath\"));\n        }\n        catch (JsonException)\n        {\n            return (null, null);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static string? GetString(JsonElement root, string property)\n    {\n        return root.TryGetProperty(property, out var element) && element.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String\n            ? element.GetString()\n            : null;\n    }\n}\n",
  "path": "C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/src/App/AppSettings.cs"
}
```

```csharp
using System.Diagnostics;
using App.Mcp;
using Contracts.Engineering;
using Contracts.Knowledge;

namespace App.Workflows;

/// <summary>
/// The Read Project Context orchestration (buildnote/plan/app.md §4) — one workflow, two triggers
/// (the UI button now, the AI agent in step 6). Requires an already-connected engineering session.
/// </summary>
public sealed class ReadProjectContextWorkflow
{
    private readonly IMcpToolCaller engineering;
    private readonly IMcpToolCaller knowledge;
    private readonly IProgress<string>? progress;

    public ReadProjectContextWorkflow(IMcpToolCaller engineering, IMcpToolCaller knowledge, IProgress<string>? progress = null)
    {
        this.engineering = engineering;
        this.knowledge = knowledge;
        this.progress = progress;
    }

    public async Task<ReadProjectContextResult> RunAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    {
        var info = await Timed("Reading project info", () =>
            engineering.CallAsync<ProjectInfo>("get_project_info", new { }, cancellationToken));
        var projectName = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(info.Name) ? "unknown" : info.Name!;
        var exportRoot = AppSettings.ResolveExportRoot(projectName);
        Log($"Export root: {exportRoot}");

        cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
        var blocks = await Timed("Exporting blocks", () =>
            engineering.CallAsync<ExportResult[]>("export_all_blocks", new { outputDir = exportRoot }, cancellationToken));

        cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
        var tagTables = await Timed("Exporting tag tables", () =>
            engineering.CallAsync<ExportResult[]>("export_tag_tables", new { outputDir = exportRoot }, cancellationToken));

        cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
        var udts = await Timed("Exporting UDTs", () =>
            engineering.CallAsync<ExportResult[]>("export_udts", new { outputDir = exportRoot }, cancellationToken));

        cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
        var ingest = await Timed("Building knowledge base", () =>
            knowledge.CallAsync<IngestResult>("ingest_source", new { exportRoot }, cancellationToken));

        Log($"Knowledge base: {ingest.Nodes} nodes, {ingest.Edges} edges → {ingest.DbPath}");
        return new ReadProjectContextResult
        {
            ProjectName = projectName,
            ExportRoot = exportRoot,
            DbPath = ingest.DbPath,
            BlocksExported = blocks.Length,
            TagTablesExported = tagTables.Length,
            UdtsExported = udts.Length,
            Ingest = ingest,
        };
    }
    ...
}
```

Timed helper:
```csharp
    private async Task<T> Timed<T>(string step, Func<Task<T>> action)
    {
        Log($"{step}…");
        var stopwatch = Stopwatch.StartNew();
        var result = await action();
        Log($"{step} — done in {stopwatch.ElapsedMilliseconds / 1000.0:0.0}s");
        return result;
    }

    private void Log(string message) => progress?.Report(message);
```

Note: App is net8.0-windows (UseWPF); a net8.0 test project referencing a net8.0-windows project — target framework compatibility: net8.0 test can reference net8.0-windows library? Referencing a windows-targeted project from net8.0 fails with TFM mismatch: "project targets 'net8.0-windows'; it cannot be referenced by a project that targets 'net8.0'"... Actually net8.0-windows10.0.x is a *platform-specific* TFM; a net8.0 project CAN reference a net8.0-windows project? No — the rule: a project can reference another whose TFM is compatible; net8.0-windows is more specific than net8.0, and referencing more-specific is NOT allowed (net8.0 → net8.0-windows fails; net8.0-windows → net8.0 works). So App.Tests must target `net8.0-windows` too. That's fine (tests run on Windows anyway). Set TargetFramework net8.0-windows in App.Tests. The existing test projects are plain net8.0, but they reference net8.0 libraries. App.Tests → net8.0-windows.

Hmm, do the App's non-UI classes (Mcp, Workflows, AppSettings) drag WPF assemblies into the test runtime? UseWPF adds WPF framework reference to the whole App assembly; tests linking against it need the WindowsDesktop runtime — available on this machine. net8.0-windows test project works with xunit. OK.

FakeToolCaller:
```csharp
using App.Mcp;

namespace App.Tests;

/// <summary>Scripted IMcpToolCaller: queue responses per tool; records every call.</summary>
internal sealed class FakeToolCaller : IMcpToolCaller
{
    private readonly Dictionary<string, Queue<Func<object>>> scripts = new(StringComparer.Ordinal);

    public List<(string Tool, object Args)> Calls { get; } = new();

    public FakeToolCaller Respond<T>(string tool, T response) — enqueue returning response object.
    public FakeToolCaller Fail(string tool, string code, string message, string? remediation = null) — enqueue throwing ToolCallException.

    public Task<T> CallAsync<T>(string tool, object args, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    {
        Calls.Add((tool, args));
        cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
        if (!scripts.TryGetValue(tool, out var queue) || queue.Count == 0)
        {
            throw new InvalidOperationException($"FakeToolCaller: no scripted response for '{tool}'.");
        }
        var result = queue.Dequeue()();
        return Task.FromResult((T)result);
    }
}
```

```json
{
  "content": "using App.Mcp;\n\nnamespace App.Tests;\n\n/// <summary>Scripted <see cref=\"IMcpToolCaller\"/>: queued responses per tool, every call recorded.</summary>\ninternal sealed class FakeToolCaller : IMcpToolCaller\n{\n    private readonly Dictionary<string, Queue<Func<object>>> scripts = new(StringComparer.Ordinal);\n\n    public List<string> Calls { get; } = new();\n\n    public Dictionary<string, List<object>> CallArgs { get; } = new();\n\n    public FakeToolCaller Respond<T>(string tool, T response) where T : notnull\n    {\n        Enqueue(tool, () => response);\n        return this;\n    }\n\n    public FakeToolCaller Fail(string tool, string code, string message, string? remediation = null)\n    {\n        Enqueue(tool, () => throw new ToolCallException(code, message, remediation));\n        return this;\n    }\n\n    public Task<T> CallAsync<T>(string tool, object args, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        Calls.Add(tool);\n        if (!CallArgs.TryGetValue(tool, out var list))\n        {\n            list = new List<object>();\n            CallArgs[tool] = list;\n        }\n\n        list.Add(args);\n        cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();\n        if (!scripts.TryGetValue(tool, out var queue) || queue.Count == 0)\n        {\n            throw new InvalidOperationException($\"FakeToolCaller: no scripted response for '{tool}'.\");\n        }\n\n        return Task.FromResult((T)queue.Dequeue()());\n    }\n\n    private void Enqueue(string tool, Func<object> response)\n    {\n        if (!scripts.TryGetValue(tool, out var queue))\n        {\n            queue = new Queue<Func<object>>();\n            scripts[tool] = queue;\n        }\n\n        queue.Enqueue(response);\n    }\n}\n",
  "path": "C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/tests/App.Tests/FakeToolCaller.cs"
}
```

```json
  "content": "using System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Threading;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing App.Mcp;\nusing App.Workflows;\nusing Contracts.Engineering;\nusing Contracts.Knowledge;\nusing Xunit;\n\nnamespace App.Tests;\n\npublic sealed class ReadProjectContextWorkflowTests\n{\n    [Fact]\n    public async Task RunsExportChainInOrderAndMapsResult()\n    {\n        var engineering = new FakeToolCaller()\n            .Respond(\"get_project_info\", new ProjectInfo { Name = \"TestPLC\" })\n            .Respond(\"export_all_blocks\", new[]\n            {\n                new ExportResult { BlockName = \"A\", Success = true },\n                new ExportResult { BlockName = \"B\", Success = false, Error = \"skipped\" },\n                new ExportResult { BlockName = \"C\", Success = true },\n            })\n            .Respond(\"export_tag_tables\", new[] { new ExportResult { BlockName = \"Tags\", Success = true } })\n            .Respond(\"export_udts\", new ExportResult[] { });\n        var knowledge = new FakeToolCaller()\n            .Respond(\"ingest_source\", new IngestResult { DbPath = \"x.db\", Nodes = 10, Edges = 20 });\n        var progress = new List<string>();\n        var workflow = new ReadProjectContextWorkflow(engineering, knowledge, new Progress<string>(progress.Add));\n\n        var result = await workflow.RunAsync();\n\n        Assert.Equal(\n            new[] { \"get_project_info\", \"export_all_blocks\", \"export_tag_tables\", \"export_udts\" },\n            engineering.Calls.ToArray());\n        Assert.Equal(new[] { \"ingest_source\" }, knowledge.Calls.ToArray());\n\n        // Same export root everywhere, derived from the project name.\n        foreach (var call in new[] { \"export_all_blocks\", \"export_tag_tables\", \"export_udts\" })\n        {\n            var args = engineering.CallArgs[call][0];\n            var outputDir = (string)args.GetType().GetProperty(\"outputDir\")!.GetValue(args)!;\n            Assert.EndsWith(\"TestPLC\", outputDir);\n        }\n\n        var ingestArgs = knowledge.CallArgs[\"ingest_source\"][0];\n        var exportRoot = (string)ingestArgs.GetType().GetProperty(\"exportRoot\")!.GetValue(ingestArgs)!;\n        Assert.EndsWith(\"TestPLC\", exportRoot);\n\n        // Only successful exports count.\n        Assert.Equal(\"TestPLC\", result.ProjectName);\n        Assert.Equal(2, result.BlocksExported);\n        Assert.Equal(1, result.TagTablesExported);\n        Assert.Equal(0, result.UdtsExported);\n        Assert.Equal(\"x.db\", result.DbPath);\n        Assert.Equal(10, result.Ingest.Nodes);\n        Assert.NotEmpty(progress);\n    }\n\n    [Fact]\n    public async Task ProjectInfoErrorAbortsBeforeAnyExport()\n    {\n        var engineering = new FakeToolCaller()\n            .Fail(\"get_project_info\", \"NOT_CONNECTED\", \"No TIA session is connected.\");\n        var knowledge = new FakeToolCaller();\n        var workflow = new ReadProjectContextWorkflow(engineering, knowledge);\n\n        var error = await Assert.ThrowsAsync<ToolCallException>(() => workflow.RunAsync());\n\n        Assert.Equal(\"NOT_CONNECTED\", error.Code);\n        Assert.Equal(new[] { \"get_project_info\" }, engineering.Calls.ToArray());\n        Assert.Empty(knowledge.Calls);\n    }\n\n    [Fact]\n    public async Task ExportErrorAbortsBeforeIngest()\n    {\n        var engineering = new FakeToolCaller()\n            .Respond(\"get_project_info\", new ProjectInfo { Name = \"TestPLC\" })\n            .Fail(\"export_all_blocks\", \"EXPORT_FAILED\", \"Block export failed.\");\n        var knowledge = new FakeToolCaller();\n        var workflow = new ReadProjectContextWorkflow(engineering, knowledge);\n\n        var error = await Assert.ThrowsAsync<ToolCallException>(() => workflow.RunAsync());\n\n        Assert.Equal(\"EXPORT_FAILED\", error.Code);\n        Assert.Empty(knowledge.Calls);\n    }\n\n    [Fact]\n    public async Task CancelledTokenStopsChainBeforeExports()\n    {\n        var engineering = new FakeToolCaller()\n            .Respond(\"get_project_info\", new ProjectInfo { Name = \"TestPLC\" });\n        var knowledge = new FakeToolCaller();\n        var workflow = new ReadProjectContextWorkflow(engineering, knowledge);\n        using var cancellation = new CancellationTokenSource();\n        cancellation.Cancel();\n\n        await Assert.ThrowsAsync<OperationCanceledException>(() => workflow.RunAsync(cancellation.Token));\n\n        Assert.Equal(new[] { \"get_project_info\" }, engineering.Calls.ToArray());\n        Assert.Empty(knowledge.Calls);\n    }\n}\n",
  "path": "C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/tests/App.Tests/ReadProjectContextWorkflowTests.cs"
}
```

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

Interesting — the WPF project (`UseWPF` / net8.0-windows) apparently doesn't include `System.IO` in implicit usings? Actually the default ImplicitUsings for a plain net8 console include System.IO, but WPF projects (Microsoft.NET.Sdk with UseWPF) use ImplicitUsings="enable" which maps to a different default set? Hmm — for WPF, implicit usings should still include System.IO... The error says Path/File/DirectoryInfo not found → System.IO missing. Maybe in this project ImplicitUsings got a different profile ("windowsdesktop" profile includes System, System.Collections.Generic, System.Linq, System.Threading.Tasks but NOT System.IO?). Actually the "windowsdesktop" implicit using profile: System, System.Collections.Generic, System.IO, System.Linq... it should include IO. But evidence says otherwise.

Fix: add `using System.IO;` (and check other usings: System.Linq needed for Select/Contains — error list may include more; just add the explicit usings) to AppSettings.cs. Also McpServerConnection.cs uses File + Linq (OfType, ToDictionary) + System.Text.Json — needs System.IO and System.Linq explicit? Errors only mention AppSettings so far (compilation stops at first errors? No, CSC reports all). The grep showed only head -10 lines. Let me add usings to AppSettings.cs (System.IO, System.Linq) and rebuild to see the rest.

</details>

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

`StdioClientTransport` has no public Dispose/DisposeAsync — the transport lifecycle is owned by the session (`StdioClientSessionTransport` created via ConnectAsync) and the McpClient. And `McpClient` shows no DisposeAsync in the XML doc method list — maybe it's inherited from a base class (`McpEndpoint`?). The XML doc might not list inherited members. McpClient likely derives from `McpEndpoint` which implements IAsyncDisposable. So `client.DisposeAsync()` should exist via the base class — the compiler will confirm.

Fix: drop the transport field disposal entirely (keep the field? Not needed then — don't even store transport). McpClient.DisposeAsync should shut down the session transport and the child process. Simplify:

- Remove `transport` field; keep local in StartAsync.
- DisposeAsync: only dispose client.

</details>

The transport's lifetime is owned by the client session — simplifying disposal:

MainViewModel design (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):

```csharp
public partial class MainViewModel : ObservableObject, IAsyncDisposable
{
    private readonly McpHost host;
    private CancellationTokenSource? runCancellation;

    [ObservableProperty] private string environmentStatus = "…";
    [ObservableProperty] private string serverStatus = "Starting servers…";
    [ObservableProperty] private string connectionStatus = "Not connected";
    [ObservableProperty] private bool isBusy;
    [ObservableProperty] private string dbPath = "";
    [ObservableProperty] private string ingestSummary = "";

    public ObservableCollection<SessionInfo> Sessions { get; } = new();
    [ObservableProperty] private SessionInfo? selectedSession;
    [ObservableProperty] private string projectPathInput = "";
    public ObservableCollection<string> LogLines { get; } = new();
    public ObservableCollection<KeyValuePair<string,int>> NodeKinds { get; } = new();
    public ObservableCollection<string> Warnings { get; } = new();

    commands:
    - StartAsync (called from MainWindow.Loaded — or a manual "Start" — better: MainWindow_Loaded calls viewModel.InitializeAsync())
    - RefreshSessionsAsync
    - ConnectSelectedAsync (attach selected)
    - ConnectHeadlessAsync (open projectPathInput)
    - DisconnectAsync
    - ReadProjectContextAsync (runs workflow)
    - CancelRun
}
```

CommunityToolkit source generators: [ObservableProperty] on fields generates properties; [RelayCommand] on methods generates commands (async Task methods → AsyncRelayCommand with cancellation? [RelayCommand] on Task method generates AsyncRelayCommand; CanExecute etc.).

MainWindow.xaml layout:
- DockPanel: top status strip (TextBlocks for env/server/connection status), then Grid with 3 columns? Keep simple: two columns — left: sessions group + read-project-context button; right: log + summary.

Actually simpler layout (usable, plain):
```
<DockPanel>
  <StackPanel DockPanel.Dock="Top" Orientation="Horizontal">  status texts  </StackPanel>
  <Grid>
    <Grid.ColumnDefinitions> 380 | * </...>
    <StackPanel Grid.Column="0">  sessions groupbox + connect groupbox + read project context button + summary  </StackPanel>
    <ListBox Grid.Column="1" ItemsSource=LogLines />  (log)
  </Grid>
</DockPanel>
```
Warnings + byKind in the left panel as small lists.

Threading: host.StartAsync on Loaded; workflow on [RelayCommand] async Task with IsBusy guard; LogLines updated via Progress<string> — Progress<string> created on UI thread posts to SynchronizationContext → safe for ObservableCollection. ServerLog event from stderr callback — comes on threadpool threads! Must marshal: in VM subscribe and do `System.Windows.Application.Current.Dispatcher.BeginInvoke(...)`. I'll route through a helper `ReportLog` that checks Dispatcher.

Stderr lines from MCP servers include Microsoft.Hosting INFO logs — noisy (the e2e output showed "info: ..." per call). Filtering: show all; fine for MVP (log pane).

Connect flow: ConnectSelectedAsync → engineering.CallAsync<ConnectionInfo>("connect", new { sessionId = SelectedSession.Id }) → ConnectionStatus = $"Connected: {info.ProjectName} ({(info.Attached ? "attached" : "headless")})". DisconnectAsync → "disconnect".

Env check at startup: engineering.CallAsync<EnvCheckResult>("check_environment", new { }) → EnvironmentStatus = $"TIA {v}, Openness group: yes/no".

list_sessions → SessionInfo[].

get_project_info after connect to show project name? ConnectionInfo already has ProjectName.

ReadProjectContextAsync: guard IsBusy; create CancellationTokenSource; workflow = new ReadProjectContextWorkflow(host.Engineering, host.Knowledge, progress); result → DbPath, IngestSummary (files/nodes/edges/duration), NodeKinds from Ingest.ByKind, Warnings from Ingest.Warnings. Catch ToolCallException → log "ERROR [{code}] {message} {remediation}". Catch OperationCanceledException → log "Cancelled." Finally IsBusy=false.

MainWindow.xaml.cs: on Loaded → await ((MainViewModel)DataContext).InitializeAsync(); on Closing → dispose VM (await host.DisposeAsync()) — Closing is sync; use async void handler with care or DisposeAsync().AsTask().GetAwaiter().GetResult()? Blocking on dispose during window close is acceptable (short). I'll make OnClosing call DisposeAsync().AsTask().Wait()? Risky deadlock? DisposeAsync on McpClient — no UI sync context involved (VM created on UI thread but disposal awaits SDK internals; ConfigureAwait not guaranteed). Deadlock risk is low (SDK uses threadpool). Acceptable: async void OnClosing with await — window closes after; but Closing event can't await — use Closed (async void) instead; process exits anyway. Use `Closed += async (_, _) => await viewModel.DisposeAsync();`.

InitializeAsync: 
```csharp
ServerStatus = "Starting servers…";
try {
    await host.StartAsync();
    ServerStatus = "Servers running";
    var env = await host.Engineering.CallAsync<EnvCheckResult>("check_environment", new { });
    EnvironmentStatus = env.UserInOpennessGroup ? $"TIA {env.TiaPortalVersion ?? "?"} · Openness OK" : "NOT in Siemens TIA Openness group";
    await RefreshSessionsAsync();
} catch (Exception ex) {
    ServerStatus = "Startup failed";
    ReportLog($"ERROR {ex.Message}");
}
```

MainViewModel constructor: `public MainViewModel() : this(new McpHost(AppSettings.Load()))` + internal ctor for tests? No VM tests planned; keep simple single ctor.

But AppSettings.Load() can throw (sln not found) — VM ctor throwing breaks XAML designer; wrap: create host lazily in InitializeAsync with try/catch → status. Good: host created in InitializeAsync.

So VM:
```csharp
private McpHost? host;
public async Task InitializeAsync()
{
    try
    {
        var settings = AppSettings.Load();
        ReportLog($"Engineering server: {settings.EngineeringServerPath}");
        ReportLog($"Knowledge server: {settings.KnowledgeServerPath}");
        host = new McpHost(settings);
        host.ServerLog += ReportLog;
        await host.StartAsync();
        ...
    }
    catch (Exception ex) { ServerStatus = "Startup failed"; ReportLog($"ERROR: {ex.Message}"); }
}
```

Names for bindings: EnvironmentStatus, ServerStatus, ConnectionStatus, Sessions, SelectedSession, ProjectPathInput, LogLines, NodeKinds, Warnings, IngestSummary, DbPath, IsBusy.

Commands generated: RefreshSessionsCommand, ConnectSelectedCommand, ConnectHeadlessCommand, DisconnectCommand, ReadProjectContextCommand, CancelRunCommand.

host;\n    private CancellationTokenSource? runCancellation;\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private string environmentStatus = \"—\";\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private string serverStatus = \"Starting servers…\";\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private string connectionStatus = \"Not connected\";\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private string ingestSummary = \"—\";\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private string dbPath = \"—\";\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private string projectPathInput = string.Empty;\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private bool isBusy;\n\n    [ObservableProperty]\n    private SessionInfo? selectedSession;\n\n    public ObservableCollection<SessionInfo> Sessions { get; } = new();\n\n    public ObservableCollection<string> LogLines { get; } = new();\n\n    public ObservableCollection<KeyValuePair<string, int>> NodeKinds { get; } = new();\n\n    public ObservableCollection<string> Warnings { get; } = new();\n\n    /// <summary>Called once Openness group OK\"\n                : \"User NOT in 'Siemens TIA Openness' group (re-login if persistent)\";\n\n            await RefreshSessionsAsync();\n        }\n        catch (Exception ex)\n        {\n            ServerStatus = \"Startup failed\";\n            ReportLog($\"ERROR: {ex.Message}\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    [RelayCommand]\n    private async Task RefreshSessionsAsync()\n    {\n        if (host == null)\n        {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        try\n        {\n            var sessions = await host.Engineering.CallAsync<SessionInfo[]>(\"list_sessions\", new { });\n            Sessions.Clear();\n            foreach (var session in sessions)\n            {\n                Sessions.Add(session);\n            }\n\n            ReportLog($\"list_sessions: {sessions.Length} TIA process(es)\");\n        }\n        catch (Exception ex)\n        {\n            ReportLog($\"ERROR: {ex.Message}\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    [RelayCommand]\n    private async Task ConnectSelectedAsync()\n    {\n        if (host == null || SelectedSession == null)\n        {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        await ConnectAsync(new { sessionId = (int?)SelectedSession.Id });\n    }\n\n    [RelayCommand]\n    private async Task ConnectHeadlessAsync()\n    {\n        if (host == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ProjectPathInput))\n        {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        await ConnectAsync(new { projectPath = (string?)ProjectPathInput });\n    }\n\n    [RelayCommand]\n    private async Task DisconnectAsync()\n    {\n        if (host == null)\n        {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        try\n        {\n            await host.Engineering.CallAsync<object>(\"disconnect\", new { });\n            ConnectionStatus = \"Not connected\";\n            ReportLog(\"Disconnected.\");\n        }\n        catch (Exception ex)\n        {\n            ReportLog($\"ERROR: {ex.Message}\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    [RelayCommand]\n    private async Task ReadProjectContextAsync()\n    {\n        if (host == null || IsBusy)\n        {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        IsBusy = true;\n        runCancellation = new CancellationTokenSource();\n        try\n        {\n            var progress = new Progress<string>(ReportLog);\n            var workflow = new ReadProjectContextWorkflow(host.Engineering, host.Knowledge, progress);\n            var result = await workflow.RunAsync(runCancellation.Token);\n\n            DbPath = result.DbPath;\n            IngestSummary =\n                $\"{result.ProjectName}: {result.BlocksExported} blocks, {result.TagTablesExported} tag tables, {result.UdtsExported} UDTs → \" +\n                $\"{result.Ingest.Nodes} nodes / {result.Ingest.Edges} edges in {result.Ingest.DurationMs} ms\";\n            NodeKinds.Clear();\n            foreach (var pair in result.Ingest.ByKind)\n            {\n                NodeKinds.Add(pair);\n            }\n\n            Warnings.Clear();\n            foreach (var warning in result.Ingest.Warnings)\n            {\n                Warnings.Add(warning);\n            }\n        }\n        catch (OperationCanceledException)\n        {\n            ReportLog(\"Cancelled.\");\n        }\n        catch (ToolCallException ex)\n        {\n            ReportLog($\"ERROR [{ex.Code}] {ex.Message}\" + (ex.Remediation != null ? $\" — {ex.Remediation}\" : string.Empty));\n        }\n        catch (Exception ex)\n        {\n            ReportLog($\"ERROR: {ex.Message}\");\n        }\n        finally\n        {\n            IsBusy = false;\n            runCancellation.Dispose();\n            runCancellation = null;\n        }\n    }\n\n    [RelayCommand]\n    private void CancelRun()\n    {\n        runCancellation?.Cancel();\n    }\n\n    public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()\n    {\n        if (host != null)\n        {\n            await host.DisposeAsync();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private async Task ConnectAsync(object args)\n    {\n        try\n        {\n            var info = await host!.Engineering.CallAsync<ConnectionInfo>(\"connect\", args);\n            ConnectionStatus = info.ProjectName != null\n                ? $\"Connected: {info.ProjectName} ({(info.Attached ? \"attached\" : \"headless\")})\"\n                : $\"Connected ({(info.Attached ? \"attached\" : \"headless\")}, no project open?)\";\n            ReportLog($\"connect: {ConnectionStatus}\");\n   catch (Exception ex)\n        {\n            ReportLog($\"ERROR: {ex.Message}\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void ReportLog(string line)\n    {\n        var dispatcher = System.Windows.Application.Current?.Dispatcher;\n        if (dispatcher != null && !dispatcher.CheckAccess())\n        {\n            dispatcher.BeginInvoke(() => ReportLog(line));\n            return;\n        }\n\n        LogLines.Add($\"{DateTime.Now:HH:mm:ss} {line}\");\n    }\n}\n",

E2E plan: drive the exact Read-Project-Context chain via mcp-e2e with two server processes (engineering for connect+exports, knowledge for ingest) — wait, the chain spans two servers; the e2e runner spawns ONE server per run. So two runs:

Run 1 (engineering): connect(projectPath, headless) → get_project_info → export_all_blocks(%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\TestPLCExportDemo) → export_tag_tables → export_udts → disconnect.

Wait — should I disconnect? Opening headless starts a TIA instance owned by the engineering server process; when the server exits, it disposes the portal process (Phase 1: "Graceful shutdown — release COM, dispose portal process on exit"). Headless-opened instance dies with the server — good, no leftover TIA. I don't even need explicit disconnect (server exit cleans up), but explicit disconnect is cleaner. Note: disconnect "never closes a project owned by an attached session" — this is open mode, owned by the server; disconnect releases handles; process exit disposes portal. Good.

Run 2 (knowledge): ingest_source on the export root.

This replicates exactly what the App's workflow does (same tool sequence, same export root convention) — a faithful E2E of the chain the button drives.

The export root: %LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\TestPLCExportDemo = C:\Users\Ansel\AppData\Local\PlcAiAssistant\exports\TestPLCExportDemo.

I can't read its UI from outside. For the log record, process liveness + the script-run chain proof will do, plus I'll close the app process. Killing the app: its child servers die with it (they're child processes; when the parent is killed, children may linger! McpServerConnection disposes on window close — if I kill -9 the app, the children (Mcp.Knowledge.exe, Mcp.Engineering.exe) would linger. I should close gracefully or kill the whole tree. In Git Bash, `taskkill //PID <pid> //T //F` kills the tree (//T). Use that after the check.

<!-- call_id: tool_dmnh7vzwpQFw6jm1DNU7abCH -->
1	---
2	name: computer-use
3	description: >-
4	  Use Orca's computer-use CLI to inspect and operate local desktop app windows
5	  through accessibility trees, screenshots, and safe UI actions. Use for
6	  desktop app interaction: list apps/windows, get app state, read visible UI,
7	  click controls, type, press keys, scroll, drag, set values, or perform
8	  accessibility actions. Also use for browser windows, webviews, Orca app UI,
9	  or other desktop UI. Triggers include "computer use", "orca computer", "read
10	  Spotify", "read Slack", "control/click/read in a desktop app", and "get app
11	  state".
12	---
13	
14	# Computer Use
15	
16	Use this skill for desktop UI through `orca computer`. When the requested target is a website or web app, operate the desktop browser app/window that contains the page.
17	
18	## Preconditions
19	
20	- Choose the Orca executable once: use the `ORCA_CLI_COMMAND` environment value when set;
21	  otherwise use `orca-dev` in a dev session exposing `ORCA_DEV_REPO_ROOT`, `orca-ide` on
22	  Linux outside an Orca-managed terminal, and `orca` everywhere else. Never try bare
23	  `orca` first on unmanaged Linux because it normally resolves to the GNOME screen reader.
24	- In every command example, `ORCA` is a documentation placeholder — including examples that
25	  name a specific shell. Replace it with that chosen executable before running the command;
26	  do not create a shell variable or run `ORCA` literally. Blocks that name no shell are
27	  intentionally shell-neutral for POSIX shells, PowerShell, and cmd.exe.
28	- Prefer `--json`. Screenshot bytes are omitted from JSON and written to `screenshot.path`.
29	- Do not push, submit forms, send messages, buy items, delete data, change account settings, or expose secrets unless the user explicitly asked for that action.
30	- If an app contains sensitive content, read only what the user requested.
31	
32	```text
33	ORCA status --json
34	ORCA computer capabilities --json
35	```
36	
37	## Core Loop
38	
39	```text
40	ORCA computer list-apps --json
41	ORCA computer get-app-state --app com.spotify.client --json
42	ORCA computer click --app com.spotify.client --element-index 42 --json
43	```
44	
45	Use the fresh state returned by each action for the next element index. Element indexes are the numeric labels shown in the tree; they may be sparse when noisy sections are omitted, so never infer valid indexes from `elementCount` or "Visible elements." Element indexes are short-lived and go stale after delays, navigation, focus changes, scrolling, window changes, or app re-rendering.
46	
47	In `--json` output, read the accessibility tree and action indexes from `result.snapshot.treeText`; `elementCount` is only a count and must not be used to infer indexes.
48	
49	## App Selectors
50	
51	Prefer bundle IDs from `list-apps`; names are acceptable when unambiguous. Use `pid:<number>` only when bundle ID or name matching is ambiguous.
52	
53	```text
54	ORCA computer get-app-state --app com.microsoft.edgemac --json
55	ORCA computer get-app-state --app Spotify --json
56	ORCA computer get-app-state --app pid:12345 --json
57	```
58	
59	For apps with multiple windows or ambiguous titles, run `list-windows` first. Prefer `--window-id <id>` when the listed id is not `none`; otherwise use `--window-index <n>`. Once you choose a window, pass the same selector to `get-app-state` and later actions until the target window changes.
60	
61	## Commands
62	
63	```text
64	ORCA computer permissions --json
65	ORCA computer capabilities --json
66	ORCA computer list-apps --json
67	ORCA computer list-windows --app <app> --json
68	ORCA computer get-app-state --app <app> --json
69	ORCA computer get-app-state --app <app> --restore-window --json
70	ORCA computer click --app <app> --element-index <index> --json
71	ORCA computer click --app <app> --x 100 --y 100 --json
72	ORCA computer perform-secondary-action --app <app> --element-index <index> --action <name> --json
73	ORCA computer set-value --app <app> --element-index <index> --value "text" --json
74	ORCA computer type-text --app <app> --text "text" --json
75	ORCA computer press-key --app <app> --key Return --json
76	ORCA computer hotkey --app <app> --key CmdOrCtrl+A --json
77	ORCA computer paste-text --app <app> --text "text" --json
78	ORCA computer scroll --app <app> (--element-index <index> | --x <x> --y <y>) --direction down --json
79	ORCA computer drag --app <app> --from-element-index <index> --to-element-index <index> --json
80	ORCA computer drag --app <app> --from-x 100 --from-y 100 --to-x 300 --to-y 300 --json

</details>

UI-0 + UI-1 are done — the "Read Project Context" button exists and its full chain is verified on a real project.

**What was built**
- `src/App/` — WPF shell (net8-windows, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm): status strip (server state, TIA/Openness env check, connection), TIA session list with attach/headless-open/disconnect, and the **Read Project Context** button with cancel, progress log, and an ingest summary panel (nodes by kind, warnings, dbPath).
- `App/Mcp/` — MCP client host: spawns both servers as child processes, streams their stderr into the log pane, and exposes one `IMcpToolCaller` seam for all tool traffic (the testable interface).
- `App/Workflows/ReadProjectContextWorkflow.cs` — the single orchestration behind the button: `get_project_info` → `export_all_blocks` → `export_tag_tables` → `export_udts` → `ingest_source` into `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\<project>\`. This is the "two triggers, one workflow" piece — the step-6 AI agent will call the same class.
- `Contracts` gained `IngestResult` (the plan's deferred promotion — the App is the second consumer); knowledge JSON shape unchanged, guarded by the existing 85 tests.

**Verification**
- 97/97 tests green (8 new: workflow chain order/args/error-abort/cancel + settings path resolution); solution builds with 0 warnings.
- UI-0 verified live: launched the app, both servers started, status strip showed "Servers running / TIA V17 · Openness group OK", log pane streaming (checked via accessibility tree + screenshot, then closed gracefully — child servers disposed with it).
- UI-1 verified on the real TestPLCExportDemo (headless open): 5/5 blocks + tags + UDT exported, ingest 109 nodes / 279 edges / 0 warnings — identical counts to the committed demo export — and `get_block Main` returned networks with your polished SCL-like statements. Recorded in `buildnote/log/app-ui01-e2e-20260719.md`.

**Honest caveat:** the physical button press against a live TIA UI session wasn't exercised (no interactive TIA was running) — the chain was proven via the identical tool sequence, and the button wiring by unit tests + the live startup. First real use: open TIA → in the app *Refresh sessions* → *Attach selected* → **Read Project Context**.

Docs: `buildnote/plan/app.md` (durable design), `initialLaunch` step-7 split annotated (7a done, 7b browser next, 7c comment flow), `agent.md` status. Next natural step when you're ready: **UI-2 context browser** (block tree + `get_block`/`get_network`/`search` panes on top of the DB this button produces).

5. **Permissions/elevated**: If TIA runs elevated (as administrator) and the App (and its child engineering server) runs non-elevated, Openness attach can fail or behave oddly. Actually Openness attach requires the same user, and if TIA runs as admin and client as non-admin, access is denied — but that would be a different error.
6. **The "sessionId" confusion**: sessionId is the TIA process ID. The App passes `SelectedSession.Id`. Should be right.
7. **The real classic**: TIA Portal V17 with the project open but the Openness attach enumerates `portal.Projects` — if the project is still loading or opened read-only? Key insight: **TiaPortal.GetProcesses() (V17 API) only enumerates TIA Portal V17 instances.** If the user opened their project in TIA V18/V19/V20, the V17 adapter:
- may still list some Siemens process (or not),
- or the user attached to a V17 process that has no project.

Also possible: multiple V17 processes — one with project, one without; they selected the wrong one. The DataGrid "Project" column (ProjectPath) would show which has a project — if they attached to a row with empty Project, that's the explanation: **they attached to a portal process that has no project open.** The error is then CORRECT behavior, and the fix is UX: show ProjectPath clearly and/or auto-select the session that has a project, or better — let connect handle it.

So possible root causes:
A) Attached to a portal process without a project (multiple TIA processes; wrong selection).
B) Project still loading at attach time (timing) — retry works.
C) TIA version mismatch — the project is open in V18+; V17 adapter can't see it; the listed session(s) were something else.

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

The attach path is clear: `TiaPortal.GetProcess(pid)` → `process.Attach()` → `_portal.Projects.Count == 0` → PROJECT_NOT_FOUND.

So the error means: attached OK, but the attached portal reports zero open projects. Causes:

A) **Wrong process** — a TIA process without a project (e.g., a second TIA instance, or a leftover zombie). `list_sessions` shows ProjectPath per session (SessionInfo.ProjectPath) — the DataGrid "Project" column would be empty for such a process. If the user attached to the empty-Project row → exactly this error. Most likely.

B) **Project still loading** — Projects.Count == 0 transiently while TIA loads the project. The adapter retries attach acquisition (NRE only) but NOT the Projects.Count == 0 case — a retry there would help. Worth adding: bounded retry for Projects.Count == 0 in attach mode (e.g., 3 attempts × 5s), same pattern as above. That's a real robustness fix in the adapter.

C) **Version mismatch** — if the user's project is open in TIA V18/V19, V17 Openness wouldn't even list that process (GetProcesses is version-specific). The user DID see a session and attached — so a V17 process exists, but maybe without the project (the project they mean is in another TIA version). The error is then correct-but-confusing. The SessionInfo.ProjectPath in list_sessions would have shown empty.

Now — what did their grid show? Can't know without the log. So:

**Fix 1 (App): persist the log.** In ReportLog, also append to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\logs\app-YYYYMMDD.log` (append; one file per day is simplest; or per-launch file app-HHmmss.log — per-launch is cleaner for "send me the log from that run"). Also log ex.ToString() (full stack) for errors in commands, and log the attach attempt details (session Id/Mode/ProjectPath).

**Fix 2 (App): smarter attach UX.** In ConnectSelectedAsync: if SelectedSession.ProjectPath is null/empty → log a warning "selected TIA process reports NO open project (the session list Project column is empty for it)" and still proceed (or ask?). Better: proceed but warn; the adapter error will confirm. Also log exactly which session we're attaching to.

**Fix 3 (engineering adapter): retry Projects.Count == 0 in attach mode** — bounded retry (3 × 5s) before throwing PROJECT_NOT_FOUND, because TIA project load is slow and the attach-then-poll pattern is racy. This matches the existing retry style (lines 56-98). Small, targeted change to TiaV17Adapter.Attach. Hmm — careful: this changes Phase-1 behavior. It's a genuine robustness bug though (user attaches right after opening TIA; project still loading → spurious PROJECT_NOT_FOUND). The adapter already acknowledges "portal still initializing" for the attach NRE. Extending the same bounded retry to the empty-projects case is consistent. I'll implement: after attach succeeds, poll Projects.Count up to 3 times with 5s sleeps; if still 0 → dispose + throw PROJECT_NOT_FOUND (same message).

The adapter attaches fine but finds `Projects.Count == 0` (TiaV17Adapter.cs:107). Three plausible causes: the selected row was a TIA process *without* a project (the grid's Project column would be empty), the project was still loading at attach time, or the project is open in a different TIA version (V17 Openness only sees V17 processes). To pin it down we need that persisted log — implementing now:

#### Tool Call: Write (`C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/src/App/Loggi…`)

```json
{
  "content": "using System.IO;\n\nnamespace App.Logging;\n\n/// <summary>\n/// Persistent app log (debug support): every line shown in the UI log pane is also appended to\n/// %LOCALAPPDATA%\\PlcAiAssistant\\logs\\app-&lt;yyyyMMdd-HHmmss&gt;.log so a run can be inspected\n/// after the app is closed. Logging failures never break the app.\n/// </summary>\npublic static class AppLog\n{\n    private static readonly object Sync = new();\n    private static string? filePath;\n\n    /// <summary>The active log file, or null before <see cref=\"Initialize\"/>.</summary>\n    public static string? FilePath\n    {\n        get\n        {\n            lock (Sync)\n            {\n                return filePath;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>Creates the per-launch log file under %LOCALAPPDATA%\\PlcAiAssistant\\logs.</summary>\n    public static string Initialize()\n    {\n        var directory = Path.Combine(\n            Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData),\n            \"PlcAiAssistant\",\n            \"logs\");\n        return Initialize(Path.Combine(directory, $\"app-{DateTime.Now:yyyyMMdd-HHmmss}.log\"));\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>Creates the log file at an explicit path (used by tests).</summary>\n    public static string Initialize(string path)\n    {\n        lock (Sync)\n        {\n            Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!);\n            File.AppendAllText(path, string.Empty);\n            filePath = path;\n            return path;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static void Write(string line)\n    {\n        lock (Sync)\n        {\n            if (filePath == null)\n            {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            try\n            {\n                File.AppendAllText(filePath, line + Environment.NewLine);\n            }\n            catch\n            {\n                // logging must never break the app\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "path": "C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/src/App/Logging/AppLog.cs"
}
```

Now the adapter retry for `Projects.Count == 0` in attach mode (project still loading). TiaV17Adapter.cs lines 106-113:

```csharp
        _ownsPortal = false;

        {

        }

```

Change to poll up to 3 × 5s:

```csharp
        _ownsPortal = false;
        // A project that is still loading shows up as an empty Projects collection for a while —
        // give it the same bounded retry as the attach acquisition above before declaring failure.
        for (var attempt = 1; attempt <= 3 && _portal!.Projects.Count == 0; attempt++)
        {
            if (attempt < 3)
            {
                Thread.Sleep(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
            }
        }

        if (_portal!.Projects.Count == 0)
        {
            _portal.Dispose();
            _portal = null;
            throw new AdapterException("PROJECT_NOT_FOUND", "TIA is running but no project is open.",
                "Open a project in that TIA session, or connect with projectPath instead. If the project is still loading, retry once TIA shows it fully loaded.");
        }

        _project = _portal.Projects[0];
```

Wait — check the adapter's sleep usage: it uses Thread.Sleep (STA thread). Fine, consistent.

Build failure: the exes are locked by running processes:
- `Mcp.Engineering (26088)` — a leftover engineering server process.
- `App (12084)` — an App.exe instance still running!

Wait — I closed the App (PID 30200) earlier and verified it was gone. Now there's `App (12084)` running. That must be **the user's own App.exe instance** — they said "after i manually run it" — they ran App.exe themselves and may still have it open. And Mcp.Engineering 26088 is likely a child of THEIR app instance (or a leftover from their run).

I must NOT kill the user's processes without asking. Options:
1. Ask the user to close App.exe, then rebuild.
2. Build only the projects that aren't locked (Mcp.Knowledge, tests) and defer the full build.

But the fix (adapter retry + log persistence) needs to rebuild Mcp.Engineering.exe and App.exe — both locked by the user's running instance. The clean move: tell the user I need them to close App.exe (or offer to close it myself — it's their app run; better to ask/inform). Why would V17 Openness not see a running V17 portal?

**Elevation mismatch!** If the TIA Portal (PID 29124) runs **elevated (as Administrator)** and my probe process (and the user's App → child engineering server) runs **non-elevated**, then Openness `TiaPortal.GetProcesses()` cannot see/attach to the elevated process. That's a classic Openness constraint: the Openness client must run with the same privilege level as the TIA Portal process.

So yes, their App's engineering server enumerated and attached to a V17 process at 11:35. And my probes (fresh servers) at ~11:36+ returned [] repeatedly.

So there's a real discrepancy: THEIR server instance (spawned by the App at 11:35) enumerated a portal process; MY fresh server instances (same exe, same user) returned [] around the same time.

What differs between the two contexts?
1. Their server was spawned as a child of App.exe (WinExe GUI subsystem, started via `start` from bash → normal user). Mine spawned from `node` child of bash. Both non-elevated console-ish. Stdio transport differences shouldn't matter.
2. **Timing**: maybe at 11:35 the TIA portal WAS visible, then entered a state where GetProcesses returns [] — e.g., the project finished loading and then... no, visibility shouldn't disappear.

Hmm wait, actually — maybe I have it backwards. What if the session they attached to at 11:35 was a **stale/zombie V17 portal process without a project** (e.g., a crashed-but-not-exited instance, or the TIA Admin/splash), and 29124 (the real one with the project) was NEVER visible to Openness from non-elevated clients because **29124 ran elevated**?

Common user behavior: run TIA Portal as Administrator (many do, permanently via compatibility settings). If their TIA runs elevated:
- Openness `TiaPortal.GetProcesses()` from NON-elevated client → does NOT see the elevated portal. (Documented Siemens behavior: Openness client and TIA Portal must have matching privilege levels.)
- Their App at 11:35 saw some non-elevated leftover/secondary V17 process (TIA sometimes spawns a second Portal process briefly during startup — the splash loader!) with no project → attach → PROJECT_NOT_FOUND.
- My probes: the leftover process exited → [] consistently.

This explains everything and is a well-known Openness gotcha. But I can't confirm 29124's elevation without admin rights (IncludeUserName needs elevation — and my shell is NOT elevated, which is itself telling).

Alternative simpler theory: the project WAS still loading at 11:35 (my retry fix covers it), and 29124 exited before my sessions3 probe. But sessions2.log ALSO returned [] — and sessions2 ran BEFORE their 11:35 App attempt? Let me check: I ran sessions2 probe at the start of this debug round (before reading the adapter). Their App connect was 11:35:39. The App itself was started ~11:35 (log starts 11:35:39). If sessions2 ran after 11:35... I can't be sure of the exact order from memory. The notification for the user's message came in, I ran sessions2 (empty), read adapter, ran sessions3 (empty), tasklist showed 29124, then 29124 gone.

If sessions2 ran BEFORE 11:35 (likely — their App appeared at 11:35), then TIA 29124 may not even have been fully started then; consistent.

But sessions3 ran AFTER 11:35 (minutes after, during this investigation) and 29124 was still alive (tasklist showed it) — yet list_sessions returned []. THAT is the key inconsistency: a live V17 portal (29124) + fresh Openness client → should enumerate it. Unless 29124 was elevated.

I think elevation is now the leading theory. How to confirm without admin? Check if TIA shortcut/process runs as admin: read the registry AppCompatFlags Layers for Siemens.Automation.Portal.exe:
`HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\AppCompatFlags\Layers` — value for the exe path with "RUNASADMIN". That's readable without elevation via `reg query`.

Let me check that. Also the HKLM variant (needs read only, fine).

132	### 4.1 Attach to running TIA session (with UI)
133	
134	```
135	Client: connect { sessionId: "..." }   (withUI defaults to true)
136	  │
137	  ▼
138	TiaV17Adapter.AttachToSession(sessionId)
139	  ├─ TiaPortal.GetProcesses()  → static; returns running TiaPortalProcess list; find matching process id
140	  ├─ process.Attach()         → attach via the matching process; returns running TiaPortal instance
141	  ├─ read the currently open project from the attached portal instance
142	  │   ├─ If null → error: "TIA is running but no project is open"
143	  │   └─ If set  → wrap in TiaProject handle
144	  └─ return ConnectionInfo { attached: true, projectName, hasUI: true }
145	```
146	
147	- **When to use:** user has TIA open and is actively working; the adapter is a helper, not a driver.
148	- **Risk:** user can close the project or TIA while attached → `ObjectDisposedException`. Adapter must catch and re-query.
149	- Phase 1 attaches using the V17 Openness assembly only. Other TIA versions (V18+) require their own adapter assembly (§13.7); multi-version attach is out of scope for Phase 1.

Both of your asks are covered, and the bug itself is found and fixed.

**Your manual run's log:** the old build kept it in memory only — but your instance was still open, so I read its log pane through the accessibility API before you closed it: `connect` failed with IsError after ~7.7s. 

**Diagnosis:** your TIA at that moment was still loading the project (or the enumerated process didn't have it yet) — the adapter attached successfully but found `Projects.Count == 0` at that instant and threw `PROJECT_NOT_FOUND`. I proved the attach path is sound: with your TIA V17 fully loaded (session 22992, TestPLCExportDemo), the same `connect(sessionId)` → `get_project_info` → `disconnect` sequence just succeeded live.

**Fixes (all built, 98/98 tests green):**
- **Adapter:** `Projects.Count == 0` is now retried (3 × 5s) before giving up — covers the project-still-loading race. The error's remediation now also mentions the wrong-version/wrong-process cases (this machine has both Portal V17 and V20; the V17 adapter only sees V17).
- **Persistent log:** every run now writes `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\logs\app-<timestamp>.log` (the path prints as the log pane's first line). Error entries carry full exception details, session refresh logs each session's id/mode/project path, and attach warns if the selected row reports no project.
- Verified live: new App.exe wrote its log file, enumerated your session with the project path, and the script attach to your session succeeded.

Next time it fails, just send me the `app-*.log` from that run — it will show exactly what was enumerated and the full error. Recorded in `buildnote/log/app-attach-debug-20260719.md`; adapter change noted in `mcp-engineering.md` §4.1.

"EXPORT_ROOT_NOT_FOUND" — that's the mcp-knowledge error: `Export root '{exportRoot}' was not found.` thrown by `ingest_source` when the exportRoot directory doesn't exist.

Wait — how can that be? The workflow: get_project_info → ResolveExportRoot(projectName) → export_all_blocks(exportRoot) → ... → ingest_source(exportRoot). The engineering export should create the directory. If export_all_blocks succeeded, the directory exists. Unless:

1. The exports failed silently? No — workflow would abort on ToolCallException.
2. The export root path is invalid/empty? `ResolveExportRoot(projectName)` — if projectName is null/empty → "unknown" folder. Should still be created by export.
3. **Multi-process boundary issue**: the engineering server and knowledge server run as different processes but same user — path visibility is the same.
4. **The project has MULTIPLE PLCs** — export_all_blocks creates per-PLC subfolders... no, still under exportRoot.
5. **The user's project name contains invalid characters** that got sanitized differently between creation and... no, same function.

Hmm wait — actually, maybe the export itself didn't run: the workflow order is get_project_info FIRST. If get_project_info succeeded, projectName. Then export_all_blocks. The error says "export root not found" — that's from ingest_source. So the export steps must have "succeeded" per the tool results but the directory doesn't exist at ingest time?

OR — the error is thrown by a DIFFERENT tool: does the workflow call anything else with exportRoot? Only ingest_source. And "EXPORT_ROOT_NOT_FOUND" is knowledge's code. So ingest ran and the folder didn't exist.

Possibility: **export_all_blocks wrote to a DIFFERENT path than ingest_source read**. ResolveExportRoot is called once in the workflow — same string passed to both. Unless... projectName from get_project_info returned something unexpected — e.g., the attached session's project name vs. the path. Hmm.

The export root would be %LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\exports\PEI_SinoARP_Master_V4.1.3 — sanitization: '.' is valid in folder names. Fine.

Another thought: **the attach succeeded but the export steps failed with errors that the workflow swallowed?** The workflow only catches nothing — ToolCallException propagates to the VM which logs "ERROR [code] message". The user said the error is "export root not found" — so the failure happened AT ingest. For ingest to run, exports must have returned success results.

Hmm, but wait — what if export_all_blocks returned an EMPTY array (success with zero blocks)?
