Empty export result → no files, but does export create the outputDir at all? If there are zero blocks to export, the adapter might never create outputDir! Then export_tag_tables → empty, export_udts → empty, ingest_source(exportRoot) → EXPORT_ROOT_NOT_FOUND because the directory was never created!

That's very plausible: attached to pid 19952 — maybe a TIA with a project that has no PLC devices (or a project still loading PLC data?), exports return empty arrays (0 blocks), no directory created, ingest fails.

OR: the user attached to the same TIA where the project is still loading → get_project_info worked? Hmm.

271	    public ExportResult[] ExportAllBlocks(string outputDir)
272	    {
273	        lock (_gate)
274	        {
275	            var plcs = PlcSoftwareResolver.FindAll(RequireProject());
276	            var results = new List<ExportResult>();
277	            foreach (var plc in plcs)
278	            {
279	                // Multi-PLC: per-PLC subfolder, each its own export root with its own metadata.json.
280	                var dir = plcs.Count > 1 ? Path.Combine(outputDir, Sanitize(plc.Name)) : outputDir;
281	                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
282	                var exportStartedUtc = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
283	                var records = new List<ExportMetadataRecord>();
284	                foreach (var (block, groupPath) in BlockEnumerator.Enumerate(plc.BlockGroup))
285	                {
286	                    ExportResult result;
287	                    try
288	                    {
289	                        result = ExportCore(block, dir);
290	                    }
291	                    catch (Exception ex)
292	                    {
293	                        result = new ExportResult
294	                        {
295	                            BlockName = block.Name,
296	                            Success = false,
297	                            Error = ex.Message,
298	                            ExportedAt = DateTime.Now,
299	                        };
300	                    }
301	                    results.Add(result);
302	                    records.Add(ExportManifest.CreateRecord(block, groupPath, dir, result));
303	                }
304	                ExportManifest.WriteAll(dir, exportStartedUtc, records, ExportManifest.BlockCategories);
305	            }
306	            return results.ToArray();
307	        }
308	    }

315	            return new ExportResult

317	                BlockName = block.Name,

323

327	        // V17 PlcBlock.Export refuses to overwrite an existing file (verified 2026-07-18) —
328	        // replace our own previous export.

1	using Siemens.Engineering;
2	using Siemens.Engineering.HW;
3	using Siemens.Engineering.HW.Features;
4	using Siemens.Engineering.SW;

6	namespace Mcp.Engineering.Adapter;
7	
8	/// <summary>
9	/// Device-tree traversal (mcp-engineering.md §13.4; namespaces verified 2026-07-17):
10	/// Project.Devices → HardwareObject.DeviceItems (recursive) → GetService&lt;SoftwareContainer&gt;()
11	/// → Software as PlcSoftware.
12	/// </summary>
13	internal static class PlcSoftwareResolver
14	{
15	    public static IReadOnlyList<PlcSoftware> FindAll(Project project)
16	    {
17	        var result = new List<PlcSoftware>();
18	        foreach (Device device in project.Devices)
19	            Collect(device, result);
20	        return result;
21	    }
22	
23	    private static void Collect(HardwareObject node, List<PlcSoftware> result)
24	    {
25	        if (node is DeviceItem item
26	            && item.GetService<SoftwareContainer>()?.Software is PlcSoftware plc)
27	        {
28	            result.Add(plc);
29	        }
30	        foreach (DeviceItem child in node.DeviceItems)
31	            Collect(child, result);
32	    }
33	
34	    /// <summary>Single-PLC projects resolve without a name; multi-PLC projects require plcName.</summary>
35	    public static PlcSoftware Resolve(Project project, string? plcName)
36	    {
37	        var all = FindAll(project);
38	        if (plcName is not null)
39	        {
40	            var match = all.FirstOrDefault(p =>
41	                string.Equals(p.Name, plcName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
42	            return match ?? throw new AdapterException("PLC_NOT_FOUND",
43	                $"No PLC named '{plcName}' in the project.", Available(all));
44	        }
45	
46	        return all.Count switch
47	        {
48	            1 => all[0],
49	            0 => throw new AdapterException("PLC_NOT_FOUND", "No PLC software found in the project."),
50	            _ => throw new AdapterException("AMBIGUOUS_PLC",
51	                $"Project has {all.Count} PLC devices; specify plcName.", Available(all)),
52	        };
53	    }
54	
55	    private static string Available(IReadOnlyList<PlcSoftware> all) =>
56	        "Available PLCs: " + string.Join(", ", all.Select(p => p.Name));
57	}

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

The resolver walks `project.Devices → DeviceItems (recursive) → GetService<SoftwareContainer>()?.Software is PlcSoftware`. If it returned 0 PLCs for the PEI project at 12:06:34 (9 seconds after attach), the most likely cause is the same class of race: **the huge project's device tree wasn't fully loaded yet** at export time. PEI is a big real-world project (D:\PEI_SinoARP_Master_V4.1.3) — attach returned at 12:06:25 once `Projects.Count >= 1`, but the project CONTENT (device tree) loads lazily/asynchronously in TIA. `get_project_info` succeeded at 12:06:34 (it only reads name/path — and PlcDevices would have been empty too, but we don't see the payload in the log).

Alternative causes:
- The project's PLC is not a standard PlcSoftware device (e.g., a drive controller, or the PLC is in a device group — recursion handles nested DeviceItems, and project.Devices covers top-level devices... device GROUPS! `project.DeviceGroups`! If the PEI project's devices are organized in **device groups** (folders in the project tree), `project.Devices` only contains ungrouped devices — devices inside groups live under `Project.DeviceGroups[].Devices` recursively. If the PEI project has all devices in a group, FindAll returns 0 PLCs! THIS is very plausible — big real projects typically organize devices in folders/groups.

Let me check the ProjectInfo — get_project_info returns PlcDevices — how does IT enumerate devices? Line 203: `var plcs = PlcSoftwareResolver.FindAll(project);` — same resolver. If the user's log showed the get_project_info payload, PlcDevices would be []. The log doesn't show payloads. But GetProjectInfo succeeded (name/path), and the workflow proceeded.

Two candidate root causes:
A) Device groups not traversed by the resolver (project.DeviceGroups ignored).
B) Project content still loading at export time (9s after attach on a huge project).

Which is more likely? The demo project (ungrouped single PLC) works. The PEI project — I can actually CHECK its structure without TIA! The project on D:\ — TIA projects store structure in files, but the .ap17 format is a proprietary container... However! The PlcSourceExporter project exported PEI_SinoARP before (the translate yaml exists from 2026-07-16!). That add-in ran INSIDE TIA and used the same Openness device traversal? Let me check how PlcSourceExporter enumerates PLCs — if it handles DeviceGroups, that confirms the gap in our resolver.

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

The reference receives a single `PlcSoftware` from the add-in host (TIA add-ins get the selected software directly) — so it never had to enumerate devices from the project tree. No device-group insight there.

So the two hypotheses remain:
A) **Device groups**: PEI's devices might live in project device groups, which `project.Devices` doesn't include.

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

Two issues:
1. The list_blocks grep returned nothing — maybe still running or the log section differs. Check the full tail of pei-probe.log.
2. The orca command output redirect: `/tmp/tia-tree.json` in Git Bash maps to a MSYS temp path, but node (Windows) interpreted `/tmp/` as `C:\tmp\` — mismatch. Node got the literal path `/tmp/tia-tree.json` → `C:\tmp\tia-tree.json` which doesn't exist. Fix: use a Windows-style temp path for the redirect, e.g. `$TEMP/tia-tree.json` or just pipe directly: `orca computer get-app-state ... | node -e "read stdin"`. Piping avoids the path issue.

</details>

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

Confirmed: **persistent `PLC_NOT_FOUND: No PLC software found in the project`** — minutes after attach. Not a loading race. The resolver's traversal (`Project.Devices` only) misses the PLC in this project — the PLC must live in a **device group** (or deeper group nesting).

The fix: extend `PlcSoftwareResolver.FindAll` to also traverse `Project.DeviceGroups` recursively. The Openness object model:

```
Project
├── Devices (DeviceComposition) — ungrouped devices
└── DeviceGroups (DeviceUserGroupComposition)
    └── DeviceUserGroup
        ├── Groups (DeviceUserGroupComposition) — nested groups
        └── Devices (DeviceComposition)
```

In V17 API: `Siemens.Engineering.HW.DeviceUserGroup` with `.Groups` (DeviceUserGroupComposition) and `.Devices` (DeviceComposition). The resolver's Collect(HardwareObject) recursion handles DeviceItem nesting below a Device; groups need their own traversal.

Implementation in PlcSoftwareResolver:

```csharp
public static IReadOnlyList<PlcSoftware> FindAll(Project project)
{
    var result = new List<PlcSoftware>();
    foreach (Device device in project.Devices)
        Collect(device, result);
    foreach (DeviceUserGroup group in project.DeviceGroups)
        CollectGroup(group, result);
    return result;
}

private static void CollectGroup(DeviceUserGroup group, List<PlcSoftware> result)
{
    foreach (Device device in group.Devices)
        Collect(device, result);
    foreach (DeviceUserGroup child in group.Groups)
        CollectGroup(child, result);
}
```

Need `using Siemens.Engineering.HW;` — already imported (line 2).

Also — same gap exists anywhere the project device tree is walked: grep for other `project.Devices` / `.Devices` uses in the adapter. FindAll is the central one (used by Resolve too), so fixing FindAll fixes Resolve/ListBlocks/exports/get_project_info.

Also the deeper question: is it POSSIBLE the PLC is somewhere else entirely (e.g., the project has the PLC under a "common" group or as an S7-1500 SoftwareController, or it's a GSD device)? The device-group theory is the overwhelmingly common case for real projects organized in folders. The fix is also trivial. I'll implement it, rebuild, and re-probe against the live session — the user's TIA is still open, so I can verify immediately: list_blocks should return the PEI blocks.

Note: PEI project may contain MANY devices (PLC + HMI + drives) — Collect only picks PlcSoftware services, so HMI panels etc. are naturally skipped.

The Openness V17 object model for device groups:
- `Project.DeviceGroups` → `DeviceUserGroupComposition` (composition of `DeviceUserGroup`)
- `DeviceUserGroup.Groups` → nested `DeviceUserGroupComposition`
- `DeviceUserGroup.Devices` → `DeviceComposition`

Namespaces: `Siemens.Engineering.HW.DeviceUserGroup`. The file already has `using Siemens.Engineering.HW;`.

Implementation:

```csharp
public static IReadOnlyList<PlcSoftware> FindAll(Project project)
{
    var result = new List<PlcSoftware>();
    foreach (Device device in project.Devices)
        Collect(device, result);
    // Devices organized in project-tree groups are not part of project.Devices (2026-07-19:
    // PEI_SinoARP group hid the Sino_PEI PLC from enumeration) — traverse groups recursively.
    foreach (DeviceUserGroup group in project.DeviceGroups)
        CollectGroup(group, result);
    return result;
}

private static void CollectGroup(DeviceUserGroup group, List<PlcSoftware> result)
{
    foreach (Device device in group.Devices)
        Collect(device, result);
    foreach (DeviceUserGroup child in group.Groups)
        CollectGroup(child, result);
}
```

Workflow fail-fast (ReadProjectContextWorkflow): after the three exports, before ingest:

```csharp
if (CountSuccessful(blocks) == 0)
{
    throw new WorkflowException / Exception(
        $"export_all_blocks returned 0 blocks for project '{projectName}'. " +
        "No PLC software was found or every block failed to export — check the project has a PLC with blocks (list_blocks) and review the export results above.");
}
```

That's exactly it — confirmed by your log and my live probe (`plcDevices: []` persists). The resolver only walks `project.Devices` (ungrouped devices); a PLC inside a project-tree group like `PEI_SinoARP` is invisible to it. Fixing the resolver to traverse device groups recursively:

#### Tool Call: Edit (`C:/Users/Ansel/orca/projects/AgentAssistPlcDev/src/Mcp.Engin…`)
<!-- call_id: tool_eMVWLiNTAFyE5bJgcJeovYVE -->
```json
{
  "new_string": "    public static IReadOnlyList<PlcSoftware> FindAll(Project project)\n    {\n        var result = new List<PlcSoftware>();\n        foreach (Device device in project.Devices)\n            Collect(device, result);\n        // Grouped devices are NOT part of project.Devices (2026-07-19: the Sino_PEI PLC sits in the\n        // PEI_SinoARP device group and was invisible to enumeration) — traverse groups recursively.\n        foreach (DeviceUserGroup group in project.DeviceGroups)\n            CollectGroup(group, result);\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    private static void CollectGroup(DeviceUserGroup group, List<PlcSoftware> result)\n    {\n        foreach (Device device in group.Devices)\n            Collect(device, result);\n        foreach (DeviceUserGroup child in group.Groups)\n            CollectGroup(child, result);\n    }",
  "old_string": "    public static IReadOnlyList<PlcSoftware> FindAll(Project project)\n    {\n        var result = new List<PlcSoftware>();\n        foreach (Device device in project.Devices)\n            Collect(device, result);\n        return result;\n    }",

### 13.4 Software discovery via `GetService<SoftwareContainer>`

TIA's device tree mixes hardware and software items. To find PLC blocks, you must discover whether a device item contains PLC software:

```csharp
public PlcSoftware ResolvePlcSoftware(DeviceItem deviceItem)
{
    // Each device item may or may not contain a software container
    var container = deviceItem.GetService<SoftwareContainer>();
    if (container?.Software is PlcSoftware plcSoftware)
    {
        return plcSoftware;
    }

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

The single failure is `FOB_SAFETY` — "The export of block 'FOB_SAFETY' is not permitted." That's a **Safety (F) block** — TIA does not permit exporting F-blocks via standard Openness export (requires safety acceptance/password). This is expected behavior on a project with safety content — and the plan's scope explicitly excludes Safety (F) blocks ("Out of scope: Safety (F) blocks"). The per-block failure was recorded in the result and the manifest, everything else exported fine (481 files in Blocks/ + DB/ + Tags + UDT folders).

Wait — 481 files in Blocks but 1152 blocks listed? Blocks/ has 481; DB/ holds the rest (DBs are blocks too). 1152 = OB/FB/FC + DBs. Fine.

Run in background? Give it 300s foreground... The ingest is a single tool call; e2e runner step timeout default 120s — set 600000 in the step. Run in background with 1200s timeout to be safe.

Complete — the whole PEI project context is now in the knowledge base, and your button path works end-to-end.

**Final numbers for PEI_SinoARP_Master_V4.1.3:**
- Export: 1368 objects (blocks/tags/UDTs) in ~7 min via the attached session. Only failure: `FOB_SAFETY` — TIA refuses Openness export of Safety F-blocks ("not permitted"); out of scope per the plan, everything else landed.
- Ingest: **1368/1368 files, 0 warnings, 9.1s → 118,347 nodes / 254,128 edges**: 4,569 networks (with SCL-like logic text), 985 call instructions, 13,911 variables, 93,056 DB members, 638 tags / 635 IO addresses, 210 UDTs.
- **Parity proof:** `search "Curent_Step"` returns `network:000_Main_PC:12` whose `logicStatements` snippet is byte-identical to your polished `translate\program-blocks.yaml` — the ported translator reproduces your polished output on the real project.

**The fixes behind it (all in the running App, pid 18424; 99/99 tests green):**
- `PlcSoftwareResolver` now traverses `Project.DeviceGroups` recursively — your `Sino_PEI` in the `PEI_SinoARP` group was the exact gap.
- The workflow fails fast with a clear message if a 0-block export ever happens again, instead of the misleading `EXPORT_ROOT_NOT_FOUND`.
- Plus the earlier attach retry (project-still-loading) and the persisted log at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PlcAiAssistant\logs\app-*.log`.

My scripts are done, so no more folder contention — the app is yours to use: *Refresh sessions → Attach selected → Read Project Context* now works on PEI directly. Everything is recorded in `buildnote/log/app-attach-debug-20260719.md`.

Next natural step when you're ready: **UI-2 context browser** — block tree + `get_block`/`get_network`/`search` panes over this 118k-node DB, so you can browse PEI's translated network logic in the app.

```csharp
private async Task<T> Timed<T>(string step, Func<Task<T>> action)
{
    Log($"{step}…");
    var stopwatch = Stopwatch.StartNew();
    var result = await action();
    Log($"{step} — done in {stopwatch.ElapsedMilliseconds / 1000.0:0.0}s");
    return result;
}
```

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

Server package (ModelContextProtocol 1.4.1): **zero** progress support — no IProgress injection for tools in this version. Core has progressToken mentions (client-side protocol) and SendNotification (on the endpoint base presumably).

So server-side MCP progress notifications aren't available via a simple injected IProgress in 1.4.1 server. Options for live progress:

**Option A — stderr streaming (zero protocol change, works today):** the engineering adapter logs per-block progress via `ILogger` → server's stderr → App's `StandardErrorLines` → log pane. The infrastructure already streams stderr live (we saw it). Changes:
- `TiaV17Adapter` gets an `ILogger<TiaV17Adapter>` injected (it's DI-registered — check how it's constructed in Program.cs) and logs at Information level every N blocks (e.g., every 25 blocks or every ~2s) and per export phase: "export_all_blocks: 230/1152 exported…".
- Same for ingest: `ingest_source` in mcp-knowledge logs progress per file (e.g., every 50 files: "ingest: 400/1368 files parsed") to stderr. The knowledge server's Importer/ExportFolderCrawler would need a progress callback — it currently has no logging. Add an ILogger or Action<string> progress sink.
- Frequency: log every N (e.g., each 100 blocks / 100 files) plus phase boundaries; avoids flooding.

This is simple, robust, uses the existing live channel. The App already prefixes [engineering]/[knowledge].

**Option B — MCP progress notifications (proper protocol):** client passes IProgress<ProgressNotificationValue> to CallToolAsync; server sends notifications/progress manually via McpServer injection (tools can take McpServer? and call SendNotificationAsync with the progressToken from the request). Feasible? The server tool can receive `McpServer` via DI (the SDK supports injecting IMcpServer/McpServer into tool methods? there's `McpServer` type in server package; tools can take it as parameter I believe). Then send notifications/progress with the tool call's progressToken — but getting the progressToken requires the RequestContext. In 1.4.1 there might be `RequestContext<CallToolRequestParams>` injectable. This is more moving parts across three layers (adapter callback → tool method → MCP notification → client → VM), and the SDK surface for it is uncertain in 1.4.1. Overkill for the polish request.

**Option C — client-side heartbeat only:** workflow logs elapsed time every few seconds during each step ("Exporting blocks… 15s elapsed"). Zero server changes, but fake progress — no real counts. The user explicitly wants to know what's ongoing; elapsed-time heartbeat is honest but weak.

Best call: **Option A (stderr progress) + keep it informative with real counts**, because it uses the already-working live channel and gives REAL progress ("blocks: 230/1152"). Combined with the existing "Exporting blocks…" start line. Optionally ALSO show the workflow-level elapsed in the step line? Not needed.

Also log per-PLC start ("PLC Sino_PEI: exporting 1152 blocks") so the total is known: "Sino_PEI: 25/1152, 50/1152…".

343	    public ExportResult[] ExportTagTables(string outputDir, string? plcName)
344	    {
345	        lock (_gate)
346	        {
347	            return ExportObjects(
348	                outputDir,
349	                plcName,
350	                plc => TagTableEnumerator.Enumerate(plc.TagTableGroup),
351	                ExportTagTableCore,
352	                (table, groupPath, root, result) => ExportManifest.CreateRecord(
353	                    table.Name, "Tags", table.GetType().Name, groupPath, root, result,
354	                    modifiedDate: ReadTagTableModified(table)));
355	        }
356	    }
357	
358	    public ExportResult[] ExportUdts(string outputDir, string? plcName)
359	    {
360	        lock (_gate)
361	        {
362	            return ExportObjects(
363	                outputDir,
364	                plcName,
365	                plc => PlcTypeEnumerator.Enumerate(plc.TypeGroup),
366	                ExportUdtCore,
367	                (type, groupPath, root, result) =>
368	                {
369	                    var (khp, created, modified, interfaceModified) = ReadTypeMetadata(type);
370	                    return ExportManifest.CreateRecord(
371	                        type.Name, "UDT", type.GetType().Name, groupPath, root, result,
372	                        isKnowHowProtected: khp,
373	                        creationDate: created,
374	                        modifiedDate: modified,
375	                        interfaceModifiedDate: interfaceModified);
376	                });
377	        }
378	    }
379	
380	    /// <summary>Shared export loop for tag tables and UDTs: per-PLC export root (subfolder when
381	    /// multiple PLCs), one manifest upsert per object. exportCore converts per-object failures
382	    /// into Failed results — it never throws for them.</summary>
383	    private ExportResult[] ExportObjects<TObject>(
384	        string outputDir,
385	        string? plcName,
386	        Func<PlcSoftware, IEnumerable<(TObject Item, string? GroupPath)>> enumerate,
387	        Func<TObject, string, ExportResult> exportCore,
388	        Func<TObject, string?, string, ExportResult, ExportMetadataRecord> createRecord)
389	    {
390	        var project = RequireProject();
391	        var plcs = plcName is null
392	            ? PlcSoftwareResolver.FindAll(project)
393	            : new[] { PlcSoftwareResolver.Resolve(project, plcName) };
394	        var results = new List<ExportResult>();
395	        foreach (var plc in plcs)
396	        {
397	            // Multi-PLC: per-PLC subfolder, each its own export root with its own metadata.json.
398	            var dir = plcs.Count > 1 ? Path.Combine(outputDir, Sanitize(plc.Name)) : outputDir;
399	            Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
400	            foreach (var (item, groupPath) in enumerate(plc))
401	            {
402	                var result = exportCore(item, dir);
403	                results.Add(result);
404	                ExportManifest.Upsert(dir, createRecord(item, groupPath, dir, result));
405	            }
406	        }
407	        return results.ToArray();
408	    }
409	
410	    private static ExportResult ExportTagTableCore(PlcTagTable table, string exportRoot)
411	    {
412	        var dir = Path.Combine(exportRoot, ExportManifest.FolderFor("Tags"));
413	        Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
414	        var path = Path.Combine(dir, $"{Sanitize(table.Name)}.xml");
415	        try
416	        {
417	            // V17 Export refuses to overwrite an existing file (verified for blocks 2026-07-18) —
418	            // replace our own previous export.
419	            if (File.Exists(path))
420	                File.Delete(path);
421	            table.Export(new FileInfo(path), ExportOptions.WithDefaults);

16	/// <summary>
17	/// TIA Portal V17 implementation of <see cref="IEngineeringPlatform"/>
18	/// (buildnote/plan/mcp-engineering.md §4). All public members serialize through a single

20	/// <see cref="OpennessAssemblyResolver.Register"/> has run.

34	
35	    public ConnectionInfo Connect(ConnectOptions options)
36	    {
37	        lock (_gate)
38	        {
39	            if (_portal is not null)
40	                throw new AdapterException("ALREADY_CONNECTED", "Already connected. Call disconnect first.");
41	            if (options.SessionId is not null && options.ProjectPath is not null)
42	                throw new AdapterException("AMBIGUOUS_CONNECT", "Provide sessionId or projectPath, not both.");
43	            if (options.SessionId is null && options.ProjectPath is null)
44	                throw new AdapterException("MISSING_CONNECT_TARGET", "Provide sessionId or projectPath.");
45	
46	            return options.SessionId is not null
47	                ? Attach(options.SessionId.Value, options.TimeoutSeconds)
48	                : Open(options.ProjectPath!, options.WithUI);
49	        }
50	    }

Edit 2 (ExportAllBlocks loop):
```csharp
            foreach (var plc in plcs)
            {
                // Multi-PLC: per-PLC subfolder, each its own export root with its own metadata.json.
                var dir = plcs.Count > 1 ? Path.Combine(outputDir, Sanitize(plc.Name)) : outputDir;
                Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
                var exportStartedUtc = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
                var records = new List<ExportMetadataRecord>();
                var blocks = BlockEnumerator.Enumerate(plc.BlockGroup).ToArray();
                _logger.LogInformation("export_all_blocks: {Count} blocks to export ({Plc})", blocks.Length, plc.Name);
                var index = 0;
                foreach (var (block, groupPath) in blocks)
                {
                    index++;
                    ExportResult result;
                    try
                    {
                        result = ExportCore(block, dir);
                    }
                    catch (Exception ex)
                    {
                        result = new ExportResult { ... };
                    }
                    if (!result.Success)
                    {
                        _logger.LogWarning("export_all_blocks: FAILED {Block} — {Error}", block.Name, result.Error);
                    }
                    else if (index % 25 == 0 || index == blocks.Length)
                    {
                        _logger.LogInformation("export_all_blocks: {Index}/{Total} ({Plc})", index, blocks.Length, plc.Name);
                    }
                    results.Add(result);
                    records.Add(ExportManifest.CreateRecord(block, groupPath, dir, result));
                }
                ExportManifest.WriteAll(dir, exportStartedUtc, records, ExportManifest.BlockCategories);
            }
```

{
  "new_string": "    /// <summary>Shared export loop for tag tables and UDTs: per-PLC export root (subfolder when\n    /// multiple PLCs), one manifest upsert per object. exportCore converts per-object failures\n    /// into Failed results — it never throws for them.</summary>\n    private ExportResult[] ExportObjects<TObject>(\n        string outputDir,\n        string? plcName,\n        string label,\n        Func<PlcSoftware, IEnumerable<(TObject Item, string? GroupPath)>> enumerate,\n        Func<TObject, string, ExportResult> exportCore,\n        Func<TObject, string?, string, ExportResult, ExportMetadataRecord> createRecord)\n    {\n        var project = RequireProject();\n        var plcs = plcName is null\n            ? PlcSoftwareResolver.FindAll(project)\n            : new[] { PlcSoftwareResolver.Resolve(project, plcName) };\n        var results = new List<ExportResult>();\n        foreach (var plc in plcs)\n        {\n            // Multi-PLC: per-PLC subfolder, each its own export root with its own metadata.json.\n            var dir = plcs.Count > 1 ? Path.Combine(outputDir, Sanitize(plc.Name)) : outputDir;\n            Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);\n            var items = enumerate(plc).ToArray();\n            _logger.LogInformation(\"{Label}: {Count} objects to export ({Plc})\", label, items.Length, plc.Name);\n            var index = 0;\n            foreach (var (item, groupPath) in items)\n            {\n                index++;\n                var result = exportCore(item, dir);\n                if (!result.Success)\n                {\n                    _logger.LogWarning(\"{Label}: FAILED {Name} — {Error}\", label, result.BlockName, result.Error);\n                }\n                else if (index % 25 == 0 || index == items.Length)\n                {\n                    _logger.LogInformation(\"{Label}: {Index}/{Total} ({Plc})\", label, index, items.Length, plc.Name);\n                }\n\n                results.Add(result);\n                ExportManifest.Upsert(dir, createRecord(item, groupPath, dir, result));\n            }\n        }\n        return results.ToArray();\n    }",
  "old_string": "    /// <summary>Shared export loop for tag tables and UDTs: per-PLC export root (subfolder when\n    /// multiple PLCs), one manifest upsert per object. exportCore converts per-object failures\n    /// into Failed results — it never throws for them.</summary>\n    private ExportResult[] ExportObjects<TObject>(\n        string outputDir,\n        string? plcName,\n        Func<PlcSoftware, IEnumerable<(TObject Item, string? GroupPath)>> enumerate,\n        Func<TObject, string, ExportResult> exportCore,\n        Func<TObject, string?, string, ExportResult, ExportMetadataRecord> createRecord)\n    {\n        var project = RequireProject();\n        var plcs = plcName is null\n            ? PlcSoftwareResolver.FindAll(project)\n            : new[] { PlcSoftwareResolver.Resolve(project, plcName) };\n        var results = new List<ExportResult>();\n        foreach (var plc in plcs)\n        {\n            // Multi-PLC: per-PLC subfolder, each its own export root with its own metadata.json.\n            var dir = plcs.Count > 1 ? Path.Combine(outputDir, Sanitize(plc.Name)) : outputDir;\n            Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);\n            foreach (var (item, groupPath) in enumerate(plc))\n            {\n                var result = exportCore(item, dir);\n                results.Add(result);\n                ExportManifest.Upsert(dir, createRecord(item, groupPath, dir, result));\n            }\n        }\n        return results.ToArray();\n    }",

1	// New code for mcp-knowledge (buildnote/plan/mcp-knowledge.md §3, §7) — not a port.
2	// Manifest-first dispatcher (2026-07-18 decision): when <exportRoot>/metadata.json exists the import is
3	// driven by ManifestImporter; otherwise this root-element folder crawl is the fallback. The crawl
4	// classifies each exported file by its SW.* root element and feeds the ported per-category import
5	// methods (Graph/TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter).
6	using System.Xml;
7	using System.Xml.Linq;
8	using Mcp.Knowledge.Graph;
9	using Mcp.Knowledge.Parsing;
10	
11	namespace Mcp.Knowledge.Import;
12	
13	public sealed class ExportFolderImportResult
14	{
15	    public ExportFolderImportResult(SemanticPlcGraph graph, int filesFound, int filesImported, IReadOnlyList<string> warnings, string source)
16	    {
17	        Graph = graph;
18	        FilesFound = filesFound;
19	        FilesImported = filesImported;
20	        Warnings = warnings;
21	        Source = source;
22	    }
23	
24	    public SemanticPlcGraph Graph { get; }
25	
26	    public int FilesFound { get; }
27	
28	    public int FilesImported { get; }
29	
30	    public IReadOnlyList<string> Warnings { get; }
31	
32	    /// <summary>"manifest" when driven by metadata.json, "crawl" for the root-element folder crawl.</summary>
33	    public string Source { get; }
34	}
35	
36	public static class ExportFolderCrawler
37	{
38	    private const string ProgramBlockPrefix = "SW.Blocks.";
39	
40	    public static ExportFolderImportResult Import(string exportRoot)
41	    {
42	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exportRoot))
43	        {
44	            throw new ArgumentException("Export root is required.", nameof(exportRoot));
45	        }
46	
47	        if (!Directory.Exists(exportRoot))
48	        {
49	            throw new DirectoryNotFoundException($"Export root '{exportRoot}' was not found.");
50	        }
51	
52	        return File.Exists(Path.Combine(exportRoot, ManifestImporter.MetadataFileName))
53	            ? ManifestImporter.Import(exportRoot)
54	            : ImportByCrawl(exportRoot);
55	    }
56	
57	    internal static SemanticGraphNode CreateProjectNode(string exportRoot, string fullRoot)
58	    {
59	        var projectName = Path.GetFileName(fullRoot.TrimEnd(Path.DirectorySeparatorChar, Path.AltDirectorySeparatorChar));
60	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(projectName))
61	        {
62	            projectName = exportRoot;
63	        }
64	
65	        return new SemanticGraphNode(
66	            $"project:{projectName}",
67	            SemanticNodeKind.Project,
68	            projectName,
69	            new Dictionary<string, string> { ["exportRoot"] = exportRoot });
70	    }
71	
72	    private static ExportFolderImportResult ImportByCrawl(string exportRoot)
73	    {
74	        var fullRoot = Path.GetFullPath(exportRoot);
75	        var relativeFiles = Directory
76	            .EnumerateFiles(fullRoot, "*.xml", SearchOption.AllDirectories)
77	            .Select(path => Path.GetRelativePath(fullRoot, path))
78	            .OrderBy(path => path, StringComparer.Ordinal)
79	            .ToArray();
80	
81	        var candidates = new List<ImportCandidate>();
82	        var warnings = new List<string>();
83	        foreach (var relativeFile in relativeFiles)
84	        {
85	            var candidate = TryClassify(fullRoot, relativeFile, warnings);
86	            if (candidate != null)
87	            {
88	                candidates.Add(candidate);
89	            }
90	        }
91	
92	        // Duplicate rule (§3): same block identity (root element + name) in several files keeps the
93	        // shallowest relative path, ties broken alphabetically; losers are reported, not imported.
94	        var winners = new List<ImportCandidate>();
95	        foreach (var group in candidates.GroupBy(candidate => candidate.Identity))
96	        {
97	            var ordered = group
98	                .OrderBy(candidate => candidate.Depth)
99	                .ThenBy(candidate => candidate.RelativeFile, StringComparer.Ordinal)
100	                .ToArray();
101	            winners.Add(ordered[0]);
102	            foreach (var duplicate in ordered.Skip(1))
103	            {
104	                warnings.Add(
105	                    $"skipped duplicate: {duplicate.RelativeFile} ('{duplicate.Name}' {duplicate.RootElement} already imported from {ordered[0].RelativeFile})");
106	            }
107	        }
108	
109	        var graph = new SemanticPlcGraph();
110	        var project = CreateProjectNode(exportRoot, fullRoot);
111	        graph.UpsertNode(project);
112	
113	        var imported = 0;
114	        foreach (var winner in winners.OrderBy(candidate => candidate.RelativeFile, StringComparer.Ordinal))
115	        {
116	            var sourcePath = Path.GetDirectoryName(winner.RelativeFile) ?? string.Empty;
117	            switch (winner.Kind)
118	            {
119	                case ImportKind.ProgramBlock:
120	                    TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportBlockXml(
121	                        winner.Xml,
122	                        new ProgramBlockComponent(winner.Name, winner.Category!, sourcePath, winner.RelativeFile),
123	                        graph);
124	                    TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.AddEdgeIfTargetExists(
125	                        graph, project.Id, TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.BlockId(winner.Name), SemanticRelationshipType.Contains);
126	                    break;
127	                case ImportKind.DataBlock:
128	                    TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportDbXml(winner.Xml, winner.RelativeFile, sourcePath, graph);
129	                    TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.AddEdgeIfTargetExists(
130	                        graph, project.Id, TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.DbId(winner.Name), SemanticRelationshipType.Contains);
131	                    break;
132	                case ImportKind.Udt:
133	                    TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportUdtXml(winner.Xml, winner.RelativeFile, sourcePath, graph);
134	                    TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.AddEdgeIfTargetExists(
135	                        graph, project.Id, TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.UdtId(winner.Name), SemanticRelationshipType.Contains);
136	                    break;
137	                case ImportKind.TagTable:
138	                    // Reference behaviour: tag tables get no project CONTAINS edge (tags float freely).
139	                    TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportTagTableXml(winner.Xml, winner.RelativeFile, sourcePath, graph);
140	                    break;
141	            }
142	
143	            imported++;
144	        }
145	
146	        return new ExportFolderImportResult(graph, relativeFiles.Length, imported, warnings, "crawl");
147	    }
148	
149	    private static ImportCandidate? TryClassify(string fullRoot, string relativeFile, IList<string> warnings)
150	    {
151	        string xml;
152	        XDocument document;
153	        try
154	        {
155	            xml = File.ReadAllText(Path.Combine(fullRoot, relativeFile));
156	            document = XDocument.Parse(xml);
157	        }
158	        catch (XmlException ex)
159	        {
160	            warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: malformed XML ({ex.Message})");
161	            return null;
162	        }
163	        catch (IOException ex)
164	        {
165	            warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: {ex.Message}");
166	            return null;
167	        }
168	
169	        var contentRoot = document.Root;
170	        if (contentRoot != null && !contentRoot.Name.LocalName.StartsWith("SW.", StringComparison.Ordinal))
171	        {
172	            // TIA exports wrap the payload in <Document>; classify by the first SW.* child.
173	            contentRoot = contentRoot
174	                .Elements()
175	                .FirstOrDefault(element => element.Name.LocalName.StartsWith("SW.", StringComparison.Ordinal));
176	        }
177	
178	        if (contentRoot == null)
179	        {
180	            warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: no SW.* content element found");
181	            return null;
182	        }
183	
184	        var rootElement = contentRoot.Name.LocalName;
185	        var kind = rootElement switch
186	        {
187	            "SW.Blocks.OB" or "SW.Blocks.FB" or "SW.Blocks.FC" => ImportKind.ProgramBlock,
188	            "SW.Blocks.GlobalDB" or "SW.Blocks.InstanceDB" or "SW.Blocks.ArrayDB" or "SW.Blocks.DB" => ImportKind.DataBlock,
189	            "SW.Types.PlcStruct" => ImportKind.Udt,
190	            "SW.Tags.PlcTagTable" => ImportKind.TagTable,
191	            _ => ImportKind.Unsupported,
192	        };
193	        if (kind == ImportKind.Unsupported)
194	        {
195	            warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: unsupported root element '{rootElement}'");
196	            return null;
197	        }
198	
199	        var name = GetAttributeListValue(contentRoot, "Name");
200	        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name))
201	        {
202	            warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: no <Name> entry in AttributeList of '{rootElement}'");
203	            return null;
204	        }
205	
206	        return new ImportCandidate(
207	            relativeFile,
208	            xml,
209	            name,
210	            rootElement,
211	            kind,
212	            kind == ImportKind.ProgramBlock ? rootElement.Substring(ProgramBlockPrefix.Length) : null,
213	            relativeFile.Count(character => character == '/' || character == '\\'));
214	    }
215	
216	    private static string GetAttributeListValue(XElement element, string name)
217	    {
218	        return element
219	            .Elements()
220	            .FirstOrDefault(child => child.Name.LocalName == "AttributeList")
221	            ?.Elements()
222	            .FirstOrDefault(child => child.Name.LocalName == name)
223	            ?.Value
224	            .Trim() ?? string.Empty;
225	    }
226	
227	    private enum ImportKind
228	    {
229	        Unsupported,
230	        ProgramBlock,
231	        DataBlock,
232	        Udt,
233	        TagTable,
234	    }
235	
236	    private sealed class ImportCandidate
237	    {
238	        public ImportCandidate(string relativeFile, string xml, string name, string rootElement, ImportKind kind, string? category, int depth)
239	        {
240	            RelativeFile = relativeFile;
241	            Xml = xml;
242	            Name = name;
243	            RootElement = rootElement;
244	            Kind = kind;
245	            Category = category;
246	            Depth = depth;
247	            Identity = rootElement + "\n" + name;
248	        }
249	
250	        public string RelativeFile { get; }
251	
252	        public string Xml { get; }
253	
254	        public string Name { get; }
255	
256	        public string RootElement { get; }
257	
258	        public ImportKind Kind { get; }
259	
260	        /// <summary>Block category (OB/FB/FC) for program blocks; null otherwise.</summary>
261	        public string? Category { get; }
262	
263	        public int Depth { get; }
264	
265	        /// <summary>Duplicate-detection identity: content root element + block name.</summary>
266	        public string Identity { get; }
267	    }
268	}

</details>

47	public static class ManifestImporter
48	{
49	    public const string MetadataFileName = "metadata.json";
50	    public const string ExpectedSchemaVersion = "1.0";
51	
52	    private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions JsonOptions = new()
53	    {
54	        PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
55	        PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
56	    };
57	
58	    public static ExportFolderImportResult Import(string exportRoot)
59	    {
60	        var fullRoot = Path.GetFullPath(exportRoot);
61	        var warnings = new List<string>();
62	        var document = ReadManifest(fullRoot);
63	
64	        if (!string.Equals(document.SchemaVersion, ExpectedSchemaVersion, StringComparison.Ordinal))
65	        {
66	            warnings.Add(
67	                $"metadata.json schemaVersion is '{document.SchemaVersion ?? "(missing)"}', expected '{ExpectedSchemaVersion}' — importing anyway");
68	        }
69	
70	        var components = (document.Components ?? new List<ComponentMetadataRecordDto>())
71	            .Where(component =>
72	                string.Equals(component.Status, "Exported", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
73	                !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(component.ExportedFile))
74	            .OrderBy(component => component.SourcePath, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
75	            .ToArray();
76	
77	        // Reconciliation baseline: every exportedFile the manifest mentions, in any status.
78	        var referencedFiles = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
79	        foreach (var component in document.Components ?? new List<ComponentMetadataRecordDto>())
80	        {
81	            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(component.ExportedFile))
82	            {
83	                referencedFiles.Add(NormalizeSeparators(component.ExportedFile!));
84	            }
85	        }
86	
87	        var graph = new SemanticPlcGraph();
88	        var project = ExportFolderCrawler.CreateProjectNode(exportRoot, fullRoot);
89	        graph.UpsertNode(project);
90	
91	        var imported = 0;
92	        foreach (var component in components)
93	        {
94	            var relativeFile = NormalizeSeparators(component.ExportedFile!);
95	            if (!File.Exists(Path.Combine(fullRoot, relativeFile)))
96	            {
97	                warnings.Add(
98	                    $"manifest entry '{component.Name}' ({component.Category}) is marked Exported but its file is missing: {relativeFile}");
99	                continue;
100	            }
101	
102	            var name = component.Name ?? string.Empty;
103	            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name))
104	            {
105	                warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: manifest entry has no name");
106	                continue;
107	            }
108	
109	            string xml;
110	            try
111	            {
112	                xml = File.ReadAllText(Path.Combine(fullRoot, relativeFile));
113	            }
114	            catch (IOException ex)
115	            {
116	                warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: {ex.Message}");
117	                continue;
118	            }
119	
120	            var category = component.Category ?? string.Empty;
121	            if (IsProgramBlockCategory(category))
122	            {
123	                TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportBlockXml(
124	                    xml,
125	                    new ProgramBlockComponent(name, category, component.SourcePath ?? string.Empty, relativeFile),
126	                    graph);
127	                TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.AddEdgeIfTargetExists(
128	                    graph, project.Id, TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.BlockId(name), SemanticRelationshipType.Contains);
129	                imported++;
130	                continue;
131	            }
132	
133	            if (string.Equals(category, "DB", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
134	            {
135	                TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportDbXml(xml, relativeFile, component.SourcePath ?? string.Empty, graph);
136	                TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.AddEdgeIfTargetExists(
137	                    graph, project.Id, TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.DbId(name), SemanticRelationshipType.Contains);
138	                imported++;
139	                continue;
140	            }
141	
142	            if (string.Equals(category, "UDT", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
143	            {
144	                TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportUdtXml(xml, relativeFile, component.SourcePath ?? string.Empty, graph);
145	                TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.AddEdgeIfTargetExists(
146	                    graph, project.Id, TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.UdtId(name), SemanticRelationshipType.Contains);
147	                imported++;
148	                continue;
149	            }
150	
151	            if (string.Equals(category, "Tags", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
152	            {
153	                // Reference behaviour: tag tables get no project CONTAINS edge (tags float freely).
154	                TiaXmlSemanticGraphImporter.ImportTagTableXml(xml, relativeFile, component.SourcePath ?? string.Empty, graph);
155	                imported++;
156	                continue;
157	            }
158	
159	            warnings.Add($"skipped {relativeFile}: unsupported category '{category}'");
160	        }
161	
162	        var diskFiles = Directory
163	            .EnumerateFiles(fullRoot, "*.xml", SearchOption.AllDirectories)
164	            .Select(path => Path.GetRelativePath(fullRoot, path))
165	            .OrderBy(path => path, StringComparer.Ordinal)
166	            .ToArray();

<details><summary>Thinking</summary>

Now implement the knowledge-side progress. Edits:

1. **ManifestImporter.Import**: signature → `Import(string exportRoot, Action<string>? progress = null)`; add progress in the component loop. The loop iterates `components` (ordered array). Add counter for processed files and log every 100: `progress?.Invoke($"ingest_source: {processed}/{components.Length} files (manifest)")`. Increment `processed` at each iteration (whether imported, warned, or skipped). Simplest: put at loop top.

2. **ExportFolderCrawler.Import**: signature → `Import(string exportRoot, Action<string>? progress = null)`; pass through to ManifestImporter.Import(exportRoot, progress) / ImportByCrawl(exportRoot, progress). In ImportByCrawl's winner loop: `progress?.Invoke($"ingest_source: {imported}/{winners.Count} files (crawl)")` every 100.

3. **KnowledgeTools**: logger ctor (optional), Ingest becomes instance method, passes `message => _logger?.LogInformation("{IngestProgress}", message)`.

```json

Done — the app now narrates ongoing work instead of going silent. New build is running (pid 20060), 99/99 tests green.

**What you'll see on the next PEI run:**
- `Exporting blocks…` (workflow line, as before)
- `export_all_blocks: 1152 blocks to export (Sino_PEI)` — total known upfront
- `export_all_blocks: 25/1152 (Sino_PEI)`, `50/1152`, … every 25 blocks — live cadence
- `FAILED FOB_SAFETY — …not permitted` — per-object failures surface immediately as warnings
- Same pattern for tag tables / UDTs, and during ingest: `ingest_source: 100/1368 files (manifest)` every 100 files
- `Exporting blocks — done in 35s` (completion, as before)

**How it works:** MCP SDK 1.4.1 has no server-side progress-notification support, so progress rides the channel that was already live — servers log to stderr, and the App already streams stderr into the log pane. The engineering adapter got a DI `ILogger` with progress in both export loops; the knowledge crawler/manifest importer got an optional progress callback wired to the tools' logger. No protocol changes, no App changes needed.

**Verified live:** PEI ingest shows the 100-file cadence; demo export shows `5 blocks to export (PLC_1)` → `5/5`. Note your screenshot's success numbers matched exactly (1151 blocks — the one F-block skipped — 7 tag tables, 210 UDTs → 118347/254128).

Recorded in `buildnote/log/app-attach-debug-20260719.md` (UX polish section). One process note: I closed and relaunched your App once more to swap in the new build — that's needed every time the server binaries change, since the running App holds them open.
